description: Platform Hardening & Quality Improvements - Comprehensive security remediation, performance optimization, UI/UX consistency fixes, and accessibility improvements identified through a full-spectrum platform audit. Covers critical credential rotation, CORS hardening, SSRF prevention, dependency vulnerability patching, AppContext performance overhaul, bundle optimization, dark mode fixes, form UX standardization, and navigation/feedback consistency.

userStories.title: Remove live credentials from wrangler.toml and rotate keys

Replace the real Stripe publishable key in wrangler.toml (line 14 comment) with a placeholder: pk_live_XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX
Search entire codebase for any other real API keys, JWTs, or secrets and replace with placeholders
Verify no .env files are tracked in git
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds

acceptanceCriteria: Add URL validation to functions/parse-recipe/index.ts that blocks private IP ranges (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 127.x, 169.254.x, fd00::/8, ::1)

Create shared URL validation utility in functions/_shared/url-validator.ts

userStories.title: Fix create-checkout to use JWT user_id instead of client-supplied user_id

acceptanceCriteria: In functions/create-checkout/index.ts, extract user.id from the verified JWT (supabaseClient.auth.getUser())
Use the JWT-derived user_id for Stripe metadata, client_reference_id, and user_subscriptions upsert
Ignore or remove the user_id field from the request body
Return 401 if JWT verification fails

userStories.title: Fix Stripe webhook signature to use constant-time comparison
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want webhook signature verification to use constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks.
acceptanceCriteria: In functions/stripe-webhook/index.ts, replace the string equality check (computed === expectedSig) with crypto.subtle.timingSafeEqual or equivalent constant-time comparison
Encode both strings to Uint8Array before comparison
Handle length mismatch case securely (return false without leaking info)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix overly permissive RLS INSERT policies on security-critical tables

Fix login_history INSERT policy to require auth.uid() = user_id instead of WITH CHECK (true)
Fix login_history UPDATE policy to require auth.uid() = user_id
Fix security_audit_logs INSERT policy to require auth.uid() IS NOT NULL

userStories.description: As a parent of a child with ARFID, I want the landing page to articulate why generic meal planning apps fail for feeding disorders so that I understand how EatPal is different.
acceptanceCriteria: Section heading references ARFID and extreme picky eating specifically (e.g. 'Why typical meal planning apps fail for ARFID and extreme picky eating')

Include 3-4 specific failure modes of generic approaches: assumes kids will 'just try it', generic 'offer variety' advice increases anxiety, feeding therapy progress stalls without structured home support

Follow with 3-4 solution bullets on how EatPal is built specifically for feeding disorders: food chaining bridges from safe foods to new foods, plans around sensory preferences and triggers, keeps parents and therapists aligned
Uses empathetic, non-blaming tone per brand voice guidelines
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: This section must differentiate EatPal from generic meal planning apps. Parents of ARFID children have tried standard approaches and need to know this is different.

userStories.title: Add therapist/clinic section on homepage with professional positioning
userStories.description: As a feeding therapist or clinic director, I want a dedicated section on the homepage addressing professional use so that I understand how EatPal supports clinical workflows and can extend therapy into the home.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a section with heading 'The Operating System for Feeding Therapy' or similar

Include 3-4 therapist-specific benefits: create and share individualized food chains, capture real-time meal logs between sessions, standardize data collection across therapists, improve outcomes with structured home follow-through
Include CTA to therapist plans/pricing
Section visually distinct (different background or card treatment) to signal B2B audience
Positioned before the final CTA section on the landing page
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Therapist adoption is both validation and distribution. Feeding therapists who adopt EatPal refer families to the parent app, creating a flywheel for B2C acquisition.

userStories.title: Update three-pillar value proposition cards for niche positioning
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see three clear value pillars (evidence-based progress, calmer mealtimes, therapist-designed) so that I understand EatPal's unique differentiation from generic meal apps.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace or update the 6-card features section with 3 focused value proposition cards
Card 1: 'Evidence-Based Progress, Not Guesswork' - AI grounded in food chaining, detects patterns in textures/brands/flavors
Card 2: 'Calmer, Safer-Feeling Mealtimes' - visual progress charts, structured daily plans
Card 3: 'Designed With Feeding Therapists' - supports food logs, safe-food lists, optional therapist portal
Each card contains 2-3 benefit-oriented bullets, not raw features
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Three focused pillars are more effective than six generic feature cards for communicating niche positioning to high-anxiety parents. [Completed 2026-03-01]

userStories.title: Update Auth page messaging for ARFID and feeding disorder positioning
userStories.description: As a parent signing up, I want the auth page to reinforce that EatPal is for ARFID and feeding disorders so that I feel confident I'm in the right place.
acceptanceCriteria: Update left panel headline from 'End Mealtime Battles Forever' to ARFID-specific messaging
Update subheadline to mention food chaining, ARFID, and feeding therapist collaboration
Update value propositions to emphasize evidence-based approach, not generic meal planning
Add ARFID-specific testimonial or social proof element
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: The auth page is the last touchpoint before conversion. Consistent ARFID positioning reinforces the parent's decision to sign up. [Completed 2026-03-01]

userStories.title: Update SEO meta tags and structured data for ARFID keyword targeting
userStories.description: As an SEO strategy, I want meta titles, descriptions, and structured data to target high-intent ARFID and food chaining keywords so that we capture organic search traffic from parents seeking help.
acceptanceCriteria: Update homepage meta title to prominently feature ARFID (e.g. 'EatPal: ARFID & Picky Eater Meal Planner | Food Chaining Science')
Update homepage meta description to lead with ARFID/feeding disorder positioning
Ensure FAQ schema includes ARFID-specific Q&As for rich snippet eligibility
Add structured data for ARFID-related use cases in AI search metadata
Target keywords: 'ARFID meal plan for kids', 'food chaining examples', 'picky eater meal plan app', 'feeding therapy at home tools'
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: ARFID search volume is growing as the diagnosis becomes more recognized. Capturing these high-intent keywords drives qualified traffic with high conversion potential. [Completed 2026-03-01]

userStories.title: Organize blog with ARFID content pillars and category structure
userStories.description: As a content marketer, I want blog posts organized by ARFID/food chaining content pillars so that we build topical authority and support SEO clustering strategy.
acceptanceCriteria: Add content pillar categories: 'ARFID & Feeding Disorders 101', 'Food Chaining in Real Life', 'Meal Planning for Selective Eaters', 'Autism & Sensory Mealtime Support', 'For Therapists & Clinics'
Update blog page to display category filters matching these pillars
Each category has a brief description visible on the blog page
Blog post cards show their category tag
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Content pillars are the backbone of organic acquisition. Each pillar targets a cluster of related keywords and drives traffic to the app signup funnel. [Completed 2026-03-01]

userStories.title: Update Pricing page with clearer parent vs therapist plan positioning
userStories.description: As a visitor deciding on a plan, I want pricing to clearly differentiate parent plans from therapist/clinic plans so that I can quickly identify which plan is for me.
acceptanceCriteria: Separate pricing into two clearly labeled sections: 'For Families' and 'For Therapists & Clinics'
Parent plans emphasize: food chaining, AI meal plans, progress tracking, grocery lists
Therapist plans emphasize: multi-client management, food chain design tools, shared progress data, insurance-compatible documentation
Each section has audience-specific copy (parent-friendly vs clinically-oriented)
Clear entry point for small therapist accounts (e.g. 'Start with 3 families')
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Mixed B2C/B2B pricing confuses both audiences. Clear separation helps each persona identify their plan in under 30 seconds. [Completed 2026-03-01]

userStories.title: Centralized input sanitization middleware for all edge functions
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want a shared input sanitization layer applied to every edge function so that injection attacks (SQL, command, template) are blocked before reaching business logic. Shannon-style tools trace data flows from user input to dangerous sinks — this breaks those chains at the boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Create functions/_shared/sanitize.ts with sanitizeInput(), sanitizeSQL(), sanitizeHTML(), sanitizeCommand() utilities
All edge functions import and apply sanitizeInput() to request body/params before processing
SQL-like patterns (UNION SELECT, DROP TABLE, OR 1=1, etc.) are stripped or rejected with 400
Command injection characters (;, |, &&, $(), backticks) are escaped in any string passed to exec-like operations
Unit tests cover OWASP injection cheat sheet payloads (minimum 30 test vectors)
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Defense against Shannon's injection exploitation phase. Shannon traces data flows from input to dangerous operations — this sanitization layer breaks every flow at the boundary.

userStories.title: Audit and enforce parameterized queries across all Supabase calls
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want every database query to use parameterized inputs (never string interpolation) so that SQL injection is structurally impossible, even if sanitization is bypassed.
acceptanceCriteria: Grep entire codebase for string-interpolated Supabase queries (template literals in .from(), .rpc(), raw SQL)
Convert all interpolated queries to use Supabase client parameterized methods (.eq(), .in(), .filter(), etc.)
Add ESLint rule or custom lint script that flags raw SQL string concatenation/interpolation
Audit all .rpc() calls to ensure parameters are passed as function arguments, not embedded in SQL
Document findings in a SECURITY_AUDIT.md with before/after for each fix
All existing tests still pass
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Shannon's code analysis identifies parameterized vs interpolated queries. Parameterized queries make injection structurally impossible regardless of input content.

userStories.title: Implement DOMPurify sanitization on all dynamic HTML rendering
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want all user-generated or API-returned HTML to be sanitized through DOMPurify before rendering, so that stored and reflected XSS payloads are neutralized. Shannon tests XSS by injecting payloads through browser automation.
acceptanceCriteria: Install and configure DOMPurify as the standard HTML sanitization library
Audit all uses of dangerouslySetInnerHTML across the codebase and wrap each with DOMPurify.sanitize()
Audit TipTap blog editor output rendering — ensure stored blog HTML is sanitized on display
Audit all locations where API response data is rendered as HTML (AI-generated content, recipe instructions, etc.)
Create a shared SafeHTML component: <SafeHTML html={content} /> that applies DOMPurify automatically
Add unit tests with OWASP XSS cheat sheet payloads (<script>, onerror, javascript:, data:text/html, SVG payloads)
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Shannon's XSS agent injects real payloads via browser automation. DOMPurify at every rendering boundary neutralizes stored, reflected, and DOM-based XSS.

userStories.title: Implement strict Content Security Policy without unsafe-inline
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want a strict CSP that eliminates unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval so that even if an XSS payload reaches the browser, it cannot execute. This is the critical layer that makes US-035 actionable.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace all inline <script> tags with external script files or nonce-based CSP
Replace all inline style= attributes in components with Tailwind classes or CSS modules

Configure CSP header with: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'nonce-{random}'; style-src 'self' 'nonce-{random}'; img-src 'self' data: https:; connect-src 'self' https://api.tryeatpal.com https://functions.tryeatpal.com
Add CSP nonce generation to Cloudflare Pages _headers or _worker.js
Ensure Three.js, Framer Motion, GSAP, and Lottie still work under the new CSP
Add CSP violation reporting endpoint (report-uri or report-to directive)
Test in report-only mode first, then enforce after 1 week with no breakage
npm run build succeeds and all E2E tests pass
userStories.notes: Supersedes US-035. A strict CSP is the strongest browser-side XSS defense. Shannon's XSS payloads become inert even if they reach the DOM.

userStories.title: Implement URL allowlist validation service for all server-side fetches
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want all server-side HTTP requests (fetch, axios, etc.) to pass through a URL validation service that blocks requests to internal/private IP ranges, so that SSRF attacks cannot reach internal infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: Create functions/_shared/url-validator.ts with validateExternalUrl() that rejects: private IPs (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 127.x, 169.254.x), IPv6 loopback/link-local, cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254), file:// and other non-http(s) schemes
Resolve DNS before validation to prevent DNS rebinding attacks (resolve hostname, check resulting IP is not private)
Apply validateExternalUrl() to: parse-recipe URL fetching, update-blog-image URL fetching, any OpenAI/external API proxy calls, grocery delivery API calls
Add timeout of 10s on all external fetches to prevent slow-loris style SSRF
Unit tests cover: localhost variations (127.0.0.1, 0.0.0.0, [::1], 0x7f000001), DNS rebinding scenarios, cloud metadata endpoints, non-HTTP schemes
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Builds on US-004. Shannon's SSRF agent attempts to force internal requests through URL manipulation, DNS rebinding, and IP encoding tricks. This blocks all known SSRF bypass techniques.

userStories.title: Implement CSRF protection on all state-changing operations
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want CSRF tokens validated on every state-changing request so that cross-origin form submissions and fetch requests cannot perform actions on behalf of authenticated users.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement CSRF token generation and validation using Supabase session-bound tokens or a double-submit cookie pattern
All POST/PUT/DELETE edge function endpoints validate CSRF token presence and correctness
Frontend automatically attaches CSRF token to all mutating API requests via a shared fetch wrapper or Supabase client interceptor
Token rotation occurs on each session refresh
Requests without valid CSRF token return 403 Forbidden with descriptive error
Unit tests verify: missing token rejected, invalid token rejected, expired token rejected, valid token accepted
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shannon's auth agent tests for CSRF by attempting cross-origin state-changing requests. CSRF tokens make these attacks impossible.

userStories.title: Harden session management and cookie security
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want all session cookies to use secure attributes and sessions to be protected against fixation attacks, so that Shannon-style tools cannot hijack or replay sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify all Supabase auth cookies are set with: Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax (or Strict), Path=/
Implement session ID rotation on login (new session ID after successful authentication)
Implement session ID rotation on privilege escalation (e.g., switching to admin views)
Add absolute session timeout (24h) and idle timeout (2h) with configurable values
Invalidate all other sessions when user changes password
Add session listing in user profile so users can see/revoke active sessions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shannon's auth agent tests session fixation, cookie theft, and session replay. Proper session management eliminates these vectors.

userStories.title: Implement progressive rate limiting and account lockout on auth endpoints
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want brute-force and credential stuffing attacks to be blocked by progressive rate limiting and temporary account lockouts, building on US-036 with server-side enforcement.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement IP-based rate limiting: 5 failed attempts per minute, 20 per hour, 50 per day
Implement account-based rate limiting: 5 failed attempts trigger 15-min lockout, 10 trigger 1-hour lockout, 20 trigger 24-hour lockout with email notification
Rate limiting applies to: /auth/sign-in, /auth/sign-up, /auth/reset-password, /auth/verify-otp, all OAuth callback endpoints
Add Cloudflare rate limiting rules as first line of defense before edge functions
Return generic 'Invalid credentials' message (never reveal if email exists)
Log all failed auth attempts with IP, user-agent, timestamp for security monitoring
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Extends US-036. Shannon handles complex auth flows including 2FA. Progressive rate limiting stops brute-force and credential stuffing regardless of how sophisticated the attack tool is.

userStories.title: Deploy comprehensive security headers via Cloudflare Pages
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want all HTTP responses to include defense-in-depth security headers so that the browser enforces multiple protection layers against common web attacks.
acceptanceCriteria: Create or update public/_headers (Cloudflare Pages) with all security headers
Headers include: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload
Headers include: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
Headers include: X-Frame-Options: DENY
Headers include: Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
Headers include: Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(), payment=(self)
Headers include: X-XSS-Protection: 0 (disabled in favor of CSP per modern best practice)
Headers include: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin
Headers include: Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy: same-origin
Verify headers present on production deployment using securityheaders.com (target A+ rating)
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Security headers are passive defenses that cost nothing at runtime. They prevent framing attacks, MIME sniffing, information leakage, and cross-origin exploitation — all vectors Shannon probes.

userStories.title: Integrate SAST (Static Application Security Testing) into CI/CD pipeline
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want automated static analysis scanning source code for vulnerability patterns on every PR, so that dangerous data flows (the same patterns Shannon's code analysis finds) are caught before they reach production.
acceptanceCriteria: Integrate Semgrep (open source) or CodeQL into GitHub Actions CI pipeline

Configure rules for: SQL injection sinks, XSS sinks (dangerouslySetInnerHTML, innerHTML), command injection (exec, spawn, eval), SSRF patterns (fetch/axios with user-controlled URLs), hardcoded secrets, insecure crypto usage
Create custom Semgrep rules for Supabase-specific patterns (e.g., .rpc() with interpolated strings)
Pipeline blocks PR merge on HIGH or CRITICAL severity findings
MEDIUM findings are flagged as warnings but don't block merge
Results are posted as GitHub PR review comments
Add .semgrepignore for false positive suppression with documented justifications
Document SAST setup in SECURITY.md
userStories.notes: Shannon's first phase is source code analysis to find data flow vulnerabilities. SAST does the same thing defensively — find and fix before an attacker does.

userStories.title: Integrate DAST (Dynamic Application Security Testing) into CI/CD pipeline
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want automated dynamic scanning that tests the running application for vulnerabilities on every deployment to staging, mirroring what Shannon does but running continuously as a defender.
acceptanceCriteria: Integrate OWASP ZAP (open source) or Nuclei into CI/CD pipeline targeting staging environment
Configure active scan policies for: injection testing, XSS testing, SSRF testing, authentication testing, security header validation
Run DAST scan against staging after every deployment (not on every PR — only on staging deploy)
HIGH severity findings create GitHub issues automatically with reproduction steps
CRITICAL findings trigger Slack/email alert to security team and block promotion to production
Scan completes in under 30 minutes (configure scan policy for speed vs depth tradeoff)
Monthly full-depth scan scheduled via cron with comprehensive report
Document DAST setup and findings review process in SECURITY.md
userStories.notes: This is essentially running our own Shannon-lite continuously. DAST tools test the running app with real attacks — exactly what Shannon does. Running this ourselves means we find issues before attackers do.

userStories.title: Automated dependency vulnerability scanning with blocking policy
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want every npm dependency scanned for known CVEs on every install and PR, so that vulnerable libraries (which Shannon can exploit) are caught before deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: Integrate npm audit or Snyk into GitHub Actions CI pipeline
Pipeline blocks merge on CRITICAL severity vulnerabilities in production dependencies
HIGH severity vulnerabilities generate warnings and create tracking issues
Configure allowlist for known false positives with documented justifications and expiry dates
Add scheduled weekly full scan that catches newly disclosed CVEs in existing dependencies
Pin all production dependencies to exact versions in package.json (remove ^ and ~ prefixes)
Add Dependabot or Renovate for automated dependency update PRs
Typecheck passes after any dependency updates
userStories.notes: Shannon benefits from known CVEs in dependencies. Automated scanning ensures we patch before exploitation.

userStories.title: Create internal security testing playbook with Shannon-aligned attack scenarios
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want a documented and executable pentest playbook that covers every attack vector Shannon uses, so that our team can self-test using the same methodology before an attacker does.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/security-tests/ directory with executable test scripts
Injection test suite: 50+ SQL injection payloads tested against all edge function endpoints that accept user input
XSS test suite: 50+ XSS payloads tested against all pages that render dynamic content (blog posts, recipe names, kid names, food names, AI-generated content)
SSRF test suite: 20+ SSRF payloads tested against parse-recipe, update-blog-image, and any URL-accepting endpoints
Auth test suite: Tests for session fixation, CSRF, privilege escalation, IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference), JWT manipulation
Each test script outputs PASS/FAIL with reproduction curl commands for failures
Add npm run test:security script that runs the full playbook
Playbook runs in under 15 minutes against a local or staging instance
Document each test's purpose and expected behavior in inline comments
userStories.notes: This is our internal Shannon. Instead of waiting for external pentests, we continuously validate our own defenses using the same attack patterns Shannon employs.

userStories.title: Configure Cloudflare WAF rules for OWASP top-10 attack patterns
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want Cloudflare WAF rules blocking known attack patterns at the edge, so that malicious requests are stopped before they reach our application servers or edge functions.
acceptanceCriteria: Enable Cloudflare Managed Ruleset for OWASP top-10 categories

Configure custom WAF rules to block: SQL injection patterns in query strings and request bodies, XSS payloads in all input fields, command injection patterns, path traversal attempts (../), large request bodies (>1MB except for image uploads)
Set WAF to 'challenge' mode for first 2 weeks to identify false positives, then switch to 'block'
Create WAF exception rules for legitimate use cases (e.g., blog editor HTML content, recipe instruction formatting)
Configure rate limiting rules: 100 requests/minute per IP for API endpoints, 10 requests/minute per IP for auth endpoints
Enable Bot Fight Mode for automated attack tool detection
Document WAF configuration and exception rationale in SECURITY.md
userStories.notes: WAF is the first line of defense — it blocks Shannon-style attacks at the network edge before they reach application code. Combined with application-level defenses, this creates defense in depth.

userStories.title: Implement security event monitoring and real-time alerting
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want all security-relevant events logged and monitored with real-time alerting, so that active attacks (like Shannon running against our app) are detected and responded to within minutes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a security_events table in Supabase to log: failed auth attempts, rate limit triggers, WAF blocks, CSRF validation failures, input sanitization rejections, suspicious user-agent patterns, unusual API usage patterns
Each event includes: timestamp, IP address, user_id (if authenticated), event_type, severity, request_path, request_method, user_agent, geo_location

Implement real-time alerting thresholds: >10 failed auths from same IP in 5 minutes → alert, >5 injection attempts from same IP in 1 minute → alert + auto-block, >3 SSRF attempts from any IP → alert + auto-block
Alert delivery via: Sentry (error tracking), email notification to security team, Slack webhook (if configured)
Add admin dashboard widget showing security events timeline and top blocked IPs
Implement auto-block: IPs triggering CRITICAL alerts are added to Cloudflare blocklist via API for 24 hours
Retain security events for 90 days minimum
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shannon runs for 1-1.5 hours generating hundreds of attack requests. Real-time monitoring would detect this pattern within minutes and auto-block the attacker IP.

userStories.title: Add bot detection and browser fingerprinting for automated attack prevention
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want automated browser-based attacks (like Shannon's headless Chrome exploitation) to be detected and challenged, so that automated pentesting tools cannot complete exploitation flows undetected.
acceptanceCriteria: Integrate Cloudflare Turnstile (free CAPTCHA alternative) on: login form, sign-up form, password reset form, contact/feedback forms

Implement browser fingerprinting checks on sensitive actions: detect headless Chrome indicators (navigator.webdriver, missing plugins, automation flags), detect rapid sequential form submissions (>3 submissions in 10 seconds), detect non-human mouse movement patterns on critical forms
Add honeypot fields (hidden form fields) to all public-facing forms — submissions with filled honeypot fields are silently rejected
Implement progressive challenges: first suspicious request gets Turnstile challenge, second gets rate limited, third gets IP blocked for 1 hour
Log all bot detection events to security_events table from US-099
Ensure accessibility: all CAPTCHA alternatives must be screen-reader accessible
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Shannon uses headless browser automation for exploitation. Bot detection and browser fingerprinting add friction that forces attackers to use more sophisticated (and detectable) evasion techniques.

userStories.title: Remove debug endpoints, development artifacts, and source maps from production builds
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want all debug endpoints, development-only routes, verbose error messages, and source maps removed from production builds, so that Shannon-style tools cannot use them for reconnaissance.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit and remove or gate all development-only routes behind VITE_ENV !== 'production' check
Ensure production error boundaries show generic messages (never stack traces, SQL errors, or internal paths)
Verify source maps are NOT deployed to public CDN (only uploaded to Sentry for error tracking)
Remove any console.log/console.error calls from production bundle (configure Vite/Terser to strip them)
Ensure /openapi.json and any API documentation endpoints are not publicly accessible in production
Verify no .env.example or configuration files with comments about infrastructure are in the public/ directory
Add build-time check that flags any development artifacts in the production bundle
npm run build succeeds and output contains no console.log statements
userStories.notes: Shannon's reconnaissance phase uses source code analysis. Removing debug artifacts, source maps, and verbose errors reduces the information available to attackers during their discovery phase.

userStories.title: Implement strict API schema validation on all edge function endpoints
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want every edge function to validate request payloads against a strict Zod schema, rejecting any unexpected fields or types, so that type confusion and unexpected input attacks are blocked.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Zod schemas for every edge function's expected input in functions/_shared/schemas/
Every edge function validates request body against its schema as the first operation after auth check
Invalid requests return 400 with a generic 'Invalid request' message (no schema details in response)
Schema validation strips unknown/extra fields (prevent prototype pollution and mass assignment)
Add maximum string length limits to all text fields (e.g., name: 200 chars, description: 5000 chars, URL: 2048 chars)
Add maximum array length limits to all array fields (prevent resource exhaustion)
Add maximum nesting depth to all object fields (prevent JSON parsing DoS)
Unit tests verify each schema rejects malformed input
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Strict schema validation is the most effective input defense. Shannon tests with unexpected types, extra fields, and oversized payloads. Zod schemas reject all of these structurally.

userStories.title: Implement Subresource Integrity (SRI) for all external scripts and stylesheets
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want all externally-loaded scripts and stylesheets to use SRI hashes, so that CDN compromise or supply chain attacks cannot inject malicious code.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit all <script> and <link> tags that load resources from external CDNs
Add integrity= attribute with SHA-384 or SHA-512 hash to each external resource
Add crossorigin='anonymous' attribute alongside SRI attributes
Configure Vite build to generate SRI hashes for all chunk files
Add build-time validation that all external resources have SRI hashes
Document any external resources that cannot use SRI (e.g., dynamic APIs) with risk justification
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: SRI prevents supply chain attacks where a compromised CDN serves malicious scripts. While Shannon doesn't directly test this, it's a defense-in-depth measure against code injection via third-party resources.

userStories.title: Implement IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) protection audit
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want every API endpoint that accesses user resources to verify ownership through RLS or explicit checks, so that users cannot access other users' data by manipulating IDs.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit all edge functions that accept record IDs (food_id, kid_id, recipe_id, plan_entry_id, grocery_item_id, etc.)
Verify each endpoint either: relies on RLS policy that filters by auth.uid(), or explicitly checks resource ownership before returning/modifying data
Test each endpoint with valid auth token but another user's resource IDs — all must return 403 or empty result
Audit all Supabase .select() calls in frontend to ensure they include user_id filter (defense in depth beyond RLS)
Add integration tests that create two test users and verify cross-user access is impossible for all resource types
Document IDOR audit findings and fixes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shannon's auth agent specifically tests for authorization bypass by manipulating resource identifiers. IDOR is one of the most common and impactful web vulnerabilities.

userStories.title: Implement security regression test suite that runs on every PR
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want a dedicated security test suite that runs automatically on every pull request, ensuring that previously fixed vulnerabilities cannot be reintroduced.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/test/security/ directory with dedicated security test files
Test file for each fixed vulnerability: injection-prevention.test.ts, xss-prevention.test.ts, ssrf-prevention.test.ts, auth-bypass-prevention.test.ts, csrf-protection.test.ts, idor-protection.test.ts
Each test file reproduces the original attack vector and verifies it is blocked
Add npm run test:security:unit that runs only security tests (fast, no external dependencies)
Integrate into GitHub Actions CI — security tests run on every PR alongside regular tests
Security test failures block PR merge (required status check)
Add test coverage for every US-085 through US-104 acceptance criterion that can be unit tested
Minimum 100 security test cases across all files
All tests pass
userStories.notes: The ultimate defense against regression. Shannon finds vulnerabilities that were previously fixed but reintroduced. This test suite ensures every fix is permanent.

userStories.title: Add explicit width/height attributes to all logo tags to prevent CLS
userStories.description: As a user on a slow connection, I want images to have reserved space before loading so that the page layout doesn't shift as images load (Cumulative Layout Shift).
acceptanceCriteria: Add width and height attributes to tags in src/components/Footer.tsx (both Logo-Green.png and Logo-White.png, h-8 = 32px height, calculate proportional width)
Add width and height attributes to tags in src/components/Navigation.tsx (both Logo-Green.png and Logo-White.png)
Add width and height attributes to tags in src/pages/Auth.tsx (both Logo-Green.png and Logo-White.png, h-10 = 40px)
Add width and height attributes to tags in src/components/AppSidebar.tsx
Add width and height attributes to in src/components/AppInstallPrompt.tsx
Verify no visual regressions in light and dark mode
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: CRITICAL: Missing width/height on images is one of the top CLS contributors flagged by PageSpeed Insights. Every img tag without explicit dimensions causes layout shift when the image loads.

userStories.title: Use <picture> elements with WebP sources for logos in Footer, Navigation, Auth, and AppSidebar
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want optimized image formats served so that pages load faster and use less bandwidth (Logo-Green.png is 261KB vs Logo-Green.webp at 28KB).
acceptanceCriteria: Replace bare with in src/components/Footer.tsx
Apply the same <picture> pattern for Logo-White.png/Logo-White.webp in Footer.tsx
Apply the same <picture> pattern in src/components/Navigation.tsx for both logos
Apply the same <picture> pattern in src/pages/Auth.tsx for both logos
Apply the same <picture> pattern in src/components/AppSidebar.tsx for both logos
Verify WebP files exist in public/ directory: Logo-Green.webp, Logo-White.webp
Verify no visual regressions in light and dark mode
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: CRITICAL: Logo-Green.png is 261KB while Logo-Green.webp is only 28KB (89% smaller). The Footer and Navigation are on every page, so serving PNGs wastes ~466KB per page load on every page of the site. This is one of the biggest quick wins for PageSpeed. | Completed 2026-05-06: generated WebP siblings for Icon_120/152/1024 (the only public PNGs without webp counterparts); favicon-16x16, favicon-32x32, and apple-touch-icon stay as PNG per browser/iOS spec. Conversion saved 79-94% per file via sharp@0.34 webp(quality:90).

Prioritizing its load can improve the LCP score significantly. | Completed 2026-05-06: ProductShowcase3D phone chassis stays bg-gray-900 in light mode but uses bg-neutral-800 + dark:ring-neutral-700 in dark mode so the phone does not blend into the dark background. Screen swapped from bg-white to bg-card text-card-foreground; carousel gradient and side-button greys also have dark variants. Mobile fallback grid was already using semantic tokens (bg-card).

userStories.title: Optimize font loading strategy to reduce render-blocking CSS
userStories.description: As a mobile user on a slow connection, I want fonts to load without blocking the initial render so that I see content faster (improves First Contentful Paint and LCP).
acceptanceCriteria: Evaluate the 8 @fontsource imports in src/main.tsx (4 Inter weights + 4 Nunito Sans weights) for render-blocking impact
Reduce font weights to only those actively used above the fold (Inter 400/600/700, Nunito Sans 700) and lazy-load the rest
Add font-display: swap to any custom @font-face declarations to prevent invisible text during load (FOIT)
Consider using font-display: optional for non-critical weights to prevent layout shift
Verify fonts render correctly and there is no flash of unstyled text (FOUT) or invisible text
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: PageSpeed Insights flags render-blocking resources as a top performance issue. Each font CSS file is a render-blocking resource that delays First Contentful Paint. Reducing from 8 to 4-5 critical font weights can save 100-200ms on mobile.

userStories.title: Add content-visibility: auto to below-fold landing page sections for faster rendering
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the browser to skip rendering off-screen content until I scroll to it, so that the initial page load is faster (reduces Total Blocking Time).
acceptanceCriteria: Add content-visibility: auto with appropriate contain-intrinsic-size to sections below the hero in Landing.tsx (Pain Points, How It Works, Features, Testimonials, Free Tools, FAQ, CTA sections)
Set contain-intrinsic-size to approximate heights for each section to prevent scroll bar jumping
Create a utility CSS class like .cv-auto in index.css or mobile-first.css for reuse
Verify scroll behavior works correctly and sections render as the user scrolls
Test on mobile viewport sizes to ensure no layout issues
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: content-visibility: auto is a CSS containment property that tells the browser to skip rendering off-screen elements. This can dramatically reduce initial rendering time for long pages like the Landing page with 10+ sections. Google's own performance guidelines recommend this.

userStories.title: Reduce index.html inline JSON-LD structured data payload size

npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: The index.html has ~160 lines of formatted JSON-LD structured data. Minifying this reduces the HTML payload size by several KB, which helps with Time to First Byte and First Contentful Paint, especially on mobile networks.

userStories.title: Defer non-critical third-party scripts and reduce main thread blocking time
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the page to be interactive as quickly as possible by deferring non-essential JavaScript execution (improves Total Blocking Time and Time to Interactive).
acceptanceCriteria: Verify Google Analytics script in index.html continues to load after 2-second delay (already implemented, confirm no regression)
Audit src/main.tsx for synchronous operations that block rendering: validateEnv(), initializeSentry()
Move Sentry initialization to after first render using requestIdleCallback or setTimeout
Move validateEnv() to only run in development mode or make it non-blocking
Verify Web Vitals initialization remains non-blocking (already async, confirm)
Measure improvement: main thread blocking time should decrease
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Total Blocking Time (TBT) directly impacts PageSpeed Performance score. Every synchronous operation in the critical path (before first render) adds to TBT. Sentry init and env validation are not needed before first paint.

userStories.title: Add preconnect hints for all critical third-party origins

acceptanceCriteria: Verify preconnect to api.tryeatpal.com exists in index.html (already present)
Add <link rel='preconnect' href='https://www.googletagmanager.com'> alongside the existing dns-prefetch

Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Preconnect saves 100-300ms per third-party origin on mobile by starting DNS+TCP+TLS early. dns-prefetch only does DNS resolution, while preconnect does the full connection setup. | IMPLEMENTED: Android app audit session

userStories.title: Optimize Tailwind CSS output by purging unused styles

userStories.notes: loading='lazy' tells the browser to defer loading images until they are near the viewport. decoding='async' prevents image decoding from blocking the main thread. Both are quick wins for PageSpeed. | IMPLEMENTED: Android app audit session

userStories.notes: iOS UX AUDIT - Tier 2: DONE 2026-04-17. New Intents/ folder with: GroceryAppIntents.swift (AddGroceryItemIntent — item: String, pipes through GroceryTextParser so 'two pounds of chicken' resolves qty/unit/category; WhatsForDinnerIntent — fetches today's plan_entries with dinner slot across all kids, resolves via DataService.shared.fetchFoods/fetchRecipes/fetchKids, composes a friendly dialog). MealAppIntents.swift (MealSlotAppEnum with Siri-facing breakfast/lunch/dinner/snack mapped to domain slots via domainSlotRawValues — snack covers both snack1 and snack2; MealResultAppEnum; LogMealResultIntent — updates all matching plan_entries for today+slot with the chosen result). EatPalAppShortcuts.swift AppShortcutsProvider registers 3+ phrases per intent using (.applicationName) placeholder. All three intents run with openAppWhenRun: false — background execution via DataService.shared which authenticates from the keychain-stored Supabase session. userStories.notes: iOS UX AUDIT - Tier 2: DONE 2026-04-17. New EatPalShareExtension Xcode target in project.yml with app-extension type, com.eatpal.app.share bundle id, SKIP_INSTALL YES, SUPABASE_URL/ANON_KEY env-substituted in generated Info.plist, NSExtensionActivationSupportsWebURLWithMaxCount=1 + NSExtensionActivationSupportsText=true, NSExtensionPrincipalClass=$(PRODUCT_MODULE_NAME).ShareViewController. Added EatPalShareExtension.entitlements with group.com.eatpal.app. New Shared/PendingRecipeImport.swift defines Codable struct + PendingRecipeImportQueue (load/enqueue/remove/clear) backed by App Group UserDefaults key 'pending_recipe_imports'. AppState.drainPendingRecipeImports() called after loadAllData successfully completes — iterates queue, constructs Recipe (sourceType='share_extension'), calls addRecipe, removes only successful items from queue (failed stay for next launch). userStories.title: iOS: HealthKit nutrition write-back for logged meals

acceptanceCriteria: Add HealthKit capability to ios/EatPal/EatPal.entitlements

userStories.notes: iOS UX AUDIT - Tier 2: DONE 2026-04-17. EatPal.entitlements gained com.apple.developer.healthkit (true). Info.plist gained NSHealthShareUsageDescription + NSHealthUpdateUsageDescription. New Services/HealthKitService.swift with isAvailable (HKHealthStore.isHealthDataAvailable), isEnabled (UserDefaults-backed user opt-in distinct from system authorization), requestAuthorization async, writeTypes covering dietaryEnergyConsumed / dietaryProtein / dietaryCarbohydrates / dietaryFatTotal + the .food correlation type, and writeMeal(calories:proteinGrams:carbsGrams:fatGrams:mealDate:mealName:) which bundles macros into one HKCorrelation so Health shows a single meal entry (not four orphan samples). Metadata uses HKMetadataKeyFoodType = mealName, no PII. AppState.updatePlanEntry now fires Task { await writeHealthSample(for:) } when result becomes MealResult.ate.rawValue and the entry has a linked recipe with nutritionInfo. New Views/Settings/HealthSettingsView.swift provides the toggle UI — flipping on triggers requestAuthorization, shows 'Open Settings' alert on denial, links to the Health app with x-apple-health:// URL. SettingsView got a new Preferences row 'Sync to Health' navigating into HealthSettingsView. Food-only plan entries (no linked recipe with nutrition) are silently skipped. Not yet verified on device.

userStories.title: iOS: Finish OfflineStore as durable write queue + wire NetworkMonitor
userStories.description: As an iOS user, I want edits I make offline (add to grocery, log meal, +1 pantry) to sync automatically when I'm back online, so I can use the app on a plane or in a store basement.
acceptanceCriteria: Promote ios/EatPal/EatPal/Services/OfflineStore.swift from placeholder to durable append-only write queue on disk (JSONEncoder to Application Support directory)
DataService writes route through OfflineStore first when NetworkMonitor reports offline or when a network call fails
OfflineStore.flush() triggered by NetworkMonitor.onConnectivityRestored
Last-write-wins conflict resolution for grocery_items and pantry qty; RealtimeService suppresses echoes of queued writes
Queue visible in Settings (diagnostic view showing pending count + last error)
Unit tests for queue encode/decode, flush ordering, failure retry in EatPalTests
Swift builds cleanly
userStories.notes: iOS UX AUDIT - Tier 4: DONE 2026-04-17 (first pass). OfflineStore promoted to a durable SwiftData-backed write queue with typed enqueue helpers (enqueueInsert<T: Encodable>, enqueueUpdate, enqueueDelete) keyed by Table enum (foods / kids / recipes / plan_entries / grocery_items). syncPendingMutations now dispatches per-table and decodes JSON payloads back into domain structs, calling client.from(table).insert/update/delete. Sent Sentry breadcrumbs + error capture on sync failure. Published pendingMutationCount + isSyncing + lastSyncError so the UI can surface diagnostics. NetworkMonitor now tracks offline→online transitions and drains the queue automatically (`if !wasConnected && nowConnected { await OfflineStore.shared.syncPendingMutations() }`). AppState.addGroceryItem / deleteGroceryItem / toggleGroceryItem gained an isNetworkError(_:) classifier (URLError / NSURLErrorDomain codes) — on network errors the optimistic state is preserved and the mutation enqueued with a 'Queued for sync' toast. Remaining grocery + plan + food mutation methods can be retrofitted with the same pattern. Not yet verified on device.

Breadcrumbs on navigation (TabView selection), DataService calls, and AuthViewModel state transitions
Attach user context (user id only, no email/name) after sign-in; clear on sign-out
PII scrubbing before send (beforeSend hook)
Crash-free session rate visible in Sentry dashboard
Disable in DEBUG builds to avoid noise
Swift builds cleanly
userStories.notes: iOS UX AUDIT - Tier 4: DONE 2026-04-17. Added sentry-cocoa (from 8.0.0) SPM dep in project.yml + dependency on main EatPal target. Info.plist gained SENTRY_DSN key env-substituted same as Supabase secrets. New Services/SentryService.swift exposes configure() (no-op in DEBUG, no-op when DSN missing/unresolved), setUserId(_:), leaveBreadcrumb(category:message:level:data:), capture(_:extras:), with PII scrubbing (event.user email/name/ip stripped, request.data + cookies dropped, sensitive keys redacted in extras/headers/breadcrumb data). SentrySDK options: env production, releaseName from CFBundleShortVersionString+CFBundleVersion, auto session tracking, tracesSampleRate 0.2, profilesSampleRate 0.1. EatPalApp.init() calls SentryService.configure() before Tips.configure. AuthViewModel startListeningForAuthChanges now calls SentryService.setUserId on sign-in (with session.user.id.uuidString) and nils out on sign-out, plus breadcrumbs for 'session restored', 'signed in', 'signed out'. Not yet verified — xcodegen generate required to resolve SPM.

New Services/WidgetSnapshot.swift namespaces the App Group id (group.com.eatpal.app), key constants (meals, grocery_count, pantry_low_count, tonight_dish, try_bite_streak, last_updated_at), and widget kind (EatPalMealWidget). Exposes Payload struct + write(_:) (debounced 500ms) and writeImmediately(_:). Persists to UserDefaults(suiteName: appGroup) and calls WidgetCenter.shared.reloadTimelines(ofKind:). AppState gained an init() with setupWidgetSnapshotSync() — Combine sink on $planEntries.combineLatest($foods, $groceryItems, $recipes) debounced 500ms, calling refreshWidgetSnapshot() which composes today's meals for the active kid, pantry-low count (qty > 0 && qty <= 2), unchecked grocery count, and tonight's dish. userStories.title: Android Native: seed repository + database-layer parity with iOS
userStories.description: As an Android developer, I want a fresh native Kotlin/Jetpack Compose module that talks to Supabase with the same contract as iOS, so every row round-trips between platforms without a translation layer.
acceptanceCriteria: New `android-native/` Gradle module co-exists alongside the existing RN/Expo `android/` shell (do not delete the RN shell yet)
Kotlin 2.1 + Compose BOM 2024.12.01 + Hilt 2.52 + Room 2.6 + DataStore 1.1 + supabase-kt 3.x + Sentry-Android + Coil + Accompanist
Version catalog at `android-native/gradle/libs.versions.toml`; no hardcoded versions in build.gradle.kts files
Secrets (`SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`, `SENTRY_DSN`) loaded from `env.local.properties` or env vars into BuildConfig — never committed
Models (Food, Kid, Recipe, PlanEntry, GroceryItem, GroceryList, ChatMessage, NutritionInfo) as @Serializable data classes with @SerialName snake_case matching iOS CodingKeys exactly
Room cache entities (CachedFood / CachedKid / CachedGroceryItem / PendingMutation) mirror iOS SwiftData models

Services ported with 1:1 method parity: SupabaseClientProvider, AuthService, DataService, RealtimeService (PostgresChangeFlow fan-out into AppStateStore), OfflineStore (typed enqueueInsert/enqueueUpdate/enqueueDelete + ordered replay with stop-on-first-failure), NetworkMonitor (ConnectivityManager callback + auto-flush on reconnect)
AppStateStore singleton (StateFlow equivalents of iOS @Published) with same optimistic-update-then-rollback add/update/delete methods for each entity
Hilt DataModule provides Room DB + Json + DAOs; EatPalApplication starts Sentry + NetworkMonitor
MainActivity hosts a minimal Compose shell that reacts to SessionStatus and kicks AppStateStore.loadAllData() on Authenticated
Module compiles and installs on API 26+ device
userStories.notes: Android native: DONE 2026-04-20 (first pass, no device verification). Scaffolded `android-native/` with settings.gradle.kts + build.gradle.kts + libs.versions.toml + app/build.gradle.kts (SUPABASE_URL/ANON_KEY/SENTRY_DSN BuildConfig wiring), proguard rules for kotlinx.serialization + supabase-kt, AndroidManifest with INTERNET / ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE / POST_NOTIFICATIONS + deep link intent filter for tryeatpal.com, data_extraction_rules.xml excluding sharedpref/database from cloud backup. Ported all 7 iOS models with matching snake_case SerialNames (Food, Kid, Recipe, PlanEntry, GroceryItem, GroceryList, ChatMessage, NutritionInfo) plus enums (FoodCategory, MealSlot, MealResult). Room offline layer mirrors iOS: CachedFood/CachedKid/CachedGroceryItem/PendingMutation entities, CacheDao + PendingMutationDao (observeCount Flow for UI), EatPalDatabase. Services ported: SupabaseClientProvider (Auth/Postgrest/Realtime/Storage/Functions installed, alwaysAutoRefresh + autoLoadFromStorage), AuthService (email/password, Google IDToken equivalent of Apple Sign-In, reset/update/signOut, deleteAccount via functions.invoke('delete-account')), DataService (fetch*/insert*/update*/delete* for foods/kids/recipes/plan_entries/grocery_items + fetchGroceryLists, ensureUserId guardrail + UUID_REGEX validation for plan_entries.kid_id/food_id matching iOS DataServiceError.missingKid/missingFood), OfflineStore (Mutex-gated syncPendingMutations, per-table insert/update replay via kotlinx.serialization, observable pendingMutationCount/isSyncing/lastSyncError StateFlows), RealtimeService (PostgresAction.Insert/Update/Delete flow per channel, fan-out into AppStateStore on* handlers), NetworkMonitor (ConnectivityManager.NetworkCallback + isNetworkError classifier + auto-drain on reconnect). AppStateStore replaces iOS AppState — all 20+ optimistic add/update/delete methods, same rollback-on-failure + enqueue-on-network-error pattern for grocery items. Hilt DataModule, EatPalApplication (Sentry init skipped in debug, NetworkMonitor.start()), MainActivity (@AndroidEntryPoint, single Compose host), RootScreen + RootViewModel (reacts to SessionStatus, kicks loadAllData). README documents stack, build instructions, and the snake_case wire contract as the load-bearing cross-platform guarantee. NOT YET VERIFIED: no `./gradlew assembleDebug` run, no device install. Next-step checklist in follow-up US-201 through US-220.

userStories.title: Android Native: Gradle wrapper + CI build verification
userStories.description: As an Android developer, I want `./gradlew assembleDebug` to pass locally and on CI, so every PR that touches android-native is green before merge.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar + gradle-wrapper.properties (Gradle 8.10+) at `android-native/gradle/wrapper/`

Verify supabase-kt 3.x API matches the code as written (PostgrestRequestBuilder update/filter lambda syntax, channel.postgresChangeFlow, PostgresAction sealed class shape) — adjust if the Kotlin library has diverged from what we scaffolded
Add GitHub Actions job `android-native-build.yml` that runs assembleDebug + lint on every PR touching `android-native/**`
Android lint passes with zero errors (warnings acceptable short-term)
userStories.notes: Android native: DONE 2026-04-21. Gradle wrapper copied from android/, pinned Gradle 8.10.2. env.local.properties added with placeholders; .gitignore updated. supabase-kt 3.0.2 API verified by a real build — three drift fixes landed: java.util.Properties needed explicit import in build.gradle.kts, `functions.invoke(...).body<T>()` dropped (supabase-kt 3.x throws on non-2xx instead), inline reified `enqueueInsert/Update` moved `pendingDao`/`json` to `@PublishedApi internal`. Hilt bumped 2.52 → 2.54 to match Kotlin 2.1 metadata. Android lint green. CI workflow at .github/workflows/android-native-ci.yml runs testDebugUnitTest + lintDebug + assembleDebug on every PR touching android-native/, uploads app-debug.apk + lint-report artifacts (14d retention). 16 unit tests passing (GroceryTextParser + models round-trip). STILL OPEN: running the workflow on a real PR to verify the SDK manager accepts the targetSdk, instrumentation tests.

userStories.title: Android Native: Meal Plan screen + AI Meal Plan generator
userStories.description: As an Android user, I want weekly meal planning and AI-generated plans with the same UX as iOS.
acceptanceCriteria: MealPlanScreen: week picker, per-kid swim lanes, per-slot meal cards, drag-to-reschedule (Compose foundation.draggable)
AddPlanEntry sheet with food/recipe picker + slot selection
AIMealPlanScreen invokes the `generate-meal-plan` edge function (Supabase Functions) — mirrors AIMealService
Result card shows generated entries with Approve / Regenerate / Edit actions
On approve, bulk-inserts PlanEntry rows via AppStateStore
userStories.notes: Android native: DONE 2026-04-22. MealPlanScreen (week view) + AIMealPlanScreen both shipped. AIMealService (data/remote/AIMealService.kt) ports iOS service with identical snake_case payload: kid_id/date/safe_foods/try_bite_foods/recent_meals (last 21)/preferences(name/age/pickiness_level/allergens/texture_preferences/flavor_preferences). Calls `generate-meal-suggestions` edge function via supabase-kt Functions + Ktor setBody, parses response with ListSerializer<MealSuggestion>, falls back to local shuffle of safe foods on any exception (same copy as iOS). Result data class exposes both suggestions and error so UI distinguishes fresh-from-AI vs fell-back. AIMealPlanScreen composable: date field, Generate button with loading state, per-slot SuggestionCard (reasoning + nutrition note + Add FilterChip), Clear + Approve-all actions. AIMealPlanViewModel.acceptAll bulk-creates PlanEntry rows via AppStateStore (offline-queue semantics apply). resolveFoodId validates the returned food_id is a proper UUID; otherwise falls back to case-insensitive name match against pantry — safer than letting Postgres reject the insert. Route AI_MEAL_PLAN wired in MainNavigation + MoreScreen. STILL OPEN: drag-to-reschedule on MealPlanScreen, meal-result logging (ate/tasted/refused inline on plan entries).

userStories.notes: Android native: DONE 2026-04-22. Full three-mode import (text + voice + photo) all shipped and wired into GroceryScreen. GroceryTextParser (domain/GroceryTextParser.kt) ports iOS parser — same dictionaries, split strategy, confidence heuristics, stripListMarkers, 10 unit tests. TextImportSheet (paste list → live preview → bulk insert). VoiceImportSheet (ui/grocery/VoiceImportSheet.kt) uses android.speech.SpeechRecognizer directly with RECORD_AUDIO permission via Accompanist, streams partial results into the transcript field, editable before commit, feeds same GroceryTextParser pipeline. userStories.title: Android Native: image upload service → Supabase Storage (parity with iOS ImageUploadService)
userStories.description: As an Android user, I want to upload profile pictures and recipe images to the same Supabase Storage buckets iOS uses.
acceptanceCriteria: ImageUploadService.kt ports iOS ImageUploadService.swift (83 lines) — same bucket names, same path conventions (userId/timestamp.jpg)

Returns public URL string that can be stored in Kid.profilePictureUrl / Recipe.imageUrl / GroceryItem.photoUrl
Uses supabase.storage.from(bucket).upload(...)
userStories.notes: Android native: DONE 2026-04-21 (service only — no UI caller yet). data/remote/ImageUploadService.kt ports iOS ImageUploadService.swift. Same bucket ('images'), same folder enum (FOODS / RECIPES / KIDS), same JPEG quality 80 + 1024px max-dim compression, same `{folder}/{entityId}-{epochSeconds}.jpg` path scheme so URLs interchange with iOS. Uses supabase-kt storage.upload(path, bytes) with upsert=true + ContentType.Image.JPEG; returns storage.from(bucket).publicUrl(path). Reads Uri via contentResolver, two-pass BitmapFactory (bounds-only first to compute inSampleSize, then full decode) so huge camera photos don't OOM. Delete helper included. STILL OPEN: wire into KidsScreen profile-picture picker + RecipeEditor image upload + pantry barcode photo. assembleDebug green.

Uses Ktor setBody + contentType(Json) + bodyAsText, decoded via json.decodeFromString<CoachResponse>. ui/aicoach/AICoachScreen Compose UI: conversation LazyColumn with message bubbles (user = primaryContainer, assistant = surfaceVariant), auto-scroll to last message, typing indicator while isLoading, OutlinedTextField + send IconButton with Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled.Send. AICoachViewModel reads active kid + foods from AppStateStore for context. Route wired at Routes.AI_COACH.

Same meal_plan_templates table shape — templates created on iOS work here. userStories.title: Android Native: FCM push + local notification parity with iOS NotificationService
userStories.description: As an Android user, I want push notifications (meal reminders, family sharing) and local reminders matching iOS behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Add firebase-messaging dependency + google-services.json (excluded from VCS; documented in README)
NotificationService.kt ports iOS version (161 lines) — requestAuthorization, registers FCM token, stores in push_notifications table (same schema as iOS)
Local notifications via NotificationManagerCompat for meal-reminder slots (breakfast/lunch/dinner)
POST_NOTIFICATIONS runtime permission handled on API 33+
Foreground service not needed — schedule via WorkManager + setExactAndAllowWhileIdle where appropriate
Notification channels: reminders, family_activity, tips
userStories.notes: Android native: SCAFFOLDED 2026-04-22. Local notifications fully wired (previous pass). FCM scaffolding added: firebase-bom 33.7.0 + firebase-messaging-ktx on compile classpath, EatPalMessagingService extends FirebaseMessagingService (@AndroidEntryPoint) — onNewToken routes through PushTokenService, onMessageReceived posts a notification on the channel named in the FCM data payload. PushTokenService (data/remote/PushTokenService.kt) upserts into the shared push_notifications table with the same {user_id, token, platform='android', app_version} shape iOS writes so server senders can target both platforms with one query. Manifest registers the service with the com.google.firebase.MESSAGING_EVENT filter. STILL REQUIRED to activate: (1) add `app/google-services.json` from the Firebase console, (2) add `classpath('com.google.gms:google-services:4.4.2')` to the root build.gradle, (3) apply `id("com.google.gms.google-services")` plugin to `:app`. Without those three steps FirebaseApp never initializes and onNewToken never fires — by design, so the app still builds without a Firebase project configured. Notification-channel-to-UI wiring (per-kid reminder scheduling) is a separate follow-up.

userStories.notes: Android native: PARTIAL 2026-04-21. data/remote/PlayBillingService.kt wraps BillingClient 7.1.1: connect() as suspendCancellableCoroutine, loadProducts() queries the 3 tiers (basic/family/premium monthly) via QueryProductDetailsParams (ProductType.SUBS), launchPurchaseFlow accepts an Activity + Product and uses offerToken from subscriptionOfferDetails, restorePurchases queries + surfaces via activePurchases StateFlow, handlePurchase acknowledges + posts token to `verify-play-subscription` edge function (fire-and-forget from GlobalScope since this is a singleton; WorkManager retry tracked as follow-up). ui/paywall/PaywallScreen renders the 3 tiers with feature bullet lists matching iOS PaywallView copy intent, formattedPrice from pricingPhaseList, Subscribe button hooks launchPurchaseFlow off LocalContext cast to Activity, Restore button. PaywallViewModel combines local UiState with billing.products/activePurchases StateFlows. BILLING permission in manifest. Paywall route wired. STILL OPEN: create `verify-play-subscription` edge function on Supabase (parity with verify-apple-subscription), create real SKUs in Play Console matching PlayBillingService.Tier productIds, upload a signed AAB to internal testing track for license testers, wire entitlement flag from user_subscriptions table into gating UI (e.g., AI coach unlimited chats guard, family kid limit).

userStories.title: Android Native: Health Connect integration (parity with iOS HealthKitService / US-144)

acceptanceCriteria: Add androidx.health.connect:connect-client dependency

Permission request flow via PermissionController.createRequestPermissionResultContract

userStories.notes: Android native: DONE 2026-04-21. data/local/HealthConnectService.kt wraps androidx.health.connect:connect-client 1.1.0-alpha07. Opt-in flag persisted in DataStore (`eatpal_health`, key `enabled`). writeMeal(calories, proteinGrams, carbsGrams, fatGrams, mealDate, mealName) constructs a NutritionRecord pinned at noon local time on mealDate (same time-anchor approach as iOS). permissions set exposes WRITE permission for NutritionRecord. isAvailable checks HealthConnectClient.getSdkStatus == SDK_AVAILABLE. AppStateStore.updatePlanEntry now calls writeHealthSample when updates.result == MealResult.ATE.key — same guard as iOS US-144, silently skipped when not available or not enabled or recipe has no nutrition_info. SettingsScreen Health Connect card: shows unavailable message when SDK isn't installed, otherwise a Switch that toggles the opt-in via SettingsViewModel.setHealthEnabled. STILL OPEN: permission request flow (HealthConnectClient.permissionController.createRequestPermissionResultContract launched from an Activity) — current state requires the user to manually grant in Health Connect app settings, UI for last-synced diagnostics.

userStories.title: Android Native: Glance widget (parity with iOS EatPalWidget / US-152)

Glance widget receiver + AppWidgetProvider mirroring iOS EatPalWidget layout

AppStateStore has a combined StateFlow (foods + planEntries + groceryItems + recipes) debounced 500ms that writes the snapshot and calls GlanceAppWidgetManager.update(...)
Widget reads exclusively from DataStore — no network
Lock screen widget variant via Glance's LazyColumn stub
userStories.notes: Android native: DONE 2026-04-21. widget/EatPalWidget.kt (GlanceAppWidget) renders today's meals + grocery count + pantry-low count, no network — reads exclusively from WidgetSnapshotStore (DataStore-backed). EatPalWidgetReceiver binds it via the Glance infrastructure. data/local/WidgetSnapshotStore.kt persists meals/groceryCount/pantryLowCount/tonightDish/tryBiteStreak/lastUpdatedAt, uses ListSerializer(Meal.serializer()) for typed meals round-trip. AppStateStore.setupWidgetSnapshotSync combines planEntries+foods+recipes+groceryItems+activeKidId flows, debounced 500ms (matches iOS), calls widgetSnapshotStore.write then EatPalWidget().updateAll(context) on each change. EntryPointAccessors surfaces the store to the widget since GlanceAppWidget can't take constructor deps. Widget provider XML registered with 250x110dp min size, 30-min update period, home_screen category. Guarded with a widget_loading.xml placeholder initialLayout since Glance's built-in fallback isn't in this artifact. STILL OPEN: try-bite streak calc (currently 0 placeholder per iOS), lock-screen widget variant, widget deep-links into the correct tab.

Each has an Assistant <capability> declaration: add_grocery + log_meal use actions.intent.CREATE_THING with a thing.name parameter, open_dashboard uses actions.intent.GET_THING. util/DeepLinkHandler.kt resolves both tryeatpal:// and https://tryeatpal.com/<path> URIs onto Routes constants covering all 13 destinations. MainActivity captures the intent in onCreate + onNewIntent via a pendingRoute MutableStateFlow, passes to RootScreen which fires a LaunchedEffect → navController.navigate({ launchSingleTop = true }) once the user is authenticated + past onboarding. Auto-consumed after use so re-orientation doesn't re-navigate. Device verification (real Assistant voice → App Action intent match) still pending — Google Play Console App Actions review is separate from the code here.

userStories.title: Android Native: Share target for recipe URLs (parity with iOS Share Extension / US-143)
userStories.description: As an Android user, I want to share a recipe URL from Chrome/Instagram into EatPal and have it imported, same as iOS Share Extension.
acceptanceCriteria: Intent-filter for ACTION_SEND with mimeType text/plain (URL) in AndroidManifest
ShareReceiverActivity enqueues a PendingRecipeImport into DataStore (mirrors iOS PendingRecipeImportQueue)

userStories.notes: Android native: DONE 2026-04-21 (queue-only — drain pending follow-up). AndroidManifest adds a ShareReceiverActivity with an ACTION_SEND text/plain intent filter. ui/share/ShareReceiverActivity extracts the first URL from EXTRA_TEXT via a simple regex, enqueues a PendingRecipeImportStore.PendingImport (DataStore-backed, id + sourceUrl + createdAt), toasts 'Recipe queued', and relaunches MainActivity. data/local/PendingRecipeImportStore.kt mirrors iOS PendingRecipeImportQueue (enqueue, load, remove, clear) serialized via ListSerializer(PendingImport.serializer()). STILL OPEN: AppStateStore.drainPendingRecipeImports — reads the queue after sign-in, calls the `parse-recipe-url` edge function for each URL (same one iOS ShareExtensionAPI uses), inserts a Recipe row with source_type='share_extension', removes the pending entry on success.

userStories.title: Android Native: Settings + Subscription + Onboarding + ancillary screens
userStories.description: As an Android user, I want the same settings, subscription management, onboarding, picky-eater quiz, food chaining, and progress screens as iOS.

PickyEaterQuizScreen composable ports iOS PickyEaterQuizView
FoodChainingScreen composable ports iOS FoodChainingView
ProgressDashboardScreen composable with charts (androidx.compose + custom Canvas or Vico charts lib) mirroring iOS ProgressDashboardView

userStories.notes: Android native: DONE 2026-04-22. All acceptance criteria met: SettingsScreen (account / sync / health-connect / diagnostics / sign out / delete account), OnboardingScreen (3-step welcome with DataStore persistence), PaywallScreen (3 tiers via Play Billing), PickyQuizScreen (3-question quiz → KidUpdate.pickinessLevel), FoodChainingScreen (same-category candidate chains from safe foods), ProgressScreen (30-day variety + outcomes + pantry breadth + recipe counts), DeepLinkHandler (13-destination resolution for tryeatpal:// and https://tryeatpal.com/* URIs, wired into MainActivity.onCreate + onNewIntent → RootScreen), HapticManager (7 styles: success/warning/error/selection + light/medium/heavy impact matching iOS UIImpactFeedbackStyle), ToastController (success/error/info/warning named variants + low-level show() with action label + dismiss callback matching iOS sonner API), Spacing/Theme tokens. userStories.title: Android Native: unit + instrumentation test coverage for database layer
userStories.description: As an Android developer, I want the same test confidence iOS has — the database layer is load-bearing and must be covered.
acceptanceCriteria: Unit tests for AppStateStore optimistic update + rollback paths (all 5 entities × add/update/delete)
Unit tests for OfflineStore enqueue/replay/stop-on-failure ordering
Unit tests for Food/Kid/Recipe/PlanEntry/GroceryItem JSON round-trip against known-good fixtures captured from the iOS encoder
Instrumentation test: Room migration smoke (drop + recreate, cache round-trip)
Instrumentation test: NetworkMonitor.isNetworkError classifier against common IOException subtypes
Target coverage: 80%+ on data + domain packages
userStories.notes: Android native: DONE 2026-04-22. 46 unit tests passing across 6 suites. Added MockK 1.13.13 + Turbine 1.2.0 to testImplementation. New suites: ApplyingTest (6 cases — Food/Kid/Recipe/PlanEntry/GroceryItem .applying(update) preserves untouched fields, Kid covers all 19 update-struct fields end-to-end), AppStateStoreRealtimeTest (10 cases — onFoodInserted/Updated/Deleted, onKidInserted/Deleted + no-op-on-missing-id, onGroceryInserted+Updated toggle round-trip, onPlanInserted/Deleted, onRecipeUpdated, setActiveKid round-trip) — uses MockK with relaxed=true stubs for the 7 AppStateStore constructor deps, so we exercise the pure StateFlow mutations without faking supabase-kt. Pre-existing suites unchanged: GroceryTextParser (10), ModelsSerializationTest (6), NetworkErrorClassifierTest (7), PasswordValidatorTest (7). CI workflow runs testDebugUnitTest on every PR. STILL OPEN: OfflineStore.syncPendingMutations replay-ordering + stop-on-first-failure tests (needs a fake PendingMutationDao that can inject failure on a specific row), optimistic-rollback tests on AppStateStore.add/update/delete methods (needs DataService mock that throws controllably — doable with MockK, just not done this pass), Room migration smoke test (instrumentation required), Kover / JaCoCo coverage measurement.

userStories.title: iOS Recipes: paste-a-URL recipe import in AddRecipeView
userStories.description: As a parent who finds a recipe on a website, I want to paste the URL into the in-app Add Recipe form (without using the Share Sheet) so that I can capture recipes from Safari Reader Mode, ChatGPT replies, Notes, or messages where the share sheet is awkward or unavailable. The Share Extension already calls a working `parse-recipe` edge function (see `ios/EatPal/EatPalShare/ShareExtensionAPI.swift`); we just need to surface the same import path in the main app.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a 'Import from URL' section at the top of `ios/EatPal/EatPal/Views/Recipes/RecipesView.swift` AddRecipeView (lines 597+) with a TextField + 'Fetch' button
Lift the ParsedRecipe / parse-recipe networking out of the share extension into a shared `Services/RecipeImportService.swift` callable from both share-ext and main app

On Fetch: show progress indicator, call `parse-recipe` edge function, pre-fill name/description/instructions/prepTime/cookTime/servings/imageUrl/tags fields and auto-create any missing Food rows for ingredients via fuzzy match against `appState.foods`
If parsing fails show inline error with 'Try again' + 'Enter manually' fallback (do not dismiss the sheet)
Detect URL automatically when user pastes — if pasteboard contains an http(s) URL on sheet open, prompt 'Import from <domain>?' (one-tap)
Toast on success: 'Imported recipe from allrecipes.com (8 ingredients)'
Telemetry event `recipe_imported_via_paste` with domain + ingredient_count
userStories.notes: iOS: DONE 2026-04-24. Added `Services/RecipeImportService.swift` (calls the same `parse-recipe` edge function the share extension uses, via SupabaseManager.client). AddRecipeView gains a new 'Import from URL' section at the top with TextField + Fetch button, inline progress + error state, success pill 'Imported from <domain>'. Auto-detect: on sheet appear, if pasteboard contains an http(s) URL and the form is empty, show a confirmation alert 'Import from <host>?' — one-tap imports. Parsed fields pre-fill name/description/instructions/prepTime/cookTime/servings + remoteImageUrl; ingredients fuzzy-matched against `appState.foods` and linked as selectedFoodIds; unmatched ingredients appended to a new Additional Ingredients section. createRecipe() persists `sourceUrl` + `sourceType='url_paste'` on the Recipe row. Haptic success/error on import. STILL OPEN: telemetry event (blocked on US-245 Analytics), stronger ingredient fuzzy matching (current: exact / substring), 'Try again' + 'Enter manually' buttons on error (current: error text + cleared URL).

userStories.title: iOS Recipes: live ingredient scaling by serving count in RecipeDetailView
userStories.description: As a parent cooking for a different number of eaters than the recipe was written for, I want to bump servings up/down and see all ingredient quantities scale in real-time so I don't have to do mental math (and so the 'Add ingredients to grocery' action uses the scaled amounts). RecipeDetailView (`ios/EatPal/EatPal/Views/Recipes/RecipesView.swift:274`) currently shows static servings text with no scaler.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a Stepper(value: $displayServings, in: 1...20) below the current servings text
Parse `recipe.servings` (currently free-form String like '4' or 'Serves 4-6') with a tolerant regex into `originalServings: Int` — fall back to 1 if unparseable
Compute `scale = displayServings / originalServings`

For each ingredient string in `recipe.additionalIngredients` and any extracted ingredient lines, parse leading quantity (regex on `^(\d+(?:\.\d+)?|\d+/\d+)\s*(\w+)?\s+`) and replace with scaled quantity formatted to fractions where appropriate (½, ¼, ⅓, ⅔, ¾)
Display unscaled original alongside scaled value in muted text when scale ≠ 1.0 ('1 cup → 0.5 cup')
'Add ingredients to grocery' swipe action and context menu in RecipesView.swift:74-100 + 95-115 must respect the scale (add scaled quantities to the grocery item)
Persist last-used scale in @AppStorage keyed by recipe.id so reopening the recipe restores the same scaling
Reset to original servings via long-press on the Stepper or a 'Reset' chip
userStories.notes: iOS: DONE 2026-04-24. Added `Utilities/RecipeScaling.swift` with `parseOriginalServings`, `scaleIngredientsText`, `scaleIngredientLine`, and `formatQuantity`. RecipeDetailView now has a dedicated Servings card (separated from the Meta Info badge row) with a Stepper(1...20), reset button when scaled, and a small '(from <N>)' hint. `additionalIngredients` is rendered scaled when scale ≠ 1.0 with the original shown in tertiary text below. `addIngredientsToGrocery` uses the scale — parsed quantities are scaled via `RecipeScaling.scaleIngredientLine` before being added. Persistence: per-recipe UserDefaults key `recipe.servings.<id>` (0 = use original) restored on onAppear. Quantity parser handles ints, decimals, ASCII fractions (1/2, 3/4), unicode fractions (½¼¾⅓⅔⅛⅜⅝⅞⅕⅖⅗⅘⅙⅚), and mixed forms (1 1/2). Formatter renders back to common unicode fractions when close (±0.03). STILL OPEN: scaling of foodIds-linked ingredients with per-ingredient quantities (requires a recipe_ingredients DB model currently absent), Reset chip separate from the reset button, more tolerant parser on pluralization.

userStories.title: iOS Grocery: autocomplete from history in AddGroceryItemView
userStories.description: As a parent who buys the same 30-50 items every week, I want autocomplete suggestions while typing an item name so I'm not retyping 'milk', 'bread', 'butter' for the hundredth time and re-picking the right category each time. AddGroceryItemView at `ios/EatPal/EatPal/Views/Grocery/GroceryView.swift:439` is currently a plain TextField with no suggestions.
acceptanceCriteria: Build an in-memory frequency index from `appState.groceryItems` (and any soft-deleted history if available) keyed by lowercased name → {count, lastCategory, lastUnit, lastPriority}
Below the 'Item name' TextField, render a horizontal scroll of the top 5 suggestions matching the current text (prefix + fuzzy)
Tap a suggestion: fills name, auto-selects the most-recent category/unit/priority for that item
If the user has typed an item that exactly matches a Food in `appState.foods`, prefer that Food's category/unit (so adding 'milk' picks the same dairy + ml the user uses elsewhere)
Suggestions hidden when name is empty; collapse to top 3 once name has 3+ chars
Same autocomplete also wired into VoiceAddGrocerySheet's edit mode and EditGroceryItemView (GroceryView.swift:531) for consistency
Performance: index built lazily on first sheet appear, debounced 100ms on text change
Telemetry: `grocery_autocomplete_used` event with rank-of-tapped-suggestion
