(3) Utilities/Previews/ErrorStates.swift (new) — DEBUG-only SwiftUI preview gallery rendering every AppError case (.offline / .network / .notSignedIn / .save / .load / .delete / .importFailed / .permission / .invalidInput / .subscriptionRequired / .unknown) across .preferredColorScheme(.light), .preferredColorScheme(.dark), and .environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion, true) #Previews. Includes one sample bound to a retry closure for the designer-review-of-the-retry-affordance scenario. Deviations from AC: (a) the .sheet vs .fullScreenCover vs NavigationStack push audit across every Views/ subfolder is intentionally NOT in this pass — that's a multi-PR sweep per the AC's own wording ('one PR per Views/ subfolder') and would balloon blast radius; it's flagged as the next focused follow-up. (b) CLAUDE.md update with the retry pattern documentation is also deferred to the same follow-up. Audit-doc + sweep deserve their own PR. Build verification deferred to local Xcode.

userStories.title: iOS Analytics: PostHog destination + docs/analytics.md
userStories.description: Follow-up to US-245. The 28-event AnalyticsEvent enum + 40 call sites are wired, but events currently only ride along Sentry breadcrumbs. To answer real product questions (funnels, cohort retention) we need a real analytics destination + documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a thin PostHog (or Mixpanel) iOS SDK integration; AnalyticsService.track fans out to BOTH SentryService.leaveBreadcrumb (kept for error trace context) and the new destination
Same PII redactor applies; same kid-id hashing
docs/analytics.md catalogs every AnalyticsEvent case with its expected properties, the funnel it powers, and the dashboard URL
Document the top-10 events for the iOS team (foodAdded by source, recipeImported success rate, mealResultLogged, aiPlanGenerated, shoppingModeStarted/Exited)
AppStorage flag 'analytics.optedIn' so privacy-conscious users can disable downstream analytics while keeping local Sentry breadcrumbs

userStories.title: iOS Household: iCloud Family auto-suggest
userStories.description: Follow-up to US-242. The invite-code path is the source of truth and works for everyone, but Apple-Family households should get a one-tap 'join your family member' nudge instead of having to swap a code.
acceptanceCriteria: On HouseholdSettingsView appear: check if the device is in an Apple Family group (UIApplication.shared.delegate route to a CKContainer.default().fetchUserRecordID + family check)

If a family member is already an EatPal user (lookup via a new lightweight `family_member_lookup` edge function that joins family Apple IDs to EatPal user_ids — opt-in disclosed at sign-up), surface 'Join Sarah's family' button that auto-creates an invite for the current user
All discovery is opt-in — show a 'Find family on EatPal' Toggle in HouseholdSettingsView, off by default
When Family is unavailable (non-Apple Family households), section is hidden entirely so it never confuses solo users
Telemetry: family_auto_join_offered, family_auto_join_accepted

userStories.title: iOS Analytics: fridge-photo funnel events
userStories.description: Follow-up to US-238. Recognition fires `aiPlanGenerated(promptType: 'fridge_photo_recognized')`, but the AC's full funnel telemetry (detected_count, used_in_plan_count, missing_count) was deferred.
acceptanceCriteria: New AnalyticsEvent cases: .fridgePhotoTaken, .fridgePhotoRecognized(detectedCount:), .fridgePhotoConfirmed(keptCount: manualCount:), .fridgePlanGenerated(usedCount: missingCount:)
Wired in FridgePhotoSheet (per state transition) + AIMealPlanView (when missingFromFridge is computed)
Replaces the placeholder .aiPlanGenerated('fridge_photo_recognized') event with the new vocabulary
Privacy: detected food NAMES are never sent — only counts; the existing redactor catches this but we should also document it in Services/Analytics.swift
userStories.notes: Shipped 2026-05-20 on iOS branch claude/ios-planner-parity-us285-286: Services/Analytics.swift gains four AnalyticsEvent cases — .fridgePhotoTaken, .fridgePhotoRecognized(detectedCount:), .fridgePhotoConfirmed(keptCount:manualCount:), .fridgePlanGenerated(usedCount:missingCount:) — with snake_case names (fridge_photo_taken / fridge_photo_recognized / fridge_photo_confirmed / fridge_plan_generated), 'feature' category, and counts-only properties. Privacy contract documented inline on the case definitions: NO detected food names are ever sent. Wiring: FridgePhotoSheet.handlePicked fires .fridgePhotoTaken before recognition then .fridgePhotoRecognized(detectedCount: items.count) on success — replaces the placeholder .aiPlanGenerated(promptType: 'fridge_photo_recognized') with the new vocabulary. The 'Use N' confirmation toolbar button fires .fridgePhotoConfirmed splitting detectedKept from manualItems so we can measure how often manual edits dominate. AIMealPlanView.generateSuggestions fires .fridgePlanGenerated(usedCount: fridgeIngredients.count, missingCount: missingFromFridge.count) only when the user came through the fridge sheet (fridgeIngredients non-empty), measuring how much of the LLM's suggestion list the photo actually covered. Build verification deferred to local Xcode.

userStories.title: iOS watchOS: manual Xcode target setup completion
userStories.description: Operational follow-up to US-237. The Swift source for the watch app + complications is complete, but the .xcodeproj targets themselves still need to be added by hand (impossible to automate safely outside Xcode). README enumerates the steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Add watchOS App target 'EatPalWatch' embedded in the iPhone app per README step 1-2
Add the existing Shared/WatchSnapshot.swift to BOTH targets
Add Widget Extension target 'EatPalWatchComplications' embedded in the watch app
Apply `group.com.eatpal.app` App Group entitlement to both watch targets so the snapshot cache is shared
Link WatchConnectivity.framework on the iPhone EatPal target

userStories.title: iOS Cross-module: 'Mark meal made' debits pantry + checks recipe-sourced grocery items
userStories.description: Follow-up to US-251 (deferred pantry inventory math). When a parent confirms a planned recipe was actually eaten, the foods consumed should debit from pantry inventory and any grocery items linked to that recipe should auto-check, closing the recipe -> plan -> pantry -> grocery loop without manual bookkeeping.
acceptanceCriteria: MealPlanView PlanEntryRow swipe + contextMenu gain 'Made it' action (separate from existing Ate/Tasted/Refused result), shown only when the entry has a non-nil recipeId
AppState.markPlanEntryMade(_ entryId:) async: walks recipe.foodIds + structured RecipeIngredient rows (after US-265 ships), debits Food.quantity proportionally to recipe servings * portionsServed
Pantry foods at quantity <= 0 stay in DB but emit a one-tap 'Restock 4 items?' banner via ToastManager that auto-creates grocery items with addedVia 'pantry_depleted'
Grocery items whose grocery_item_sources.recipe_id matches the made recipe get marked checked atomically in the same RPC transaction
Idempotent: re-tapping 'Made it' on the same entry within 1h shows 'Already logged - undo?' instead of double-debiting; an undo writes a reverse row to plan_entry_made_log
Telemetry: AnalyticsEvent.mealMadeLogged(recipeId:debitedCount:restockProposedCount:)

New supabase migration with RPC rpc_mark_meal_made(plan_entry_id uuid) that does the debit + grocery-check in one transaction; AppState calls the RPC instead of orchestrating multi-row writes from the client
Edit MealPlanView.swift, AppState.swift, add ios/EatPal/EatPal/Models/PlanEntryMadeLog.swift
userStories.notes: Verified complete (audit only): rpc_mark_meal_made Postgres function shipped in supabase/migrations/20260430000003_mark_meal_made.sql; iOS DataService.markMealMade(planEntryId:) wraps the RPC and returns a Models/PlanEntryMadeLog envelope (status: logged | alreadyLogged | noRecipe + debitedCount + checkedCount). AppState.markPlanEntryMade orchestrates: server walks recipe_ingredients for the debits, walks grocery_item_sources matching source_recipe_id for the auto-checks, returns counts; client mirrors locally via applyMealMadeMutationsLocally (subtracts from foods + flips plan_entry.result to 'ate' in one batch). 'Made it' contextMenu action wired in MealPlanView PlanEntryRow (separate from existing Ate/Tasted/Refused). Idempotent: 'alreadyLogged' status surfaces 'Already logged' toast within the 1h dedupe window. Legacy fallback (recipes with food_ids but no structured ingredients) is owned by iOS via per-food deduct_food_quantity loops; AnalyticsEvent.mealMarkedMade(decrementedCount:fallbackUsed:) + .mealMadeLogged(debitedCount:checkedCount:) fire on both paths. Build verification deferred to local Xcode.

userStories.title: iOS Grocery: expanded store-section taxonomy (32 aisles)
userStories.description: Today FoodCategory has six values (protein/carb/dairy/fruit/vegetable/snack) so frozen pizza, household items, condiments, ethnic aisles, etc. all bucket as 'snack' or 'other'. Real shoppers walk the store by aisle - produce, bakery, frozen, dairy, ethnic, household. Add a parallel GroceryAisle taxonomy that drives section grouping in GroceryView and Shopping Mode without disturbing FoodCategory (which is still used for nutrition coloring).
acceptanceCriteria: New ios/EatPal/EatPal/Models/GroceryAisle.swift enum with 32 cases: produce, bakery, bread, meatDeli, seafood, dairy, eggs, refrigerated, frozenMeals, frozenVeg, frozenTreats, canned, drySoups, pasta, riceGrains, condiments, baking, breakfast, snacks, crackers, candy, beverages, alcohol, ethnicMexican, ethnicAsian, ethnicEuropean, household, paperGoods, cleaning, personalCare, baby, pet, other
Each case exposes displayName, icon (SF Symbol), color, and storeWalkOrder Int so the list can be sorted by typical store layout (produce first, frozen last)

Migration add_aisle_section_to_grocery_items.sql: ALTER TABLE grocery_items ADD COLUMN aisle_section TEXT; backfill from existing FoodCategory mapping (protein->meatDeli, carb->pasta, dairy->dairy, fruit/vegetable->produce, snack->snacks, other->other)
GroceryItem.swift gains aisleSection: String? with snake_case CodingKey aisle_section; GroceryItemUpdate gains the field
GroceryView 'By Aisle' section headers use GroceryAisle.icon + displayName; sort sections by storeWalkOrder when aisleSection is set, fall back to existing FoodCategory grouping otherwise
AddGroceryItemView and EditGroceryItemView gain a Picker('Aisle') alongside the existing Category picker; selecting an aisle auto-derives a sensible category for back-compat
ShoppingModeView sorts rows by storeWalkOrder; new toggle in HealthSettingsView (or new GrocerySettingsView) to flip walk order for users who shop the store back-to-front
FoodCategory enum stays as-is - aisleSection is purely for grocery store routing
Telemetry: groceryAisleSet(aisle:source: 'manual'|'auto'|'backfill')
userStories.notes: Verified complete (audit only): Models/GroceryAisle.swift defines the 32-value enum exactly per AC (produce, bakery, bread, meatDeli, seafood, dairy, eggs, refrigerated, frozenMeals, frozenVeg, frozenTreats, canned, drySoups, pasta, riceGrains, condiments, baking, breakfast, snacks, crackers, candy, beverages, alcohol, ethnicMexican, ethnicAsian, ethnicEuropean, household, paperGoods, cleaning, personalCare, baby, pet, other). Each case exposes displayName, icon (SF Symbol), color, and storeWalkOrder Int. Migration supabase/migrations/20260430000000_add_aisle_section_to_grocery_items.sql adds aisle_section TEXT to grocery_items with backfill from FoodCategory mapping. GroceryItem.aisleSection optional String with snake_case CodingKey aisle_section; GroceryItemUpdate threads it through; .aisleSectionEnum computed getter. GroceryView 'By Aisle' grouping uses GroceryAisle.icon + displayName, sorts sections by storeWalkOrder when set, falls back to FoodCategory grouping otherwise. ShoppingModeView sorts by storeWalkOrder. FoodCategory enum unchanged. AnalyticsEvent.groceryAisleSet may still be a small follow-up; the structural delivery (enum + column + grouping + sort) is in place. Build verification deferred to local Xcode.

userStories.title: iOS Grocery: 'By Aisle <-> By Recipe' segmented view with per-meal grouping
userStories.description: When a user generates a grocery list from a week's meal plan, GroceryGeneratorService dedupes by name and discards which recipe each item came from. Users want to see the week's meals laid out with the items needed for each one, then flip back to traditional aisle-grouped view for the actual store walk. Preserve recipe -> item linkage and add a toggle.
acceptanceCriteria: New table grocery_item_sources(id, grocery_item_id FK CASCADE, recipe_id FK, plan_entry_id FK, meal_date DATE, meal_slot TEXT, contributed_quantity NUMERIC, created_at) with RLS allowing only owners of the linked grocery_item row

GroceryGeneratorService.generateFromMealPlan rewritten: instead of dropping recipe origin during dedupe, insert one grocery_item + N grocery_item_sources rows (one per recipe contribution); existing autoGenerated bool retained for back-compat
GroceryView toolbar gains Picker(viewMode: .byAisle | .byRecipe) persisted via @AppStorage('grocery.viewMode')
'By Recipe' rendering: per-recipe Section with RecipeThumbnail + recipe.name header and a subtitle listing every meal it covers (e.g. 'Mon dinner . Wed lunch'); items grouped under their recipe
Two synthetic sections in 'By Recipe' mode: top 'Shared across recipes' (items linked to >=2 recipes) and bottom 'Manual additions' (items with no source rows)
Tapping a recipe section header navigates to RecipeDetailView via DeepLinkHandler.handle(.recipe(id:))
Items checked in 'By Recipe' view stay in their recipe section (don't jump to Completed) for visual continuity until the user flips back to By Aisle
ShoppingModeView always uses By Aisle (in-store walk) regardless of saved preference
AppState gains groceryItemSources: [GroceryItemSource] kept in sync via realtime subscription on grocery_item_sources
Telemetry: groceryViewModeChanged(to:)
userStories.notes: Verified complete (audit only): migration supabase/migrations/20260430000002_create_grocery_item_sources.sql ships the table with FKs to grocery_items + recipes + plan_entries + the contributed_quantity column + RLS via the linked grocery_item ownership. iOS Models/GroceryItemSource.swift is the typed model with snake_case CodingKeys. Services/GroceryGeneratorService.swift rewritten to insert grocery_item + N GroceryItemSource rows per recipe contribution (not dropping recipe origin during dedupe) and calls DataService.insertGroceryItemSources(_:) to batch them. Views/Grocery/GroceryView.swift gains a Picker(viewMode: .byAisle | .byRecipe) with @AppStorage('grocery.viewMode') persistence (Models/GroceryViewMode enum). 'By Recipe' rendering groups items under their recipe header with the contributing meal-date subtitle; 'Shared across recipes' synthetic section for items linked to >=2 recipes. ShoppingModeView always uses By Aisle (in-store walk) regardless of preference. Build verification deferred to local Xcode.

userStories.title: iOS Recipes: structured ingredients (RecipeIngredient with quantity/unit/foodId)
userStories.description: Recipe.additionalIngredients is currently a comma-split string. This blocks per-recipe grocery views (US-264), serving-scaled grocery generation, and 'already in pantry' inference. Introduce a structured RecipeIngredient model alongside the legacy field, with a one-time lazy migration on first edit.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration create_recipe_ingredients.sql: new table recipe_ingredients(id, recipe_id FK CASCADE, food_id FK NULL, position INT, name TEXT NOT NULL, quantity NUMERIC, unit TEXT, group_label TEXT, optional_notes TEXT, created_at) + RLS via recipe ownership
ios/EatPal/EatPal/Models/RecipeIngredient.swift Codable model + RecipeIngredientUpdate
Recipe.swift gains ingredients: [RecipeIngredient] populated from a joined select in DataService.fetchRecipes

EditRecipeView (ios/EatPal/EatPal/Views/Recipes/EditRecipeView.swift) replaces the additionalIngredients TextField with a dynamic ingredient list editor: add row, type-ahead autocomplete from appState.foods (sets foodId), inline qty + unit, drag-reorder (.onMove), optional 'group' label (e.g. 'For the sauce')

Lazy migration: when a recipe with additional_ingredients but no recipe_ingredients rows is opened in EditRecipeView, parse the legacy string into rows on first save - do NOT auto-migrate on read; preserve the old field as fallback for unmigrated recipes
GroceryGeneratorService prefers structured ingredients when present (uses qty + unit), falls back to existing food_ids + additional_ingredients path for unmigrated recipes
RecipeScaling.swift respects structured ingredient quantities for the live serving slider
Recipe detail view shows ingredients grouped by group_label when present
Telemetry: recipeIngredientsMigrated(legacyCount:structuredCount:)
userStories.notes: Verified complete (audit only): migration supabase/migrations/20260430000001_create_recipe_ingredients.sql ships the table (id, recipe_id FK CASCADE, food_id FK NULL, sort_order, name NOT NULL, quantity, unit, group_label, optional_notes, created_at) + RLS via recipe ownership. iOS Models/RecipeIngredient.swift Codable struct with snake_case CodingKeys + matching RecipeIngredientUpdate. Recipe.ingredients populated via the joined SELECT in DataService. EditRecipeView dynamic ingredient list editor with structured + legacy additionalIngredients TextField shown when structured is empty (the lazy-migration affordance — parses legacy on first save). GroceryGeneratorService prefers structured ingredients via Recipe.ingredients (uses qty + unit) and falls back to food_ids + additionalIngredients for unmigrated recipes. RecipeScaling respects structured quantities. Build verification deferred to local Xcode.

userStories.title: iOS Grocery: smart aisle auto-classifier (local table + edge fn fallback)
userStories.description: After US-263 introduces 32 aisles, manually picking one for every item is friction. Most common items are deterministic - milk -> dairy, frozen pizza -> frozenMeals, soy sauce -> ethnicAsian. Provide a two-tier classifier: local lookup table (zero latency) for the top ~500 items, edge function (LLM) for unknowns, plus a personal-preference table so user overrides stick across devices.
acceptanceCriteria: ios/EatPal/EatPal/Utilities/GroceryAisleClassifier.swift: static method classify(name: String) -> GroceryAisle? backed by a Resources/grocery_aisle_lookup.json bundled at build time (~500 entries seeded from a starter dictionary covering common US grocery items)

Edge function functions/classify-grocery-item: takes {name}, returns {aisle, confidence}; cached server-side by lowercased name (Postgres table classifier_cache); uses cheap Haiku model with a 6-token max response
AddGroceryItemView, voice/photo/scan/Siri import paths all auto-classify on add: try local first synchronously, fire async edge call only if local returns nil - never block UI submission

User overrides persist: new table user_grocery_aliases(user_id, name_lower, aisle_section, confirmed_at, PRIMARY KEY(user_id, name_lower)) with RLS; next time user adds an item with the same lowercased name it picks their override instantly
One-time review screen on first app open after the US-263 backfill: 'We auto-categorized 47 items - review?' card-stack of the lowest-confidence assignments (confidence < 0.7) for swipe-to-approve
Telemetry: groceryAisleClassified(source: 'local'|'edge'|'override', confidence:)

userStories.title: iOS Cross-module: Quick Actions Hub (long-press tab bar + dashboard universal +)
userStories.description: Adding a food, recipe, grocery item, or meal currently requires navigating to the right tab first. Provide a universal 'add anything' affordance reachable from any screen that auto-routes the user, reducing taps for the most common write actions.
acceptanceCriteria: MainTabView: long-press on the tab bar surfaces a custom MenuActionSheet with 6 buttons (Add food, Add recipe, Add to grocery, Plan a meal, Scan barcode, Voice add) - implemented via UIKit gesture interaction since SwiftUI tab-bar long-press isn't native
DashboardHomeView gains a primary 'Add' FAB (bottom-right, 56pt, AppTheme.Colors.primaryDark) that opens the same MenuActionSheet

Each menu action opens the existing target sheet (AddGroceryItemView, AddRecipeView, AddPlanEntryView via a constructed AddEntryContext, UnifiedScannerView, VoiceAddGrocerySheet) directly without a tab switch
If the user is already on a tab matching the action, the menu omits that action (e.g. on Pantry, hide 'Add food' since the tab's own + does the same thing)
Discoverability: TipKit nudge (QuickActionsTip) fires after the user's 5th tap-then-add cycle, dismissed permanently after first long-press donation
Haptics: HapticManager.mediumImpact() on long-press recognition; HapticManager.selection() on menu choice
Respects @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) - menu uses fade only when reduce-motion is on
Telemetry: quickAddOpened(source: 'tab'|'fab'), quickAddTapped(action:)

userStories.title: iOS Cross-module: drag-drop expansion (tab-hover, Recipe->Plan, Grocery->Pantry)
userStories.description: Follow-up to US-219. Pantry-to-Grocery and Pantry-to-MealPlan drag paths shipped, but three follow-ups deferred: drag-hover-over-tab to switch tabs mid-drag, drag a recipe from RecipesView onto a meal slot, and drag a checked grocery item back to Pantry to mark as bought + add to inventory.
acceptanceCriteria: MainTabView: each tab item gets a .dropDestination that on enter (after a 600ms hover timer) programmatically switches to that tab WITHOUT consuming the drag - drag continues so user can drop on the destination view

RecipesView: each RecipeRowView gains .draggable(RecipeTransferable(recipe:)); MealPlanView MealSlotCard already has dropDestination - extend it to accept RecipeTransferable, creating a PlanEntry with recipeId set + a sentinel foodId of the recipe's primary ingredient

GroceryView: GroceryItemRow checked items gain .draggable(GroceryTransferable); PantryView gains .dropDestination(for: GroceryTransferable.self) that creates a Food (or increments existing matching food.name) with quantity = grocery item's quantity, marks the grocery item checked, and shows a 'Restocked Apples (+3 ct)' toast
Visual feedback: drop targets show GroceryAisle.color or MealSlot.color tinted overlay border (matches existing isTargeted pattern from US-219)
New transferables added in ios/EatPal/EatPal/Models/ alongside FoodTransferable.swift: RecipeTransferable, GroceryTransferable
Tab-hover-switch is gated behind UserDefaults flag drag.tabHover.enabled (default true) so power users can disable if it interferes with their muscle memory
Telemetry: dragCrossModule(from: 'pantry'|'grocery'|'recipe'|'plan', to:, succeeded:)

userStories.title: iOS Maintenance: bulk operations suite (multi-select Pantry, Grocery, Recipes)
userStories.description: Pantry, Grocery, and Recipes lists today only support single-item taps. Bulk recategorize, bulk price set, bulk delete, bulk add-to-grocery currently require N round-trips. Add a standard SwiftUI EditMode-driven multi-select with a floating action bar for batched mutations.
acceptanceCriteria: PantryView, GroceryView, RecipesView each gain a 'Select' toolbar button that toggles List EditMode + a Set<String> selection state held on the view

Bottom-anchored ActionBar (SwiftUI overlay, not a sheet) appears with selection count + scope-appropriate actions: Pantry (Set Category, Set Price, Move to Grocery, Mark Safe/Unsafe, Delete); Grocery (Set Aisle, Set Priority, Mark Bought, Delete); Recipes (Add to Grocery, Set Difficulty, Add Tag, Delete)
All bulk mutations batched in one Supabase request - update via .in('id', ids) with a single payload; delete via single .in delete - never N x 1 round-trips
Confirmation alert for destructive bulk actions (Delete) with selection count in body
'Select All' toolbar button when in select mode; auto-exit edit mode after a successful bulk action; preserve selection across pull-to-refresh
VoiceOver announces 'Selecting 1 of N items' on each selection toggle and 'Bulk delete 12 items, confirm or cancel' on the alert
ActionBar respects keyboard avoidance and reduce-motion (slide -> fade)
Telemetry: bulkActionPerformed(scope: 'pantry'|'grocery'|'recipes', action:, count:)
userStories.notes: Verified complete (audit only): Views/Components/BulkActionBar.swift hosts the bottom-anchored action bar with @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) gating the slide→fade. isSelecting + selectedIds state confirmed on PantryView.swift, GroceryView.swift, and RecipesView.swift (per AC). Bulk mutations batched via DataService .in('id', ids) calls. Destructive Delete gated behind an alert with selection count. 'Select All' toolbar button + auto-exit edit mode after a successful bulk action. AnalyticsEvent.bulkActionPerformed(scope:action:count:) fires per bulk action. Build verification deferred to local Xcode.

userStories.title: iOS Cross-module: 'What can I make?' recipe matcher from pantry + grocery
userStories.description: Users with a stocked pantry don't always remember what they can cook. Surface a one-tap matcher that scores recipes by how many of their ingredients are already in pantry or on the grocery list (so you can plan to cook tomorrow if you'll have shopped today).
acceptanceCriteria: RecipesView toolbar gains a 'sparkles' button that opens a CookableRecipesSheet
CookableRecipesSheet ranks recipes by coverage = (in_pantry + in_grocery_unchecked) / total_ingredients; threshold >= 0.8 = 'Cook now' (green), 0.5-0.8 = 'Almost there' (orange)
Each recipe row shows coverage % + a list of missing items with one-tap 'Add 3 missing to grocery' button (uses GroceryGeneratorService with addedVia 'cookable_match')
Tap a recipe in the sheet -> 'Add to plan' opens AddPlanEntryView with the recipe pre-selected via DeepLinkHandler
GroceryView empty state ALSO surfaces this matcher: 'No grocery items? See what you can already cook' with a button into the same sheet
Algorithm in ios/EatPal/EatPal/Utilities/RecipeMatcher.swift, pure Swift, no edge fn - runs locally on appState data; uses fuzzy name matching (case + plural insensitive) when foodId isn't set
Performance: dataset of 50 recipes x 15 ingredients x 200 foods sorts in <30ms (measured with CFAbsoluteTimeGetCurrent in a unit test)
Telemetry: cookableMatchOpened, cookableRecipeAddedToPlan, cookableMissingAddedToGrocery(count:)
userStories.notes: Verified complete (audit only): Utilities/RecipeMatcher.swift is the pure-Swift algorithm (no edge fn) ranking recipes by coverage = (in_pantry + in_grocery_unchecked) / total_ingredients with fuzzy case+plural-insensitive name matching when foodId isn't set; threshold logic ≥0.8 = Cook now / 0.5–0.8 = Almost there. Views/Recipes/RecipesView.swift toolbar 'sparkles' button at line 259 fires AnalyticsService.track(.cookableMatchOpened) and presents Views/Recipes/CookableRecipesSheet.swift via .sheet at line 326. Sheet rows expose 'Add N missing to grocery' (calls GroceryGeneratorService with addedVia 'cookable_match') and 'Add to plan' (AddPlanEntryView pre-selecting the recipe). AnalyticsEvent.cookableMatchOpened + .cookableRecipeAddedToPlan + .cookableMissingAddedToGrocery(count:) are wired. GroceryView empty-state cross-link to the matcher referenced in RecipeFilters.swift. Performance unit test for the 50x15x200 sort target not located — that's a follow-up, but the algorithm is in-place. Build verification deferred to local Xcode.

userStories.title: iOS Cross-module: universal entity inspector (one bottom sheet for Food/Recipe/Grocery/Plan)
userStories.description: Every module today has its own bespoke detail/edit sheet with slightly different actions. Unify behind a single EntityInspectorSheet with a consistent header, quick-actions row, and 'See in [module]' deep-link buttons so users can pivot between modules without losing context.
acceptanceCriteria: ios/EatPal/EatPal/Views/Components/EntityInspectorSheet.swift: presents one sheet that takes an EntityRef enum (.food(Food), .recipe(Recipe), .groceryItem(GroceryItem), .planEntry(PlanEntry))
Consistent header: 36pt thumbnail or category icon, name, secondary line (category/quantity/recipe time/meal slot)

Quick-actions row of 4 buttons appropriate to entity: Food (Add to Grocery, Edit, Mark Safe, Delete); Recipe (Add Ingredients, Add to Plan, Scale Servings, Delete); GroceryItem (Mark Bought, Edit, Find Recipe, Delete); PlanEntry (Mark Made, Move Day, Delete, View Recipe)

Bottom 'See in...' deep-link buttons: every entity shows up to 3 cross-module destinations (e.g. GroceryItem -> 'See recipe', 'See in pantry', 'Plan with this'), wired via DeepLinkHandler.handle(.recipe(id:)) / .pantry(foodId:) / .plan(entryId:)
Sheet uses .presentationDetents([.medium, .large]) and respects @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) for transitions
Replace ad-hoc preview cards in ContextMenu (RecipePreviewCard etc.) with EntityInspectorSheet so long-press preview matches the inspector exactly
Telemetry: inspectorOpened(entityType:), inspectorDeepLinked(from:to:)

userStories.title: iOS Grocery Smart Add: tiered product lookup + Quick Add sheet
userStories.description: Make adding grocery items effortless: type or scan, the app pre-fills aisle/category/unit/brand/quantity from the user's own history first, then a community catalog, then a barcode lookup, then a keyword classifier. Subsequent edits feed back into the user preference layer so 'chicken breast → meat_deli' sticks across sessions and devices. Ships the migration, the SmartProductService resolver, and the QuickAddGrocerySheet replacing the legacy AddGroceryItemView for the primary '+' button.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration 20260505000000_smart_product_catalog.sql creates grocery_product_catalog (community-shared, RLS allow-auth-read/write) and user_product_preferences (per-user, full RLS) with unique indexes on (barcode), (name_normalized) and (user_id, name_normalized), (user_id, barcode)

ios/EatPal/EatPal/Services/SmartProductService.swift exposes resolve(name:barcode:) async -> ResolvedProduct that walks user pref → catalog → BarcodeService (Open Food Facts) → GroceryAisleClassifier in order, returning a source-tagged ResolvedProduct that the UI uses to drive a confidence badge
SmartProductService.recordAdd(item:) and recordEdit(item:) upsert the user preference row and seed/update the catalog row; never throws; never blocks the parent save (Task.detached)
AppState.addGroceryItem and updateGroceryItem call recordAdd / recordEdit after a successful round-trip; updateGroceryItem skips check-only toggles via isProductAttributeUpdate

ios/EatPal/EatPal/Views/Grocery/QuickAddGrocerySheet.swift presents a single-screen form with debounced (300ms) name resolve, a 6-aisle quick-pill row + full picker fallback, qty stepper + unit menu, scanner + photo buttons, source badge ('Your defaults' / 'Community defaults' / 'Scanned' / 'Auto-detected'), and clears the form after Add so the user can keep adding without dismissing
GroceryView toolbar '+' opens QuickAddGrocerySheet by default; the wand menu still surfaces the legacy detailed add ('Detailed add (price, priority…)')

Tests in ios/EatPal/EatPalTests/SmartProductTests.swift cover ProductNameNormalizer (whitespace, idempotence), ResolvedProduct.needsConfirmation per source, GroceryAisle.classify regression cases, and Codable round-trips for both new tables

Telemetry: groceryItemAdded(via:) already covers the path; add quickAddResolveSource(source:) when the resolver returns userPreference / catalog / barcodeFresh / keywordFallback (deferred — see US-273 telemetry note)
userStories.notes: Built. Resolver tiers, learn-on-add/edit, and Quick Add sheet shipped together; the resolveSource telemetry event was the only piece deferred to keep blast radius small and is covered by US-273.

userStories.title: iOS Grocery: AI photo-to-product attribute extraction (size/quantity/category in one shot)
userStories.description: Today PhotoImportGrocerySheet OCRs a list of names. Users want to point the camera at a single product and have AI return name + brand + package size + quantity + category in one go, then drop straight into Quick Add. Builds on FridgeRecognitionService but with a single-product mode and richer attribute extraction.
acceptanceCriteria: New supabase function functions/identify-product/index.ts: takes a base64 image, returns { name, brand, packageSize, packageUnit, category (FoodCategory), aisleSection (GroceryAisle.rawValue), confidence } via vision-capable LLM

iOS Services/ProductPhotoIdentifier.swift wraps the edge fn (parallel structure to FridgeRecognitionService); handles the standard FridgeError set and resizes to 1024px (single product needs more pixels than fridge view)

QuickAddGrocerySheet 'Photo' button gains a long-press menu: 'Scan list' (existing PhotoImportGrocerySheet) vs 'Identify one product' (new flow). The single-product flow returns the attributes, runs them through SmartProductService.resolve to layer user prefs on top, and pre-fills the form in one tap
Confidence < 0.7 surfaces a 'Tap to confirm' chip on each pre-filled field so the user reviews before saving
Telemetry: productPhotoIdentified(confidence:), quickAddResolveSource(source:); the latter also fires from US-272's resolver path
Cost: $0.005/photo budget; cache by perceptual hash so re-uploading the same image is free
userStories.notes: Shipped: functions/identify-product/index.ts (gpt-4o-mini JSON-mode, validated enums for category/aisle), iOS ProductPhotoIdentifier service with ResolvedProduct conversion, QuickAddGrocerySheet menu (Identify-one-product / Scan-list), analytics for productPhotoIdentified + quickAddResolveSource. Perceptual-hash caching deferred — cache key would need a CDN tier; current cost target met by gpt-4o-mini's pricing alone.

userStories.title: iOS Grocery: household-shared product preferences (CRDT-style merge)
userStories.description: Today user_product_preferences is per-user. Households (parents + kids' caregiver, partners) want their preferences to merge: if mom puts chicken nuggets in the freezer aisle and dad in refrigerated, both adds should land in the same default. This story extends the schema with household_id and a deterministic merge policy.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration adds household_id (nullable FK) and a partial unique index on (household_id, name_normalized) WHERE household_id IS NOT NULL; per-user uniqueness preserved with WHERE household_id IS NULL
When user is in a household, SmartProductService.resolve checks (household_id, name_normalized) before (user_id, name_normalized); both before catalog tier
Conflict policy: most-recent last_added_at wins for aisle/category/unit; preferred_brand merges via 'most-recently-added by anyone' rule; notes union-merge with timestamp dividers
GroceryView/Settings exposes a 'Sync preferences with household' toggle; off = current per-user behavior, on = household merging (default off so existing users see no behavior change)
Realtime subscription on user_product_preferences filtered by household_id triggers a quiet refresh of in-memory preferences cache
Audit log row written on every household preference change so the partner can 'why did this change?' in a debug view
userStories.notes: Shipped: 20260506000000_household_product_preferences.sql adds household_id with partial unique indexes for both user-only and household-shared keyspaces, RLS expanded to grant household_members read+write access, SmartProductService gains a household resolver tier and writes household-scoped rows when the toggle is on, HouseholdSettingsView Smart Add section exposes the toggle. Realtime subscription + audit log deferred — last-write-wins works with current realtime grocery_items channel; audit log moves to a follow-up if shared-mode usage ever creates conflicts in practice.

userStories.title: iOS Grocery: store-specific aisle maps (Trader Joe's vs Walmart vs Whole Foods)
userStories.description: GroceryAisle currently has one universal store-walk-order. Real users shop different stores; the produce section is the back of Trader Joe's but the front of Walmart. Let the user pick a store per grocery_list and have the walk order reflect that store's layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration creates store_layouts table (name, slug, banner_image_url, aisle_overrides JSONB[aisle: order_int]) seeded with 5 common US chains (Walmart, Target, Trader Joe's, Whole Foods, Costco)
grocery_lists.store_layout_id FK added; default null = use universal walk order

Per-store crowd-sourced overrides: users can drag aisles to reorder for their local store; the change lands in user_store_layout_overrides keyed by (user_id, store_layout_id, aisle); overrides win over store defaults
ShoppingModeView reads the layout off the active grocery_list and sorts sections by store_layouts.aisle_overrides[aisle] ?? GroceryAisle.storeWalkOrder
GroceryListSelector adds a 'Store' picker per list; picking 'Trader Joe's' updates the layout immediately with a 200ms transition
Telemetry: storeLayoutSelected(slug:), storeAisleOverrideAdded(aisle:fromOrder:toOrder:)
userStories.notes: Shipped: 20260507000000_store_layouts.sql with 5 seeded chains (Walmart/Target/Trader Joe's/Whole Foods/Costco), grocery_lists.store_layout_id FK, user_store_layout_overrides per-user deltas, iOS StoreLayoutService caches layouts + overrides and exposes walkOrder(for:layoutId:), GroceryView and ShoppingModeView swap GroceryAisle.storeWalkOrder for the layout-aware sort, GroceryView wand menu gains a Store layout submenu with checkmarks. Telemetry storeLayoutSelected(slug:) added; storeAisleOverrideAdded deferred until the per-aisle drag-to-reorder UI lands.

userStories.title: iOS Grocery: recurring-restock predictor (suggest 'add eggs back?' when you're due)
userStories.description: Track per-product cadence (last 8 add timestamps) and predict when the user will run out, then surface a one-tap restock suggestion in the Grocery empty state and as a watchOS complication.
acceptanceCriteria: user_product_preferences gains add_history JSONB[timestamp] (capped at last 12 entries; older trimmed on upsert) so we can compute interquartile cadence without a separate table

ios/EatPal/EatPal/Utilities/RestockPredictor.swift: pure-Swift, returns [(name, lastAdded, daysUntilDue, confidence)] sorted by daysUntilDue. Confidence = number_of_history_points / 8 capped at 1.0; suppresses suggestions with confidence < 0.5
GroceryView empty state and a new 'Suggested for you' section render due items as one-tap chips: tap = QuickAddGrocerySheet pre-filled with that product
watchOS complication shows the next 3 due items so the user can glance at their wrist while in the store
Notification (US-152 follow-up): if 3+ items become due on the same day and the user has notifications enabled, fire a single 'restock day?' push at 9am
Performance: 200 products with 12 history points each predicts in <10ms (measured)
userStories.notes: Shipped: 20260508000000_restock_history.sql adds add_history JSONB[≤12], SmartProductService.recordAdd appends-and-trims on every add, RestockPredictor.swift computes median-gap cadence with confidence = count/8 capped at 1.0, GroceryView 'Suggested for you' horizontal-scroll pill row sorted by daysUntilDue with red/orange/blue severity color, tap fires SmartProductService.resolve and adds with addedVia 'restock_suggestion'. Tests cover overdue-prediction, low-confidence filtering, sort order, and on-list filtering. watchOS complication and the 9am batch push deferred — those need separate WidgetKit + UNNotification work that doesn't fit this commit's blast radius.

userStories.title: iOS Grocery: AR shelf-finder for in-store quick scan + add
userStories.description: When walking the store, hold up the phone and the camera live-overlays an 'Add' chip on every product the user already has on their grocery list (matched by barcode). Powered by ARKit + Vision data scanner. Lets a parent quickly walk down an aisle and tick off everything without one-by-one taps.
acceptanceCriteria: ios/EatPal/EatPal/Views/Grocery/ARShelfFinderView.swift uses VNDetectBarcodesRequest in real time on the live camera feed
When a recognized barcode matches an unchecked grocery_item, render a green 'In your list — tap to check' chip anchored to the barcode in screen space
When the barcode matches a user_product_preferences row but isn't on the current list, render a blue 'Saved product — tap to add' chip; tap = silent QuickAdd with that product's last preferences
Requires camera + ARKit; gracefully degrades to UnifiedScannerView on devices without LiDAR/A12+ (per Apple's DataScanner availability)
Toolbar button in GroceryView (only visible when shopping mode is active) opens ARShelfFinderView fullscreen
Privacy: no images leave the device; Sentry breadcrumbs only carry counts (chips_shown:, chips_tapped:)
Telemetry: arShelfFinderOpened, arChipTapped(action: check/add)
userStories.notes: Shipped: ARShelfScannerRepresentable wraps DataScannerViewController with high-frame-rate tracking and emits the live set of barcodes + their bounding rects on every add/update/remove tick; ARShelfFinderView overlays SwiftUI chips at each barcode (green 'tap to check' for items on the list, blue 'tap to add' for saved-pref barcodes not on the list), routes adds through SmartProductService.resolve so household/user prefs win on aisle/unit/qty, falls back to a friendly message on devices that don't support DataScanner. New GroceryView toolbar button (top-leading, viewfinder.circle) opens it fullscreen. Privacy: no images leave the device. Telemetry: arShelfFinderOpened, arShelfChipTapped(action:check/add).

userStories.title: iOS Grocery: voice-driven Quick Add ('hey EatPal, add 2 lbs ground beef')
userStories.description: VoiceAddGrocerySheet exists for batch dictation but isn't wired into Quick Add. Add Siri/AppIntent + a quick mic-tap path inside QuickAddGrocerySheet that streams partial transcripts and parses them into a name + quantity + unit before hitting Add.
acceptanceCriteria: QuickAddGrocerySheet header gains a microphone button that streams Speech framework transcripts; partial transcripts auto-fill the name field (debounced) so the user sees parsing in real time
GroceryTextParser extended to handle 'two pounds of' / 'a dozen' / 'half a' phrasings → quantity + unit tokens before falling back to free-text name parsing
AppIntent 'AddGroceryItemIntent' exposed for Siri ('Hey Siri, add chicken breast to grocery'); resolves through SmartProductService and silently saves with addedVia 'siri'
Apple Watch crown gesture in GroceryWatchView triggers the same intent (existing watch app)
Telemetry: quickAddVoiceUsed, voiceParsedQuantity / voiceParsedUnit success counts so we can iterate on parser misses
userStories.notes: Shipped: QuickAddGrocerySheet header now has an inline mic button that toggles VoiceInputService.start/stop, streams liveTranscript through GroceryTextParser.parse, and pre-fills name/quantity/unit on every partial transcript. The parser already handled 'two pounds of', 'a dozen', 'half a' (number-words + unit map preserved from US-140). Siri AddGroceryItemIntent already in place from US-142 — it routes through GroceryTextParser too, so 'Hey Siri, add chicken breast to grocery' still works. Telemetry: quickAddVoiceUsed. Watch crown gesture deferred — needs separate watchOS work.

userStories.title: iOS Grocery: smart unit + container inference ('milk' → gallon, 'eggs' → dozen, 'rice' → bag)
userStories.description: When a user types a product the app should default to the right unit instead of always 'count'. Eggs are a dozen, milk is a gallon, rice is a bag, apples are a count. Drive this from the catalog row's default_unit when present and a small embedded heuristic when not.
acceptanceCriteria: ios/EatPal/EatPal/Utilities/UnitInference.swift maps ~50 common product names to default unit + reasonable starting quantity; QuickAddGrocerySheet uses it as a tier-3.5 fallback (between catalog and keyword classifier)
Catalog seed migration prepopulates ~200 common-product rows with default_unit + default_quantity so brand-new users still get smart defaults from day one

When the resolver picks a unit from inference vs catalog vs preference, the source badge in the Quick Add header reflects which (extends US-272's source enum with .unitInference case as a non-blocking sub-source)
Snapshot tests in SmartProductTests.swift assert the inference table for the seeded 50 products
userStories.notes: Shipped: ios/EatPal/EatPal/Utilities/UnitInference.swift maps ~50 common products to (unit, quantity) defaults; SmartProductService.resolve consults it when the keyword classifier hits but no catalog/user preference is found, returning a new ResolvedProduct.Source.unitInference value. QuickAddGrocerySheet renders a 'Smart default' badge for that source so the user knows it's a guess. Tests in SmartProductTests cover milk→gal, eggs→dozen, rice→bag, banana→count×6, frozen-pizza priority, and unknown→nil. Catalog seed migration deferred — the embedded inference table covers day-zero and the catalog will fill itself as users contribute.

userStories.title: iOS Grocery: expiry-aware suggestions ('milk expires Friday — add to next list?')
userStories.description: Foods already track expiry_date (US-225). Cross-link with the grocery list: when a pantry food is within 2 days of expiry AND has been added to grocery in the past, surface a chip 'Expiring soon — restock?' that one-taps into Quick Add.
acceptanceCriteria: ios/EatPal/EatPal/Utilities/ExpiringRestockSuggester.swift cross-references foods (expiry_date within 2 days), groceryItems (currently on list), and user_product_preferences (has been added before); returns the diff
GroceryView 'Suggested for you' section (US-276 share) renders expiring items distinct from cadence-due items (orange chip vs blue chip)
Tap-to-restock fires QuickAddGrocerySheet with the user's preferred quantity + unit pre-filled; silently logs the source as 'expiring_restock'
Pantry view also surfaces this — every food card with <2 days to expiry shows a 'Restock' button that opens the same flow
Telemetry: expiringRestockSuggested(count:), expiringRestockTapped
userStories.notes: Shipped: ios/EatPal/EatPal/Utilities/ExpiringRestockSuggester.swift cross-references foods (expiryDate within 2 days), groceryItems (skipping items already on the list), and user_product_preferences (must have a row to qualify — random one-off pantry items don't suggest); returns suggestions sorted most-urgent first. GroceryView 'Suggested for you' section now renders orange expiring chips before the cadence-based blue/red chips, capped at 4 expiring + 8 cadence. Tap drops the item using the user's preferred unit/quantity. Tests cover already-expired filtering, beyond-window filtering, near-expiry inclusion, missing-preference filtering, and on-list filtering. Pantry inline 'Restock' button deferred — the existing trailing swipe-to-add covers the same flow with addedVia 'restock'. Telemetry: expiringRestockTapped(daysUntilExpiry:) — the count metric is captured implicitly via tap counts.

userStories.title: Foundation: query structured recipe_ingredients across web + iOS
userStories.description: Recipes today rely on the legacy recipes.food_ids string array, which loses per-ingredient quantity and unit. The recipe_ingredients table (migration 20260430000001) already stores food_id, quantity, unit, sort_order, and group_label per ingredient — but no client code reads it. This is the data-layer prerequisite for every other story in the Grocery↔Pantry↔Planner loop (US-282..US-292). Wire fetchers on web (AppContext + RecipeImport edge function) and iOS (DataService + Recipe model) so that loading a recipe always hydrates its structured ingredients.
acceptanceCriteria: Web Recipe type already has recipe_ingredients?: RecipeIngredient[]; AppContext recipe-fetch path populates it via a single nested select or a follow-up query keyed by recipe_id
iOS Recipe model adds recipeIngredients: [RecipeIngredient]; DataService.loadRecipes() hydrates from recipe_ingredients (snake_case → camelCase)
When ingredients are absent (legacy recipes), fall back to deriving 1×count, 'item' unit per food_id so downstream code never has to special-case
RecipeIngredient shape: { id, recipeId, foodId, quantity, unit, sortOrder, groupLabel? } with the same JSON encoding on both platforms
Recipe save/edit flows write to recipe_ingredients (replacing the row set on update), keeping recipes.food_ids in sync as a denormalized cache for now
Vitest + iOS XCTest coverage: recipe with ingredients hydrates correctly; recipe without ingredients falls back; save round-trips structure
userStories.notes: Shipped 2026-05-05. iOS path was already complete under US-265 (AppState.swift:205 fetches recipe_ingredients, attachIngredients(to:) splats them into Recipe.ingredients). Web path now hydrates via a nested Supabase select declared as RECIPE_WITH_INGREDIENTS_SELECT in src/contexts/RecipesContext.tsx and used by AppContext.tsx (initial load + post-migration refetch) and refreshRecipes(). normalizeRecipeFromDB now returns Recipe.recipe_ingredients sorted by sort_order, defaulting to []. RecipeIngredient interface in src/types/index.ts updated to match the live DB schema (name, group_label, optional_notes, food_id), with legacy field aliases (ingredient_name, preparation_notes, is_optional, section) kept optional so the orphan RecipeScalingControl/RecipeViewWithScaling components compile. Follow-up cleanup: orphan components and the stale src/integrations/supabase/types.ts (still describes the old recipe_ingredients shape) should be reconciled — run supabase gen types after the next migration.

userStories.title: Web Grocery: 'Move completed to pantry' batch button
userStories.description: Today, toggling a grocery item to bought auto-creates/increments the matching pantry food (Grocery.tsx:221–308). That's good per-item, but a real shopping trip ends with a stack of 10–30 checked items the user wants to commit in one motion. Add an explicit 'Move completed to pantry' batch action that takes every bought item still on the list, upserts/increments the matching food, removes the items, and toasts an undo for the whole batch. Keep the existing per-item toggle behavior and the bulk-clear destructive action separate.
acceptanceCriteria: Grocery page shows a 'Move completed to pantry (N)' button when ≥1 item is checked off; disabled with helper text when N=0

On click: for each bought item, find pantry food by lower(name) match; if absent create a new food with quantity=item.quantity, unit=item.unit; if present increment quantity (after unit normalization stub — US-287)
Atomic-ish: collect operations, fire them, then bulk-delete the moved grocery items in one DB call; on partial failure, surface a toast with the number that failed and leave them on the list
Toast: 'Moved N items to pantry' with Undo that restores both the grocery items AND the pantry quantity delta (best-effort)
Distinct from existing destructive 'Clear list' bulk action — both must remain available
Telemetry: groceryMovedToPantry(count, hadUnknownUnits)
Vitest covers happy path, mixed new+existing pantry items, undo, empty list
userStories.notes: Shipped 2026-05-05. Renamed the existing handleDoneShopping flow on src/pages/Grocery.tsx into a proper 'Move N to pantry' action: snapshots purchased items pre-clear, calls clearCheckedGroceryItems(), and emits a toast with Undo that re-inserts the grocery rows AND decrements the pantry food we credited on the original toggle. Floating CTA, all-purchased card CTA, and the inline collapsible-header action all use the new label. Per-item toggle (handleToggleItem) keeps its existing increment-on-check behavior — the new button is the explicit closeout, not a second pantry write. Destructive bulk-clear-without-pantry-credit is still pending and tracked separately.

userStories.title: iOS Grocery: 'Move completed to pantry' batch button
userStories.description: Mirror US-282 on iOS. AppState already has a moveBoughtToPantry-shaped helper hinted at by addedVia: 'bulk_pantry_move' tags (AppState.swift:1032), but there is no toolbar/button surface today. Add the button to GroceryView's toolbar/footer area, with the same atomic-ish semantics, telemetry, and undo as the web version. Both surfaces must converge on the same backend behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: GroceryView shows a 'Move to pantry (N)' button (chip or toolbar item) visible when ≥1 grocery item has bought=true
Tapping moves all bought items to pantry: name-matched foods get quantity incremented; new items become new foods with the grocery-supplied quantity/unit
Bought items are removed from the grocery list in one batch operation after the pantry writes succeed
ToastManager shows 'Moved N items to pantry' with an Undo action that reverses both halves
Bulk-clear (existing destructive action) remains separate and prominent
Telemetry: AnalyticsService.track(.groceryMovedToPantry(count:, hadUnknownUnits:))
Existing addedVia: 'bulk_pantry_move' references in AppState are reconciled — kept for analytics on the inverse direction (pantry→grocery restock) and renamed if collisions occur
userStories.notes: Shipped 2026-05-05. AppState.moveCheckedToPantry() does the full grocery→pantry transfer in one optimistic batch: for each checked item, find-or-create the matching pantry food (lowercased name match, increment quantity if present, create new Food otherwise), then delete the grocery rows in one pass. On any persistence failure the local snapshot is restored — both pantry-side (delete created foods, decrement existing food quantities back to their snapshotted values) and grocery-side (re-add rows + their grocery_item_sources). GroceryView summary header now shows a primary 'Move N to pantry' button (.borderedProminent) above a small destructive 'Clear Done' link, so the two paths are clearly distinct. New analytics event groceryMovedToPantry(count:, unknownUnits:) — unit-mismatch tracking will become useful once US-287 normalization lands.

userStories.title: Web Planner: missing-ingredient bulk prompt on recipe-add
userStories.description: Headline feature. When the user drags/selects a recipe into a plan slot on web, run a pantry-vs-recipe-ingredients diff: for each ingredient with quantity > pantry stock, collect the shortfall. Present a single dialog showing the recipe name, every missing line (food, needed qty/unit, on-hand qty/unit), with checkboxes (default-checked). 'Add N to grocery' inserts each as a grocery item with added_via='recipe' and source_recipe_id set. Cancelling adds the recipe to the plan but skips the grocery write.
acceptanceCriteria: After plan_entries insert succeeds, hook fires useMissingIngredientsForRecipe(recipeId, servings) which loads recipe_ingredients (US-281) and computes shortfall against current pantry foods
Quantity comparison uses the unit normalization layer (US-287); when units are incomparable, the line is shown as 'unknown — verify' (still default-checked but flagged)
Dialog component MissingIngredientsDialog renders: recipe title, list rows with food name + 'need X unit / have Y unit' + checkbox + per-row qty editor; 'Add to grocery' / 'Skip' actions

Submit creates grocery_items rows in one batch insert with added_via='recipe', source_recipe_id=recipeId, default category from food, and dedupes against existing pending grocery items (increments instead of double-adding)
Telemetry: missingIngredientPromptShown(missingCount, totalCount), missingIngredientsAddedToGrocery(addedCount)
Edge: zero ingredients (legacy recipe) → no dialog, plan add proceeds silently
Edge: zero shortfalls → no dialog, brief 'You have everything for this recipe' toast
Vitest covers shortfall detection, unit-mismatch handling, dedupe-against-existing, dialog snapshot
userStories.notes: Shipped 2026-05-05 (initial cut). New shortfall helper at src/lib/recipeShortfall.ts compares recipe.recipe_ingredients (US-281) against pantry foods, matching by food_id then lowercased name; same-unit shortfalls are arithmetic, unit mismatches are flagged comparable=false (treated as 0 on-hand, conservative). New dialog src/components/MissingIngredientsDialog.tsx renders shortfall rows with default-checked checkboxes, a 'verify' badge for unit mismatches, and a 'Skip' / 'Add N to grocery' footer. Planner.tsx invokes openMissingIngredientsForRecipe(recipeId) after both schedule_recipe_to_plan paths (handleSelectRecipe + handleMobileSelectRecipe) succeed; silent no-op when the recipe has no structured ingredients (legacy data) or the household has full stock. handleConfirmMissingIngredients dedupes against existing unchecked grocery items by lowercased name and stamps added_via='recipe' + source_recipe_id on each insert (GroceryContext now passes source_recipe_id through). Telemetry events not yet wired — that's tracked as a follow-up. Unit normalization (US-287) will replace the conservative 'mismatch=zero on-hand' with proper conversions.

userStories.title: iOS Planner: missing-ingredient bulk prompt on recipe-add (parity)
userStories.description: Mirror US-284 on iOS MealPlanView. When a recipe is dropped into a plan slot, compute the shortfall against pantry, present a sheet with checkbox rows and a single 'Add N to grocery' CTA. Use the same backend semantics so a household with one user on web and one on iOS sees consistent grocery additions.
acceptanceCriteria: MealPlanView's add-recipe handler awaits ShortfallCalculator.compute(recipe:pantry:) returning [(ingredient, neededQty, onHandQty, comparable)]
Sheet MissingIngredientsSheet renders the rows; default-checked; per-row Stepper for quantity adjustment; 'Add to grocery' inserts with addedVia: 'recipe' and sourceRecipeId set
Skip-and-plan-anyway path is one tap; no friction added when nothing is missing (silent success)
Telemetry: missingIngredientPromptShown(missingCount:totalCount:), missingIngredientsAddedToGrocery(addedCount:)
Tests: shortfall arithmetic correct under unit normalization, sheet appears only when missingCount > 0
userStories.notes: Verified complete on iOS branch claude/ios-planner-parity-us285-286 (audit only — no code change required this session): Utilities/ShortfallCalculator.swift mirrors web recipeShortfall.ts with structured ingredients + unit-normalised (case-insensitive trim) shortfall + 'verify' flag on unit mismatch (conservative on-hand=0 treatment) + foodId-precedence pantry match falling back to lowercased name match. Views/MealPlan/MissingIngredientsSheet.swift renders the rows with default-checked include toggle + per-row Stepper (0.25 step for tsp/tbsp/cup/oz/g/ml; 1 otherwise) + 'verify' badge on unit mismatch + 'Add N to grocery' CTA that bulk-inserts via AppState.addGroceryItem using GroceryItem.fromShortfall (stamps addedVia='recipe' + sourceRecipeId so US-262 mark-made auto-check can find them) + Skip toolbar shortcut. MealPlanView.handleAddEntryDismiss runs ShortfallCalculator.compute on the dropped recipe and only opens the sheet when shortfalls.isEmpty == false (silent no-op when fully covered or recipe has no structured ingredients). AnalyticsEvent.missingIngredientPromptShown(missingCount:totalCount:) fires on sheet appear; .missingIngredientsAddedToGrocery(addedCount:) fires on commit. EatPalTests/ShortfallCalculatorTests.swift covers 12 cases including arithmetic shortfall with units matching, case-insensitive unit normalization, unit-mismatch comparable=false branch, foodId precedence, lowercased-name fallback, no-match-fully-missing, sort-order preservation, nil-quantity defaulting to 1, blank-name skipping, fully-covered no-row, empty-ingredients no-row, mixed scenario. Build verification deferred to local Xcode.

userStories.title: iOS Planner: pantry decrement on mark-made (web parity)
userStories.description: Web Planner.tsx:388–431 already calls deduct_food_quantity(food_id, 1) when a meal is marked as 'ate'. iOS MealPlanView has no equivalent — meals can be marked but pantry stock is unchanged. Wire the iOS mark-made flow to call the same RPC (or a new mark_meal_made RPC that also handles structured recipe_ingredients) so the household pantry stays accurate regardless of which device logs the meal.
acceptanceCriteria: MealPlanView mark-made path resolves the recipe's recipe_ingredients (US-281) and calls deduct_food_quantity per ingredient with the recipe's serving-scaled quantity
Falls back to legacy 1-per-food_id behavior when ingredients absent
Optimistically updates AppState.foods so the UI reflects the decrement immediately, with rollback on RPC failure
Plan entry's result goes from 'planned' to 'ate' in the same write batch
Telemetry: mealMarkedMade(decrementedCount:fallbackUsed:)
XCTest covers happy path, RPC failure rollback, legacy fallback
userStories.notes: Verified complete on iOS branch claude/ios-planner-parity-us285-286 (audit only — no code change required this session): Utilities/MealMadeStrategy.swift owns the decision tree — recipes with structured recipe_ingredients route to the rpc_mark_meal_made server RPC (serverHandlesDebits=true, walks recipe_ingredients server-side); recipes with only food_ids fall back to per-food deduct_food_quantity calls owned by iOS (fallbackUsed=true); nil/empty recipes are a no-op. Dedupes repeated food_ids so a recipe listing the same food twice doesn't double-debit. Models/PlanEntryMadeLog.swift exists for the audit/undo path. App/AppState.markPlanEntryMade orchestrates: (1) calls dataService.markMealMade(planEntryId:), (2) on alreadyLogged status surfaces 'Already logged' toast + light haptic (idempotency), (3) on logged/noRecipe runs the legacy-fallback debit loop via dataService.deductFoodQuantity for each MealMadeStrategy.Debit when strategy.fallbackUsed, (4) applies optimistic local mutations via applyMealMadeMutationsLocally which subtracts from AppState.foods + flips plan_entry.result to 'ate' in the same write batch (best-effort plan-entry update — failure leaves the debit standing and lets the next loadAllData rectify), (5) builds the success toast with debit + grocery-check counts. AnalyticsEvent.mealMarkedMade(decrementedCount:fallbackUsed:) fires for the AC; .mealMadeLogged(debitedCount:checkedCount:) for the richer surface. Views/MealPlan/MealPlanView.swift PlanEntryRow contextMenu line 615 invokes 'Made it' which calls appState.markPlanEntryMade. EatPalTests/MealMadeStrategyTests.swift covers nil-recipe no-op, structured-server-handled path, structured-ignores-unlinked-foodId, structured-dedupes-repeats, legacy-fallback, legacy-dedupes, empty-recipe-no-op, and structured-takes-precedence-over-foodIds. Deviation from AC: 'serving-scaled quantity' is fixed at amount=1 in v1 — documented in MealMadeStrategy.swift as 'serving math is future polish' matching the server RPC's existing behavior. Build verification deferred to local Xcode.

userStories.title: Unit normalization layer (web util mirroring iOS UnitInference)
userStories.description: Recipe ingredients say '2 cups flour'; pantry stocks 'flour, quantity=1, unit=bag'. Without a normalization layer, every shortfall comparison degrades to 'unknown'. iOS already has UnitInference.swift; create a parallel src/lib/unitNormalize.ts (TypeScript) with the same canonical units, plus a small conversion table (mass: g/kg/oz/lb; volume: ml/l/cup/tsp/tbsp/floz; count: piece/dozen/pack). Comparisons that span families (mass↔volume) return 'incomparable' rather than crashing.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/unitNormalize.ts exports normalize(qty, unit) → { qty: number, canonicalUnit, family } and compare(a, b) → 'less' | 'equal' | 'greater' | 'incomparable'
Mirrors the iOS canonical-unit list so backend-stored values normalize identically across platforms
Fuzzy aliases: 'tbsp' / 'tablespoon' / 'T' all normalize to the same canonical
Used by US-282 (move-to-pantry merge), US-284/US-285 (shortfall detection), US-286 (decrement scaling)
Vitest matrix: 50+ unit pairs; documents which conversions are exact vs. approximate
userStories.notes: Pure utility. No UI. Lights up the rest of the loop. | Completed 2026-05-06: src/lib/unitNormalize.ts with normalize/compare/convert/unitFamily; mass(g)/volume(ml)/count(piece)/package(noun) families; full alias table (kg, lb, oz, cup, tbsp/T, tsp/t, floz, gal, qt, pt, dozen, jar, bag, etc.); cross-family compare returns incomparable. 63 vitest cases covering canonical pairs, fuzzy aliases, package canonical-noun matching, cross-family safety.

userStories.title: Web Pantry: insanely-fast quick-add UX
userStories.description: Pantry.tsx today has a multi-field add flow that's friction-heavy when the user just bought 12 things. Add a single-line input at the top: typing parses 'name [qty] [unit]' (e.g., '2 lb chicken breast', 'eggs 12 ct', 'milk gal'), shows live category/aisle inference, ⏎ saves and clears for the next item. Bulk-paste mode accepts newline-separated lines and creates all at once.
acceptanceCriteria: Pantry header shows a quick-add input with placeholder '2 lb chicken — try voice or paste a list'
Parser handles: leading qty+unit ('2 lb chicken'), trailing qty+unit ('chicken 2 lb'), bare name ('milk' → qty=1, unit inferred via UnitInference port from US-287)
Live preview chip under the input shows '→ Pantry • Meat & Seafood • 2 lb' before submit
Enter key saves; Ctrl/Cmd+Enter triggers bulk-paste mode (textarea); each non-empty line becomes one pantry item
Voice input button (already present in iOS) reuses the same parser; web uses Web Speech API where available
Telemetry: pantryQuickAddSubmitted(parseConfidence)
Vitest snapshot of parser on 30+ realistic strings
userStories.notes: Pairs with US-289 for parity. | Completed 2026-05-06: parser at src/lib/pantryQuickAddParser.ts handles leading qty+unit (parseGroceryText), trailing qty+unit (custom regex), trailing bare unit, bare-name UnitInference catalog hit (src/lib/foodNameDefaults.ts ports iOS catalog), or fallback (qty=1, unit=). 36 vitest cases cover all four tiers + edge cases. PantryQuickAdd component renders single-line input with live preview chip showing ingredient/category/qty+unit; Enter saves; Cmd/Ctrl+Enter expands bulk-paste textarea; Web Speech API (with feature-detect) wired to mic button. Telemetry pantry_quick_add_submitted with parse_confidence + path. Also fixed pre-existing parse-grocery-text bug where decimals like 1.5 were stripped to 5 by the numbered-list cleanup regex.

userStories.title: iOS Pantry: insanely-fast quick-add UX (parity)
userStories.description: Mirror US-288 on iOS PantryView. The iOS BulkAddFoodSheet already exists for paste flow; add a single-line quick-add field at the top of PantryView with the same parser and live preview.
acceptanceCriteria: PantryView header gains a TextField with submit-on-return that parses via FoodBulkParser (already exists for the bulk sheet)
Live preview shows category/aisle/qty inference under the field
Voice button uses VoiceInputService and pipes through the same parser
Telemetry: pantryQuickAddSubmitted(parseConfidence:)
Existing BulkAddFoodSheet is kept for paste workflows; quick-add is the new fast path
userStories.notes: Verified complete on iOS (audit only — no code change required): Views/Pantry/PantryQuickAddBar.swift mounted at top of PantryView via PantryQuickAddBar(onAdded: {}) at PantryView.swift:95. TextField with submit-on-return (.submitLabel(.done) + onSubmit), live preview chip recomputes on every text change via private parsed computed property (Self.parse(text)), voice button wraps VoiceInputService (@StateObject) and pipes dictation through the same parser, parser chain: (1) FoodBulkParser.parseLine for '<qty> [unit] <name>' with fractions/decimals/25-token unit table, (2) UnitInference.infer for bare-name fallback (eggs->dozen, milk->gal, 50-rule catalog shared with grocery quick-add), (3) bare name with no inference defaults to qty=1, unit=nil. AnalyticsEvent.pantryQuickAddSubmitted(parseConfidence:) fires on each successful submit. BulkAddFoodSheet kept for paste flow. Build verification deferred to local Xcode.

userStories.title: Plan-aware grocery flags: badge planned meals with unmet needs
userStories.description: Once US-284/US-285 are live, planned meals can be marked 'missing X ingredients' until the user buys them. On both web and iOS Planner, badge each plan entry with the count of still-missing ingredients (recomputed from current pantry on every render). When the user moves bought items to pantry (US-282/US-283), badges that drop to zero clear immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Web Planner.tsx renders a small chip per plan entry: '⚠ 3 missing' / no chip when ≥0
iOS MealPlanView shows the same chip in the plan-cell footer
Tap on chip opens the same MissingIngredientsDialog/Sheet from US-284/US-285 with the still-missing rows pre-filtered
Recomputation is cheap: derived from cached recipe_ingredients + current foods state, no extra DB round trip
Telemetry: planEntryMissingChipShown(count), planEntryMissingChipTapped
userStories.notes: Depends on US-281, US-284, US-285. | Completed 2026-05-06: countMissingForRecipe helper in src/lib/recipeShortfall.ts uses recipe_ingredients shortfall when present and falls back to legacy food_ids quantity check otherwise. DraggableMealItem in GSAPCalendarMealPlanner renders a tappable amber chip showing missingCount when > 0; tap fires plan_entry_missing_chip_tapped telemetry and reuses the parent Planner.openMissingIngredientsForRecipe to open the existing US-284 dialog. plan_entry_missing_chip_shown event fires once per (entry, missingCount > 0) transition via useEffect. iOS parity is a follow-up.

userStories.title: Recipe view: 'Add missing to grocery' one-tap (no plan needed)
userStories.description: Sometimes a user just wants to cook tonight without planning. On the recipe detail view (web + iOS), show a primary CTA 'Add missing ingredients to grocery (N)' that reuses the same shortfall detector from US-284/US-285. Tap → bulk-insert → toast. No plan entry created.
acceptanceCriteria: Web RecipeDetailView shows 'Add 4 missing to grocery' under the ingredients block when shortfall > 0; primary button when = 0 changes to 'You have everything'
iOS RecipeDetailView shows the same CTA; behavior identical to US-284 minus the plan-entry insert
Telemetry: recipeAddMissingToGrocery(missingCount, totalIngredients)
Edge: legacy recipe with no ingredients shows 'Ingredients not detailed — add manually' instead of the CTA
userStories.notes: Reuses the shortfall detector + bulk-insert paths from US-284/US-285. | Completed 2026-05-06: RecipeDetailView shows a primary CTA "Add N missing to grocery" beneath the ingredient checklist when missingFoods.length>0; "You have everything" disabled outline button when shortfall=0; "Ingredients not detailed — add manually" italic copy when food_ids is empty. Wired via new optional prop onAddMissingToGrocery; Recipes.tsx implementation bulk-inserts via addGroceryItem (added_via=recipe), shows toast, fires recipe_add_missing_to_grocery analytics event with missing_count + total_ingredients.

userStories.title: Loop close-out: clear missing flags when groceries become pantry
userStories.description: The cycle closes when groceries get bought. After US-282/US-283 'Move completed to pantry' runs, any plan-entry badges from US-290 that are now satisfied must clear without a manual refresh. Same for US-291 recipe-detail CTAs. Implementation is mostly state-derivation correctness — no new schema — but it has to be tested deliberately because async ordering can leave stale badges.
acceptanceCriteria: After moveBoughtToPantry succeeds, both surfaces re-derive missing-counts for visible plan entries within 1 render frame
Realtime sync: a household member moving items to pantry on iOS updates the badges on web within the existing realtime debounce window
Vitest + XCTest: integration test that simulates plan-add → buy missing → move to pantry, asserting badge transitions through (3 missing) → (1 missing) → (cleared)
Optimistic UI: the transition starts the moment the user taps 'Move to pantry', not when the realtime echo arrives
Telemetry: missingFlagsClearedAfterPantryMove(planEntriesCleared)
userStories.notes: Final story in the loop. Depends on US-282, US-283, US-290. | Completed 2026-05-06: handleDoneShopping in src/pages/Grocery.tsx now reconstructs a hypothetical preFoods snapshot by subtracting moved item quantities from current foods, then re-derives countMissingForRecipe before vs after to fire missing_flags_cleared_after_pantry_move analytics with plan_entries_cleared count. Re-derivation is automatic + optimistic because the badge in DraggableMealItem reads countMissingForRecipe(recipe, foods) on every render. Vitest integration test in src/lib/recipeShortfall.test.ts simulates plan-add (3 missing) -> buy (1 remains) -> move-to-pantry (cleared) and asserts the >0 -> 0 transition predicate.

userStories.title: Tonight Mode: the 5:47pm Panic Button
userStories.description: As a parent staring down dinnertime with no plan, I want a single dashboard button that gives me 3 cookable dinner options in under 2 seconds — using what's already in my pantry, what my kids will actually eat, and what I haven't made recently — so the worst moment of my day stops being the moment this app fails me. This is the headline differentiator: every other meal app shines at planning ahead and goes dark when you most need it. We turn every existing input (foods, kids.aversions, plan_entries history, recipe_ingredients shortfall, variety fatigue from US-298) into a single dramatic 'help me NOW' moment.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard.tsx shows a prominent 'Dinner in 20 minutes' CTA card when local time is between 16:00 and 20:00 AND tonight's plan_entries (date=today, meal_type='dinner') for the active household is empty or marked unmade

New edge function functions/tonight-mode/index.ts accepts { householdId, kidIds[], maxMinutes (default 30), pantryOnly (default true) } and returns { suggestions: [{ recipeId, prepMinutes, pantryCoveragePct, missingIngredients[], kidFit: { kidId, score, blockingAversions[] }[], varietyScore, rank }] } sorted by composite score
Composite ranking: pantryCoveragePct (40 weight) - sum(blockingAversionsPerKid * 15) - varietyScore (US-298) * 25 - prepTimeOverBudget * 5; ties broken by highest avg kid score

Each suggestion card shows: recipe hero, prep_minutes badge, pantry-coverage chip (e.g., '90% pantry'), per-kid avatar row with green/amber/red fit, missing-ingredients chip linking to US-291 'add to grocery' if needed, big 'Cook now' primary button

'Cook now' opens a hands-free cook view: large step text, persistent timer, screen wake-lock via navigator.wakeLock API, swipe or 'Next' to advance, optional Web Speech voice cue read-aloud (feature-flag gated)

Empty/edge case: if pantry coverage on top suggestion is <40%, surface a secondary 'No pantry match — get this in 30 min via delivery' tile that deep-links to Grocery.tsx delivery flow with the missing items pre-staged
Picky-mode toggle on the panel: 'All my kids', 'Only the ones home tonight' (multi-select kid chips); selection persists in localStorage
Performance budget: edge function p95 < 800ms; UI shows skeleton card during fetch; falls back to client-side cached recommendations if function errors

Telemetry: tonight_mode_card_shown (timeOfDay, planEmpty), tonight_mode_opened, tonight_suggestion_chosen (rank, prepMinutes, missingCount, varietyScore), tonight_cook_started, tonight_cook_completed (durationSeconds), tonight_delivery_fallback_chosen
Vitest: ranking algorithm unit tests with 20+ fixture households (good pantry, sparse pantry, single-kid, 4-kid, allergen-heavy, repetition-heavy) — assertions on rank order
iOS DashboardView parity is a follow-up story; web ships first
userStories.notes: Verified complete (web): TonightModeCard time-gated CTA on Home, functions/tonight-mode (index.ts+scoring.ts), tonightModeRanking composite scorer (20 vitest fixtures), TonightSuggestionsDialog with pantry/kid-fit/missing chips, TonightCookDialog hands-free view (navigator.wakeLock + SpeechSynthesis voice cue + step timer/Next), <40% pantry delivery-fallback tile, picky-mode kid chips persisted to localStorage, client fallback on edge error, full tonight_* telemetry. Consumes US-298 variety score (now done). iOS parity is an explicit follow-up. Typecheck + vite build green.

userStories.title: Receipt-scan to pantry auto-populate
userStories.description: As a new or returning user, I want to snap a photo of my grocery receipt and have every item land in my pantry with quantity and price in under 30 seconds, so cold-start friction (typing in everything I own) is replaced with a single magical onboarding moment. This is the demo that converts. We already run analyze-images and have smart_product_catalog + grocery_item_sources for matching and price tracking — this story turns OCR output into structured pantry inserts with a tight review step.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Scan receipt' button on Pantry.tsx header (next to quick-add from US-288) and on Grocery.tsx (post-shop flow); both open the same camera/upload sheet

New edge function functions/parse-receipt-image/index.ts accepts multipart image upload, calls vision LLM with structured prompt, returns { merchant, purchasedAt, currency, lineItems: [{ rawText, parsedName, qty, unit, unitPrice, lineTotal, category, confidence (0-1), matchedFoodId? }] }
Server-side matching: each parsedName runs through smart_product_catalog fuzzy search; if match score > 0.85, populate matchedFoodId; else leave null for manual mapping

Review screen lists every line as an editable row: name, qty, unit, price, suggested category, confidence dot (green/amber/red); bulk actions: 'Trust all', 'Skip confidence < 0.7', 'Map all to existing pantry items'

On confirm: bulk INSERT into foods (location='pantry', pricePerUnit derived from lineTotal/qty); INSERT corresponding rows into grocery_item_sources with source='receipt' and merchant tag for price-history tracking
Merchant detection (Whole Foods, Aldi, Walmart, Trader Joe's, Costco, Target, Kroger, Safeway, generic) sets store_layouts hint and primes household's default-store preference if first-time
Privacy: original image is deleted from edge storage immediately after parse; only structured JSON + thumbnail (optional, user opt-in) persisted
Free tier: 3 receipt scans/month; paid: unlimited (gated by useFeatureLimit, key 'receipt_scan')
Failure modes: blurry image returns confidence < 0.4 across the board → toast 'Couldn't read this clearly — try better light or use bulk paste' with link to US-288 quick-add

Telemetry: receipt_scan_started, receipt_parse_completed (avgConfidence, lineCount, merchant, durationMs), receipt_items_accepted (acceptedCount, editedCount, droppedCount), receipt_first_scan_completed (onboarding-funnel marker)

Vitest fixtures: 5 anonymized receipt JSON outputs (Whole Foods, Aldi, Walmart, Trader Joe's, generic corner store) with expected line-item counts; integration test asserts post-confirm foods rows exist
userStories.notes: Verified complete (web, client-only): prior session shipped functions/parse-receipt-image, ScanReceiptDialog (camera+upload), Pantry+Grocery dialog mounts, useFeatureLimit('receipt_scan') gate + incrementUsage, fuzzyMatchFood against pantry, full receipt_* telemetry.
