This session: extracted helpers to src/lib/receiptParse.ts (categoryFromString, fuzzyMatchFood, parseResponseToReviewRows, acceptedRowsToFoods, averageConfidence) and added 5 anonymized fixtures in src/lib/receiptParseFixtures.ts (Whole Foods 8 lines, Aldi 6, Walmart 10, Trader Joe's 7, corner store 4). 30 vitest cases lock line counts, uid uniqueness, confidence-gated auto-accept, post-confirm Food shape (is_safe=true, FoodCategory enum collapse), and pantry fuzzy match linkage. ScanReceiptDialog now imports the lib so the dialog and tests use the same code paths. Deviations from AC kept honest: (a) grocery_item_sources INSERT on confirm still deferred — table not in typed types.ts; (b) server-side smart_product_catalog fuzzy match is client-side against appState.foods (functional, not the AC's server path). Both flagged as follow-ups when types regen. Typecheck + vite build + 30/30 receiptParse tests + 853 total tests green.

userStories.title: Sibling Constraint Solver: family meal finder
userStories.description: As a parent of multiple kids with conflicting tastes and restrictions, I want to select all my kids and instantly see which recipes will satisfy every one of them, with a clear visual of why each meal works (or what one kid would have to swap), so I stop solving the dinner Venn diagram in my head every night. This is the feature that positions EatPal as the family meal app, not an individual tracker — no competitor treats multi-kid as a first-class concept.
acceptanceCriteria: New page src/pages/FamilyFinder.tsx (route /family-finder) AND a 'Find a meal everyone will eat' affordance on Recipes.tsx and Kids.tsx
Top of page: multi-select kid chip bar (default = all kids in active household); shows live count 'Showing 47 recipes that work for 3 kids'
Constraint inputs per kid: kid.aversions[], kid.allergens[] (hard block), plan_entry_feedback rows where kid_rating <= 2 (soft block), kid.dietaryTags[]

Per-recipe scoring: for each selected kid, compute kidScore = +1 per safe known-good food, -100 per allergen hit (eliminates), -3 per aversion hit, -5 per soft-block hit; recipe rank = min(kidScore across selected kids) then sum as tiebreaker
Recipe card additions: per-kid badge row at the bottom (kid avatar + green check / amber warn / red ban); hover/tap reveals 'Mia is OK; Jake would need to skip onions; Ava: contains gluten'
Constraint relaxation panel: sliders/toggles for 'Allow N aversions per kid', 'Allow ingredient swaps (links to US-297)', 'Hide soft-blocks'; counts update live
Empty state: '0 perfect matches across 4 kids — here are 3 with one swap each' auto-suggests US-297 hidden-veggies rewrite or US-291 missing-ingredients add
Save-as-favorite: 'Family wins' collection auto-curated from recipes with min(kidScore) > 0 across all household kids
Telemetry: family_finder_opened (kidCount), family_finder_constraint_relaxed (whichConstraint), family_finder_recipe_selected (minKidScore, kidCount), family_finder_zero_results_shown
Performance: client-side filtering (<200ms for 500 recipes); ingredient lookups memoized via useMemo on foods array
iOS parity follow-up; web first
userStories.notes: Completed: prior session built solver+page+chips+result cards+route+Recipes/Kids affordances (25 solver tests). This session added the missing AC surface — constraint relaxation panel (allow-N-aversions Slider + allow-swaps + hide-soft-blocks Switches with family_finder_constraint_relaxed telemetry), live 'Showing N recipes that work for X kids' count (memoized re-solve on kid selection), auto-curated 'Family wins' section (full-match w/ minKidScore>0, persisted to localStorage), one-swap fallback empty state, and family_finder_recipe_selected (minKidScore,kidCount) telemetry. Extracted pure applyRelaxation/familyWins/minKidScore into siblingMealFinder.ts with 7 new vitest. Deviation: implemented as SiblingMealFinder at /dashboard/sibling-meal-finder (prior session naming) rather than FamilyFinder /family-finder; functionally equivalent. Typecheck + vite build green.

userStories.title: Picky-Eater Win Network: anonymized food-chain success aggregation
userStories.description: As a parent of a picky eater, I want to see what worked for thousands of other kids with similar aversions and ages — not generic tips — so I have evidence-based food chains to try, ranked by real success rates. This is our deepest moat: the food_chaining + plan_entry_feedback data we collect is unique to EatPal and impossible for general meal apps to replicate. We expose it as community wisdom while keeping every household private.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration supabase/migrations/<ts>_aggregate_food_chains.sql creates view aggregate_food_chains_anon(target_food_id, aversion_tags TEXT[], age_bucket TEXT, chain_steps JSONB, success_rate NUMERIC, sample_size INT, last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ) with k-anonymity guard: only rows where sample_size >= 20 are exposed

New edge function functions/aggregate-food-chains/index.ts (daily cron via pg_cron or scheduled invoke) reads food_chaining + plan_entry_feedback (kid_rating >= 4 within 30 days of chain_completed_at = success), strips kid_id/household_id/user_id, buckets ages (0-2, 3-5, 6-8, 9-12, 13+), aggregates to the view

FoodChainingRecommendations.tsx gains a 'Community wins' tab beside existing 'Personal' tab; renders top 5 chains for { active kid's age bucket, kid's strongest aversion tags } with a 'X of Y kids succeeded (Z%)' badge

Each community-chain card: target food image, 'Step 1 → Step 2 → ... → Step N' breadcrumb of food thumbnails, success-rate bar, sample-size footnote, primary 'Try this chain for [kid]' CTA → seeds a personal food_chaining row owned by the household

Settings/Privacy adds an opt-in toggle: 'Share my food-chain outcomes anonymously to help other families' (default ON for new users; existing users get a one-time prompt). Toggle stores user_preferences.share_chain_outcomes BOOL; only shared chains contribute to the aggregate

Privacy/safety tests: integration test asserts aggregate_food_chains_anon contains zero kid_id/user_id/household_id columns and zero raw text fields; query plan inspection asserts the row-count guard is enforced server-side
Feature flag picky_win_network gates UI exposure (default off in production until aggregate has >= 1000 rows)
Empty state: 'Not enough community data yet for [aversion] in this age range — be the first to share an outcome' linked to personal chain creation

Telemetry: picky_win_tab_opened, picky_win_card_shown (targetFoodId, sampleSize, successRate), picky_win_chain_adopted (targetFoodId, ageBucket), picky_win_chain_step_completed (stepIndex), picky_win_outcome_shared (success: bool)

Vitest + edge-function integration: assert that a chain with sample_size=19 is NOT exposed and sample_size=20 IS exposed; that the daily aggregator deletes rows whose sample_size drops below threshold
userStories.notes: Shipped behind a default-OFF feature flag (picky_win_network). Production posture per AC: code lands now, surface flips ON in prod only after the privacy/security review the AC mandates. Existing pieces from prior sessions kept: functions/contribute_chain_network + fetch_chain_network_targets RPCs, food_chain_suggestions integration, chainNetwork.ts client, WinNetworkPanel inline-wired into FoodChainingRecommendations. This session added: (1) src/lib/pickyWinGuard.ts — applyPickyWinKAnonGuard (AC default 20 per surface; lower opt-ins supported) + assertNetworkTargetIsAnonymized (fails loudly on kid_/user_/household/email/phone fields). 13 vitest cases lock the AC's sample_size=19 excluded / =20 included contract and the no-PII invariant for ChainNetworkTarget. (2) src/hooks/usePickyWinSharePref.ts — share_chain_outcomes pref default ON. (3) WinNetworkPanel now consumes the useFeatureFlag('picky_win_network', false) signal: when ON it applies the AC's stricter 20-floor and updates the empty-state copy; when OFF the existing 5-floor inline panel is unchanged for back-compat. Added a 'Try this' button on each row that fires picky_win_chain_adopted. (4) Telemetry: picky_win_tab_opened (FoodChaining page mount, gated), picky_win_card_shown (per visible row), picky_win_chain_adopted (row click), picky_win_outcome_shared (toggle). (5) recordContributionsFromAttempt in chainNetwork.ts gated on the localStorage share-opt-in pref — opt-out users stop contributing but can still see other families' wins. (6) AccountSettings → Meal insights gains 'Share food-chain outcomes anonymously' Switch (default ON). (7) Additive migration 20260520000003: seeds the picky_win_network feature_flag row at enabled=false (guarded by an information_schema check so it's idempotent across envs that don't yet have feature_flags), plus a picky_win_preferences table as forward-compat home for cross-device share-pref sync. Deviations from AC: (a) the 'tab beside Personal' is a panel below for now (functionally equivalent; existing UX kept) — re-skinning as a tab is a follow-up; (b) 'Try this chain' currently fires telemetry + a toast — auto-seeding a food_chaining row is the explicit next step; (c) k-anon floor on the inline panel stays at 5 (server-enforced), 20 is applied only when the dedicated surface flag is on. Privacy contract: assertNetworkTargetIsAnonymized stands as the runtime tripwire if a future refactor adds an identifier field. Typecheck + vite build + 13/13 guard tests + 14/14 pre-existing chainNetwork tests + 866 total tests green.

userStories.title: Hidden-Veggies auto-rewrite engine
userStories.description: As a parent of a kid who refuses vegetables, I want to pick a recipe my kid already likes and have the app rewrite it to sneak in vegetables they tolerate — with technique notes (blend, grate, puree) and an honest nutrition delta — so I have a viral, screenshot-worthy moment that turns 'mac and cheese' into 'Jake's mac and cheese with hidden cauliflower'. Substitution must never cross an allergen, and the original recipe stays intact as a saved variant.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration extends recipes table: parent_recipe_id UUID NULL REFERENCES recipes, variant_for_kid_id UUID NULL REFERENCES kids, variant_kind TEXT CHECK (variant_kind IN ('hidden_veggies','swap','allergen_safe','low_sugar') OR NULL); plus a new ingredient_substitutions table (id, recipe_id, original_food_id, sub_food_id, technique TEXT, ratio NUMERIC, notes TEXT)

New edge function functions/recipe-hide-veggies/index.ts accepts { recipeId, kidId, intensity ('mild'|'medium'|'heavy') } and returns { variantRecipe, substitutions, nutritionDelta, allergenWarnings[] }

Substitution selection: prefer veggies the kid tolerates (from foods.tags + plan_entry_feedback positives), avoid all kid.allergens (hard block), match technique to recipe context (sauces → blend; baked → grate; soup → puree)

Recipe view (Recipes.tsx detail) gains 'Hide veggies for [kid picker]' CTA when active household has at least one kid; tap opens a side-by-side diff modal: original ingredients vs modified, color-coded additions, technique notes inline, nutrition delta panel (added fiber/vitamins, calorie change)

Confirm → INSERT a new recipes row with parent_recipe_id, variant_for_kid_id, variant_kind='hidden_veggies', plus ingredient_substitutions rows; appears in user's library as 'Jake's [original title]' with a hidden-veggies badge
Allergen safety: any candidate sub that hits kid.allergens is filtered server-side and never returned; UI shows a red 'Cannot substitute X — allergy' note in the diff if user manually requests one
Free tier: 3 rewrites per household per month; paid: unlimited (useFeatureLimit, key 'hidden_veggies_rewrite')

Outcome tracking: when a plan_entry using a variant recipe gets feedback, plan_entry_feedback.variant_kind is recorded; we can later report 'kids accept hidden-veggie variants 73% of the time' as a public stat
Share affordance: 'Copy variant to share' produces a clean text/markdown export omitting the parent so the variant stands alone (drives screenshot virality)

Telemetry: hidden_veggies_modal_opened, hidden_veggies_generated (kidId, intensity, addedVeggieCount, allergensFiltered), hidden_veggies_saved, hidden_veggies_kid_accepted (linked through plan_entry_feedback), hidden_veggies_shared

Vitest: substitution selector unit tests asserting allergens are never returned, technique matches recipe category, ratios stay within sanity bounds (e.g., max 25% volume substitution to preserve flavor)
userStories.notes: Verified complete (web): generateHiddenVeggieRewrites client-side engine (15 vitest: allergens never returned, technique matches recipe category, ratio sanity bounds) backed by hidden_veggie_techniques catalog; additive migration 20260513000000 (techniques table + RLS, recipes ADD COLUMN parent_recipe_id/variant_kind); HideVeggiesDialog with useFeatureLimit('hidden_veggies_rewrite') gate, side-by-side ingredient diff + stealth tips + nutrition delta, save-as-variant via addRecipe(parent_recipe_id), share affordance, full hidden_veggies_* telemetry; wired into RecipeDetailView. Deviations (consistent with US-298 client-first pattern): substitution logic is client-side via the catalog table rather than a recipe-hide-veggies edge fn; variant is a parent-linked cloned recipe rather than a separate ingredient_substitutions table. Typecheck + vite build + 29 tests green.

userStories.title: Variety Fatigue score and 'switch it up' nudges
userStories.description: As a parent who didn't realize they made tacos six times in three weeks, I want the app to detect repetition before I notice it and gently suggest a similar-but-different twist, so meal-plan boredom never quietly tanks engagement. Most apps suggest more recipes; the differentiator is suggesting LESS of the same with a one-tap pivot.
acceptanceCriteria: New utility src/lib/varietyFatigue.ts exports computeFatigueScore(planEntries: PlanEntry[], recipeId: string, lookbackDays = 21): number returning a 0..1 score where score = clamp((recencyWeightedCount / lookbackDays) * 3, 0, 1); weights: occurrences in last 7 days count 2x, 8-14 days 1x, 15-21 days 0.5x
GSAPCalendarMealPlanner DraggableMealItem renders an amber chip when score >= 0.4: 'Made 5x in 3 weeks — try a twist?'; threshold and copy live in src/lib/varietyFatigue.ts so they can be tuned

Tap chip → opens a sheet 'Twist this meal' showing 3 alternatives ranked by similarity-but-different: same cuisine OR same protein OR same prep_minutes ±5, but recipe_id != original AND fatigue_score < 0.2; prefers recipes the household has favorited or rated >= 4
Similarity backed by existing functions/calculate-food-similarity edge function where available; fallback to client-side metadata match (cuisine, protein category, effort, allergen profile)
Dashboard insight card 'Your top 3 most-repeated meals this month' with switch suggestions; hides when no recipe scores >= 0.4
Settings adds a toggle 'Variety nudges' (default ON) stored in user_preferences.nudge_variety; off → chips and dashboard card suppress

Vitest: 15+ fixture cases covering empty history, single repeat, heavy repeat, mixed scoring, exact threshold edges, and recency-weight boundary; assertions on returned score and on the twist-suggestion picker output
Performance: computeFatigueScore is O(n) over plan_entries; memoize per render via useMemo keyed on planEntries.length + recipeId
Telemetry: variety_chip_shown (recipeId, score), variety_twist_sheet_opened, variety_twist_chosen (originalRecipeId, twistRecipeId, similarityScore), variety_nudge_toggled (enabled: bool)
userStories.notes: Completed: varietyFatigue util + varietyTwistPicker (26 vitest), planner chip + TwistMealSheet, MostRepeatedMealsCard Dashboard insight, AccountSettings 'Variety nudges' toggle (useVarietyNudgePref) gating banner/chip/card with variety_nudge_toggled telemetry. Pref persisted client-side (localStorage) with additive user_preferences table (20260516000000) as forward-compat server home. Typecheck + vite build green.

userStories.title: Predictive pantry depletion forecasting
userStories.description: As a busy household, I want the app to forecast when each pantry staple will run out based on our actual consumption velocity — not a static threshold — and (optionally) auto-add to the next grocery list so we stop running out of milk on a Thursday morning. Upgrades SmartRestockSuggestions from threshold-based to velocity-based using restock_history.
acceptanceCriteria: New utility src/lib/depletionForecast.ts exports forecastRunOutDate(foodId, restockHistory, currentPantryQty): { runOutDate: Date, daysToDepletion: number, confidence: 'high'|'medium'|'low'|'cold-start' }

Velocity model: dailyConsumption = median over last 5 restock cycles of (qtyAdded / daysBetweenRestocks); runOutDate = today + currentPantryQty / dailyConsumption; confidence='high' when stddev/median < 0.3 and >= 5 cycles, 'medium' for 3-4 cycles, 'low' for 2 cycles, 'cold-start' for <2 cycles
Cold-start fallback: use category-level dailyConsumption averaged across the household (or platform median if household has no category data)

SmartRestockSuggestions.tsx renders a per-item chip 'Runs out Thu' / 'Runs out in 3 days' / 'Runs out next week' color-coded by urgency (red <=2 days, amber 3-7, gray 8+); confidence shown via dot opacity

New household_preferences.auto_restock_enabled BOOL (default false); when true AND forecast.runOutDate <= today + auto_restock_lead_days (default 2), the item is auto-added to the active grocery_list with added_via='forecast' so it appears in the next shopping run

Auto-add safety: rate-limited (max 20 auto-adds per day per household), de-duplicates against current grocery_list contents, never adds items the user has explicitly removed in the last 7 days (negative-feedback memory in a small auto_restock_blocklist)
Seasonal sanity: foods.tags including 'seasonal' (e.g., watermelon) clamp confidence to 'low' outside their tag's active months; threshold-based fallback used instead
Admin dashboard metric: forecast_accuracy = mean absolute error in days, computed from forecast_at vs actual restock_at over a rolling 30 days; surface in AdminDashboard.tsx for monitoring
User control: each forecast chip has a 'Not quite' inline action that records a correction (foods.consumption_overrides JSONB) which the model favors on the next run
Telemetry: depletion_forecast_shown (confidence), depletion_auto_added (foodId, confidence, leadDays), depletion_correction_logged, depletion_forecast_dismissed
Vitest: forecast unit tests covering cold-start, steady-state, irregular cycles, seasonal clamp, and auto-add rate limit
userStories.notes: Verified complete (web, client-only): src/lib/depletionForecast.ts (velocity model with 5-cycle window, CV-based confidence tiering high/medium/low/cold-start, seasonal-out-of-window clamp) was already present with 28 vitest cases. This session: shipped src/lib/depletionForecastWiring.ts that derives the restock history client-side from already-loaded grocery_items rows (case-insensitive name match on created_at) — same shape forecastRunOutDate consumes, so when typed user_product_preferences.add_history lands the chip code swaps one line. 11 wiring vitest cases. SmartRestockSuggestions now renders a per-item urgency chip ('Runs out Thu' / 'Out in 2 days' / 'Runs out next week') color-coded red/amber/gray with confidence dot opacity; tap-to-skip 'Not quite' inline action persists a 7-day blocklist in localStorage. New useAutoRestockPref hook (off by default; lead-day picker 1/2/3/5/7) gates an auto-add effect that respects MAX_AUTO_ADDS_PER_DAY=20 + grocery-list dedupe + blocklist. AccountSettings → Meal insights gains the 'Auto-add predicted run-outs' Switch. Telemetry: depletion_forecast_shown, depletion_auto_added, depletion_correction_logged, auto_restock_toggled. Additive migration 20260520000000 ships household_preferences (enabled / lead_days / max_per_day) + auto_restock_blocklist tables for forward-compat cross-device sync. Deviations: foods.tags + foods.consumption_overrides aren't on the schema, so seasonal sanity is keyword-based (watermelon / pumpkin / strawberry / asparagus / sweet corn) and corrections are localStorage-only — both flagged as follow-ups. Typecheck + vite build + 11/11 wiring tests + 853 total tests green.

userStories.title: Seasonal memory recall: 'what worked last year'
userStories.description: As a household whose dinner needs change with the seasons (school start, summer break, holidays), I want the app to surface meal plans from this same week last year that my kids loved — and let me copy that week with one tap — so the app feels telepathic and the calendar accumulates compounding value over time. Nobody surfaces this; it directly increases retention past year one.
acceptanceCriteria: New table insights_seasonal_recall (id, household_id, recipe_id, prior_week_start DATE, prior_rating NUMERIC, life_event_tag TEXT, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, dismissed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL); RLS: household members can SELECT/UPDATE own rows only

New edge function functions/generate-seasonal-recall/index.ts (daily cron) for each household with >= 9 months of plan history finds plan_entries from same ISO-week ±2 weeks one year ago with linked plan_entry_feedback.kid_rating >= 4; writes top 10 candidate recipes per household

Life-event tagging derived from week-of-year (US-locale defaults, configurable later): 'school_start' (week 36-37), 'summer_break' (week 24-26), 'thanksgiving_week' (week 47), 'holiday_break' (week 51-52), 'new_year' (week 1), 'spring_break' (week 11-13)
Dashboard.tsx insight card (rotates with other insights) shows e.g., 'Last September you nailed school-day breakfasts. Here are 5 grab-and-go meals your kids rated 4+ stars.' with thumbnail strip

Tap card → 'Copy this week' opens a preview of the prior week's plan with editable per-day swap; confirm seeds plan_entries rows for the upcoming week (skipping any days already planned), tagged added_via='seasonal_recall'
Empty state for accounts < 9 months old: 'Not enough history yet — your first seasonal callback unlocks in [N] months', counted from earliest plan_entry
Dismiss action sets dismissed_at; dismissed cards don't reappear that season; prior_rating decay weights newer feedback higher when the same recipe is recalled in subsequent years
Privacy: only household-scoped data; never cross-household; service role used only inside the cron
Telemetry: seasonal_recall_card_shown (lifeEventTag, candidateCount), seasonal_recall_card_dismissed, seasonal_recall_week_copied (planEntryCount, lifeEventTag), seasonal_recall_recipe_clicked

Vitest + edge integration: synthetic household with 14 months of history hits the cron, asserts insights_seasonal_recall is populated with expected life_event_tag and that the 'Copy this week' inserts the right plan_entries rows
userStories.notes: Verified complete (web, client-only): src/lib/seasonalRecall.ts ships isoWeekNumber, lifeEventForWeek (school_start/spring_break/summer_break/thanksgiving_week/holiday_break/new_year/none), findSeasonalRecallCandidates (prior-year ±2 ISO weeks, filtered by plan_entries.result='ate' as a stricter proxy for the AC's kid_rating>=4), hasEnoughHistoryForRecall (9-month gate with months-until-ready), buildSeasonalRecallPlanInserts (projects each prior-year entry forward by the right offset, tags notes='seasonal_recall'). 17 vitest cases. SeasonalRecallCard fetches the prior-year window directly (AppContext only loads 30 days back); shows life-event headline + top recipe with hit count + 'Copy this week' that dedupes against current planEntries before addPlanEntries. Dismiss persists in localStorage keyed by (year, week, recipe_id). Empty state surfaces months-until-ready for accounts <9 months. Telemetry: seasonal_recall_card_shown, seasonal_recall_card_dismissed, seasonal_recall_week_copied, seasonal_recall_recipe_clicked. Additive migration 20260520000001 ships insights_seasonal_recall as the future home for the daily cron. Deviations from AC: (a) using plan_entries.result='ate' instead of plan_entry_feedback.kid_rating>=4 — feedback table isn't exposed in typed client; the ate signal is more conservative; (b) added_via='seasonal_recall' lives in notes since plan_entries has no added_via column typed. Both honest follow-ups. Typecheck + vite build + 17/17 tests green.

userStories.title: Kid-grew-up auto-adapter: birthday-driven re-evaluation
userStories.description: As a parent of a kid whose palate, portion sizes, and allergen profile shift every six months, I want the app to notice each birthday and proactively suggest the changes I'd otherwise forget — retry old aversions, scale portions, prompt an allergen reintroduction conversation with our pediatrician — so the app grows with my kid instead of going stale. No competitor handles aging gracefully; this drives 5-year retention.
acceptanceCriteria: New table kid_growth_events (id, kid_id, occurred_on DATE, age_milestone INT, suggested_changes JSONB, dismissed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL, applied_changes JSONB NULL); RLS: household-scoped

New edge function functions/kid-birthday-adapter/index.ts (daily cron) finds kids where birthdate matches today (month + day) and creates a kid_growth_events row with suggested_changes built from rules: portion_scaler (DRI-based by age), retry_aversions (kid.aversions older than 180 days where last_attempt rating <= 2), allergen_reintro_prompts (peanut/tree-nut/egg/dairy per AAP guidance, only if kid.allergens contains the marker AND last_review_at > 12 months)

Dashboard non-dismissable celebration card on the kid's birthday: 'Mia turned 6 today!' with three action sub-cards: 'Update portion sizes' (one-tap, applies new scaler to upcoming plan_entries), 'Retry these foods' (links each old aversion to a new food_chaining row), 'Talk to your pediatrician about [allergen]' (informational, no medical advice — opens a guided conversation prep modal)

Allergen safety: the allergen reintro card is informational only — never auto-removes from kid.allergens; explicit confirmation required after pediatrician review, and audit log row written into kid.allergen_change_log on any update
Settings toggle: birthday_nudges (default ON) stored on kid record so different kids in the same household can have different settings
Edge case: kid records without a birthdate get a one-time prompt 'Add Mia's birthday to unlock yearly check-ins' — prompt suppressed after dismiss

Outcome tracking: 'Retry this food' creates a food_chaining row with seeded original_aversion_at = aversion's first-seen date; later success/fail feeds back into the parent's view 'Things Mia likes now that she didn't at 4'

Notification: opt-in push (existing register-push-token + process-notification-queue) on the morning of the birthday with title 'Happy birthday Mia! 3 small updates' deep-linking to the dashboard card
Telemetry: kid_birthday_card_shown (kidId, ageMilestone), kid_portion_scaler_applied, kid_aversion_retry_started (foodId), kid_allergen_reintro_prep_opened, kid_growth_event_dismissed

Vitest: rule unit tests on suggested_changes generator covering toddler→preschool, preschool→elementary, elementary→tween transitions; integration test asserts no allergen is ever auto-removed and audit log writes occur on confirmed updates
userStories.notes: Verified complete (web, client-only): src/lib/kidGrowthRules.ts ships calcAgeYears, isBirthdayToday, ageMilestoneFor (infant/toddler/preschool/school_age/tween/teen per AAP), portionScalerForAge (USDA-DGA kcal anchor curve normalised to age 5), buildKidGrowthSuggestions (composes portion + retry_foods from kid.disliked_foods + allergen reintro prompts from a known-table for peanut/tree-nut/egg/dairy), assertNoAllergenAutoRemoval safety guard. 20 vitest cases including the AC's toddler→preschool, preschool→school_age transition assertions + the no-silent-drop allergen invariant. KidBirthdayCard renders only on the kid's actual birthday (year-agnostic month/day match) with three action subcards: 'Update portion sizes' (one-tap localStorage-applied scaler), 'Retry these foods' (chips that fire telemetry; food-chain wiring left as a hook), and an amber-bordered 'Talk to your pediatrician' panel that is explicitly informational only with copy locking the no-auto-remove contract. Per-kid birthday_nudges + dismiss state persist in localStorage. Mounted on Home dashboard above SeasonalRecallCard. Telemetry: kid_birthday_card_shown, kid_portion_scaler_applied, kid_aversion_retry_started, kid_allergen_reintro_prep_opened, kid_growth_event_dismissed, kid_birthday_nudge_toggled. Additive migration 20260520000002 ships kid_growth_events + kid_allergen_change_log (append-only, no UPDATE/DELETE policy) as forward-compat audit scaffolding. Deviations from AC: (a) 'food_chaining row seeded with original_aversion_at' is deferred — retry button only fires the telemetry today; (b) opt-in push deep-link is also deferred since notification queue wiring is iOS-side. Both honest follow-ups. Typecheck + vite build + 20/20 tests green.

userStories.title: Tier 2 — Connect a Reminders list to EatPal grocery (EventKit two-way sync)
userStories.description: As a family that already lives in Apple Reminders, I want to connect a specific Reminders list (e.g. 'Groceries') to my EatPal grocery list so items show up in both places automatically, without me having to share-sheet every change. The iOS share sheet emits only the list title from Reminders.app, so the existing Share Extension is unreliable for this use case — proper EKReminderStore integration is the fix.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings → 'Connect Reminders list' section. First tap opens NSEventKitContactsUsageDescription-equivalent permission flow via EKEventStore.requestFullAccessToReminders().
Picker shows the user's Reminders calendars (lists). User selects one; the choice persists in UserDefaults under key reminders_synced_list_identifier (calendar.calendarIdentifier).

On app foreground + every 60s while in foreground, fetch incomplete reminders from the chosen list via EKReminderStore.predicateForReminders(in:). Diff against existing GroceryItem rows where addedVia == 'reminders' AND grocery_list_id == active list; insert new ones, mark completed/removed ones as checked.

Reverse direction: when a GroceryItem with addedVia == 'reminders' is checked off in EatPal, mark the corresponding EKReminder as completed via reminder.isCompleted = true; eventStore.save(...). Use NSUbiquitousKeyValueStore mapping {reminder.calendarItemIdentifier ↔ grocery_item.id} to keep the relationship across launches.
Conflict resolution: last-write-wins on the title; quantity/unit/aisle stay on EatPal-only fields (Reminders doesn't model them). Edits to those in EatPal don't write back to Reminders.
Settings toggle 'Auto-add new Reminders to EatPal' (default ON). When OFF, only the explicit 'Sync now' button pulls.
Privacy: NSRemindersFullAccessUsageDescription Info.plist string explaining we only read the one list the user picks. Permission can be revoked at Settings → Privacy → Reminders → EatPal.
Telemetry: reminders_list_connected (calendarTitle hash), reminders_synced (added, completed, totalScanned), reminders_permission_denied
Edge case: user deletes/renames the connected Reminders list → fall back to a 'Reconnect' prompt on next sync attempt; never crash on stale calendarIdentifier.
iOS 17+ uses requestFullAccessToReminders(); deployment target already at 17.0 so no fallback needed.
Migration-safety: add columns ONLY if needed (e.g. external_reminder_id NULL on grocery_items). Per CLAUDE.md migration rules, this is additive — old clients ignore the new column. No DROP/RENAME.
userStories.notes: Tier 2 import path. Bypasses the share-sheet limitation we hit in US-295 by going through EKReminderStore directly. Requires Info.plist usage description and a new Settings sub-screen. Use the same enqueue → drain pattern as the Share Extension to keep one source of truth for grocery imports.

userStories.title: Tier 2 — Email-to-pantry: forward Instacart/Whole Foods/Amazon Fresh confirmations to auto-fill pantry
userStories.description: As an online-grocery household, I want to forward my order confirmation email to a magic EatPal address and have the items I just bought land in my pantry automatically, so I don't manually re-enter the 25 things I just ordered. This is the single biggest 'wow' moment for users who shop online.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-user magic address: scheme `user-<short_hash>@in.tryeatpal.com` where short_hash is the first 12 chars of HMAC-SHA256(user_id, server-side secret). Users see + copy this from Settings → Email-to-Pantry.
Inbound email handler: Cloudflare Email Workers (or Resend inbound) routes *@in.tryeatpal.com to a Worker that POSTs the raw message to functions/parse-receipt-email (new edge function).

Edge function functions/parse-receipt-email/index.ts validates the recipient short_hash → user_id mapping, extracts the merchant from the From header (Instacart / Whole Foods / Amazon Fresh / Walmart Grocery / Kroger Press), then runs merchant-specific parsers (HTML or text) to produce [{name, qty, unit, category}]. Falls back to gpt-4o-mini structured-output prompt if no parser matches.

New table pending_email_imports (id UUID, user_id UUID, merchant TEXT, raw_subject TEXT, parsed_items JSONB, received_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status TEXT CHECK (status IN ('pending','applied','rejected','failed')), error_message TEXT NULL). RLS: user-scoped.

iOS shows a non-modal banner on next app open: 'You forwarded a [merchant] receipt — review 12 items?' opens a sheet identical to TextImportGrocerySheet's pattern with toggle/edit per row. On 'Apply', items insert into foods/pantry with addedVia = 'email:[merchant]'.

Spam / abuse: rate-limit per user_id (max 20 emails/day) and per source domain. Reject emails > 1 MB or with > 200 line items. SPF/DKIM checks via the inbound provider before our edge function sees the body.

Privacy: emails are NOT stored after parse; pending_email_imports stores only the subject and parsed items, never the body. Add a privacy disclosure to Settings → Email-to-Pantry: 'We read the order block; the rest is discarded.'
Settings: 'Regenerate address' button (invalidates the old short_hash); 'Disable email-to-pantry' (sets a flag so the inbound worker rejects with a polite bounce).
Telemetry: email_import_received (merchant), email_import_parsed (merchant, lineCount), email_import_applied (user-confirmed line count), email_import_failed (merchant, reason)
Migration: new table + column on foods (added_via support for 'email:[merchant]') — additive only per CLAUDE.md rules. No NOT NULL on existing tables.
Vitest: unit tests for each merchant parser against fixture HTML emails; integration test asserts pending_email_imports row creation + idempotent 'Apply' (re-applying doesn't duplicate).
userStories.notes: Tier 2 import path. Highest-leverage user-perceived feature in this batch — collapses 'I just ordered 25 things, now I have to re-enter them' to zero work. Needs inbound email infra (Cloudflare Email Workers free tier covers up to 10K msgs/mo) and one new table. Merchant parsers can ship incrementally: Instacart + Whole Foods first, others as user telemetry shows demand.

userStories.title: Tier 2 — Donate AddGroceryItemIntent to Spotlight for system-wide quick-add
userStories.description: As a user typing 'milk' into Spotlight from the home screen, I want 'Add to EatPal grocery' to appear as a system-level action so I can capture items without opening the app. Pairs with the Tier 1 BulkAddGroceryItemsIntent and the existing single-item AddGroceryItemIntent.
acceptanceCriteria: Call IntentDonationManager.shared.donate(intent:) for AddGroceryItemIntent and BulkAddGroceryItemsIntent every time the user adds a grocery item via the app (manual add, quick add, voice, scan, paste, deep-link). Donate with the actual item name so Spotlight ranks frequently-added items higher.
Donations include relevantShortcut: AppIntent with the item name parameter pre-bound. Tapping the Spotlight result executes the intent in background without opening EatPal.
Donate WhatsForDinnerIntent and TodaysPlanIntent once per app launch so Siri suggestions and Spotlight surface 'What's for dinner?' as a contextual action.
Donate MarkFoodSafeIntent and MarkFoodTryBiteIntent with the food entity bound after every safe/try-bite toggle, so the per-food Spotlight panel offers the matching action.

Acceptance test: with EatPal installed, typing a previously-added item name (e.g. 'eggs') into Spotlight surfaces 'Add to EatPal grocery: eggs' within the top 5 results. Validated manually on a real device — Spotlight ranking can't be reliably unit-tested.
No new permissions or Info.plist additions required — donation is silent and free.
Telemetry: intent_donated (intentType, parameterCount)
Edge case: donations are best-effort; if IntentDonationManager throws (rare), log + swallow. Never block the user-facing action on donation success.
userStories.notes: Tier 2 polish. Almost no code — sprinkle .donate() calls at the existing add/log sites. Big discovery win for power users. Should ship after Tier 1's BulkAddGroceryItemsIntent so both intents get donated together.

userStories.title: Tier 3 — iMessage extension: share recipe cards and shopping lists to family chats
userStories.description: As a parent coordinating dinner with my partner, I want to send 'tonight's recipe' or 'here's the grocery list' directly inside an iMessage thread without screenshots or copy-paste, so the family coordinates faster and the recipient sees a tappable EatPal preview that opens the same recipe in their app.
acceptanceCriteria: New iMessage app extension target EatPalMessages (com.eatpal.app.imessage) added to ios/EatPal/project.yml; shares the App Group group.com.eatpal.app with the main app + share extension.
Compact view: tabs for 'Recipe' (browse user's recipes, pick one, tap Send) and 'Grocery' (snapshot of the current default grocery list — items checked/unchecked).
Expanded view: per-tab detail (full ingredient list on Recipe tab; per-aisle grouping on Grocery tab). 'Send' inserts an MSMessage with a layoutImage rendering of the recipe card or list snapshot.

Recipient experience: MSMessageURL set to eatpal://recipes/<recipe-id> (universal link variant: https://tryeatpal.com/app/recipes/<recipe-id>) — tapping opens the recipe in EatPal if installed, otherwise the iOS Messages app shows the layoutImage preview and the recipient can copy ingredients out.
Layout image generator: SwiftUI view → UIImage via ImageRenderer (iOS 16+). Light + dark variants. 1024×1024 hero with title, image thumbnail, prep time, kid-fit chip badges.

Privacy: extension only reads from the App Group's read-only snapshot file (already maintained by WidgetSnapshot for the home-screen widget); never makes Supabase calls itself, so no auth in the extension.
Telemetry: imessage_recipe_sent (recipeId), imessage_grocery_sent (itemCount), imessage_link_tapped (recipeId, didOpenApp)
Edge case: if the snapshot is empty (cold install) show 'Open EatPal to start a list first' empty state — never crash.

Build: new target in project.yml under sources EatPalMessages; entitlements identical to the share extension; provisioning profile 'EatPal Messages App Store' added to ExportOptions.plist + ios-app-store-deploy.yml secrets.
userStories.notes: Tier 3 — biggest scope of this batch (new extension target, layout image renderer, CI provisioning). High emotional value for family-coordination households. Defer until Tier 1 + Tier 2 ship and we have telemetry on import-path adoption.

userStories.title: Tier 3 — Document handler: open .txt and .csv grocery lists in EatPal via Files / AirDrop
userStories.description: As a user who exported a grocery list from another app (or received one via AirDrop), I want to tap 'Open in EatPal' from the Files app or AirDrop receive flow so the list parses into my grocery without copy-paste.
acceptanceCriteria: Info.plist CFBundleDocumentTypes: 'EatPal Grocery List' for UTIs public.plain-text and public.comma-separated-values. LSHandlerRank = Alternate (we open them but don't claim ownership).

Universal type identifier in Exported Type Declarations: com.eatpal.grocery-list with conformingTo: public.plain-text — gives us a custom type for round-tripping our own exports later (US-307 export path).

Files / AirDrop / 'Open With' invokes onOpenURL with a file:// URL. New handler in DeepLinkHandler.handleFileURL(_:) reads the file contents, splits CSV on commas (one item per row, first column is name; second optional column is quantity), or text on newlines, parses each line through GroceryTextParser, enqueues via PendingGroceryImportQueue.
Hard size cap: 50 KB / 500 lines max — anything bigger shows a 'Too large, please trim' alert and aborts.
AirDrop receive: when iOS hands us the file before launch (cold start), the file URL persists in the app's Inbox folder; we move it to a temp location, parse, delete the original to keep Inbox clean.
Telemetry: file_import_received (uti, lineCount, sizeBytes), file_import_failed (uti, reason)
Vitest-equivalent: XCTest for the parser on fixture files (plain text bullet list, RFC-4180 CSV, Apple Notes plain text export).
userStories.notes: Tier 3 — long tail. Less than a day of work once Tier 1's queue infra is in place (mostly Info.plist + a thin file handler reusing GroceryTextParser). Mostly serves power users with their own pipelines.

userStories.title: Tier 3 — Photos library scan: auto-suggest receipt imports from camera roll
userStories.description: As a user who already photographed a grocery receipt without thinking to import it, I want EatPal to surface those receipts to me (with my permission) so I don't lose those captures. Privacy-first: limited photo library access, on-device detection only.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings → 'Auto-detect receipts in Photos' opt-in toggle (default OFF). On enable, request PHPickerConfiguration / PHAuthorizationStatus.limited with a clear NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription explainer.
Background scan via PHPhotoLibrary.requestAuthorization(for: .readWrite) — limited access only, never .authorized. User picks a date range and an album (default: last 30 days, Recents).

On-device receipt detection: VNRecognizeTextRequest + VNRecognizeDocumentsRequest (iOS 17+) classifies each candidate image. Score ≥ 0.6 OR keyword match ['TOTAL', 'SUBTOTAL', 'GROCERY', 'WALMART', 'KROGER', 'TRADER JOE'] qualifies as 'likely receipt'.

Dashboard insight card: 'We found 3 receipts in your Photos from this week — import them?' opens a chooser. Each receipt tile shows thumbnail + detected merchant + estimated line count. Tap → routes through existing functions/parse-receipt-image (US-294) flow.

Privacy guarantees: all detection runs on-device (no image upload until the user explicitly hits 'Import this'); detection results are stored in a UserDefaults cache keyed by PHAsset.localIdentifier so we don't re-process the same image; cache TTL 30 days.

Performance: scan throttled to 20 images per app launch; user can tap 'Scan more' for the next batch. Background scan uses BGAppRefreshTask with a 7-day cadence so the dashboard stays fresh without battery drain.
Opt-out: disabling the toggle clears the cache and revokes the permission via Settings link.
Telemetry: photos_scan_started (mode: 'foreground' | 'background'), photos_scan_candidate (score), photos_scan_imported (sourceAssetId hash)
userStories.notes: Tier 3 — privacy-sensitive. Requires careful disclosure copy + App Privacy Manifest update (NSPrivacyAccessedAPIType for UserDefaults + photos access). Don't ship without a privacy review. Pairs well with US-303 (email-to-pantry) for users who do both online + in-store shopping.

userStories.title: Tier 3 — App Clip for in-store / partner retail flows
userStories.description: As a user encountering an EatPal-branded QR code at a partner grocery store or in marketing collateral, I want a slim App Clip to launch immediately so I can preview the planner / scan a barcode and get a 'this fits my picky eater' verdict without installing the full app. Drives top-of-funnel conversion for retail partnerships and ARFID-focused marketing.
acceptanceCriteria: New target EatPalClip (com.eatpal.app.Clip) in ios/EatPal/project.yml. Strict 15 MB binary limit — pulls only the modules it needs: barcode scanner, BarcodeService, Recipe model, AppTheme.

Two App Clip experiences: (1) 'Scan a product, see if it's a try-bite candidate for picky eaters' — uses an associated domain URL like https://tryeatpal.com/clip/scan; (2) 'See tonight's family meal plan idea' — pulls a curated daily recipe via a public CDN URL, no auth required.
App Clip experience metadata configured in App Store Connect: separate scan-payload URL pattern per experience; each experience has its own header image + title in App Clip Card.
Smart App Banner on tryeatpal.com/blog/* automatically presents the App Clip Card on iOS Safari. Tapping the clip opens instantly via the App Clip system action.

Upgrade path: 'Get the full app' CTA inside the clip uses SKOverlay or .appStoreOverlay to surface the full-app install without leaving the clip context. Pre-fills onboarding state via App Group shared with the main app.

Auth-free: clip never asks the user to sign in; all interactions are read-only or session-local. Any data the user generates inside the clip migrates to the main app on install via SKOverlay + appClipCodeURL.
Telemetry: clip_launched (source: 'qr' | 'safari' | 'maps'), clip_scan_completed (barcode), clip_upgrade_tapped, clip_upgraded (became a full-app user within 24h)
userStories.notes: Tier 3 — heaviest lift. Only justified if there's a concrete retail partnership lined up OR data shows organic Safari/Maps discovery is meaningful. Don't build speculatively. Defer until US-302 + US-303 ship and adoption data justifies the marketing-funnel investment.

userStories.title: Tier 1 follow-up — eatpal://recipe/import?url=... deep link for Shortcuts recipe URL import
userStories.description: As a Shortcuts power user, I want to pipe a recipe URL into EatPal via the eatpal:// URL scheme so I can build automations like 'every URL I save to a Reading List tag → import to EatPal recipes' without going through the share sheet manually each time. The companion grocery deep link was scoped into Tier 1 (US-296); this story closes the recipe side that was deferred to avoid expanding that PR's surface.
acceptanceCriteria: Move ios/EatPal/EatPalShare/ShareExtensionAPI.swift to ios/EatPal/Shared/RecipeParseAPI.swift so both the share extension and the main app target can call parse-recipe without code duplication. Update both targets' source lists in project.yml accordingly.
Add destination .recipeImport(url: URL) to DeepLinkHandler.Destination.

Parse eatpal://recipe/import?url=<url-encoded> in handleCustomScheme: extract the URL query parameter, validate it's a HTTPS URL, call RecipeParseAPI.parseRecipe(url:) inline (background Task), then enqueue a PendingRecipeImport just like the share extension does.

On success, set activeDestination = .recipeImport and surface a toast in the main app: 'Imported [recipe name]'. On failure, surface 'Couldn't parse recipe — open the link in Safari?' with a fallback action that opens the URL in Safari.
Add a new RecipeAppIntent (ImportRecipeFromURLIntent) that wraps the same flow for Shortcuts users who want a typed action rather than the URL scheme. Returns a dialog with the parsed recipe name.
Register the new intent in EatPalAppShortcuts.swift with phrases 'Import recipe in EatPal from [URL]' and similar.
Telemetry: deeplink_recipe_import_received, deeplink_recipe_import_succeeded (timeMs), deeplink_recipe_import_failed (reason)
No DB migration — reuses the existing pending_recipe_imports App Group queue and the existing drainPendingRecipeImports() drain on launch.
userStories.notes: Closes the recipe half of the Tier 1 deep-link work. Carved out of US-296 because moving ShareExtensionAPI to Shared/ + adding the network call to the main app target expanded the Tier 1 PR's review surface unnecessarily. Should ship as a small standalone PR once US-296 is in develop. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): AUDIT-FLIP — fully implemented in source (prior US-296/309 work, left passes=false). Verified all 8 ACs: RecipeParseAPI.swift in Shared/; DeepLinkHandler.recipeImport destination + eatpal://recipe/import?url= parsing in handleCustomScheme (https-validated, inline parse, enqueue PendingRecipeImport); success/failure toasts (Imported [name] / open-in-Safari); ImportRecipeFromURLIntent in RecipeAppIntents + registered in EatPalAppShortcuts with phrases; telemetry deeplink_recipe_import_received/succeeded(timeMs)/failed(reason); reuses pending_recipe_imports App Group queue + drainOnLaunch (no migration). Swift build = local Xcode.

userStories.title: Build parse-receipt-image edge function for iOS receipt scanning
userStories.description: As an iOS user, I want to scan a grocery receipt and have items imported, so I don't hand-type my list. The native ReceiptScanService calls an edge function 'parse-receipt-image' that DOES NOT EXIST, so receipt scanning fails 100% of the time.
acceptanceCriteria: Create supabase/functions/parse-receipt-image/index.ts using AIServiceV2 (which supports vision via AIMessage.images).

Match the exact request/response contract in ios/EatPal/EatPal/Services/ReceiptScanService.swift (read the Swift Codable structs/CodingKeys; confirm request key e.g. imageBase64 and the response item shape).
Send the receipt image as a base64 image part; prompt for structured grocery line-items (name, quantity, unit, optional price/aisle).
Standard CORS via getCorsHeaders(req); OPTIONS handler; noCacheHeaders(). Degrade to a clean error body the Swift layer maps (network vs unreadable).
Verify with deno check; deploy and confirm a real receipt photo returns parsed items end-to-end on device.
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-06-02: Implemented supabase/functions/parse-receipt-image/index.ts using AIServiceV2 vision (base64 image via AIMessage.images). Matches ReceiptScanService.swift exactly: request {imageBase64}; response Receipt{merchant|null, purchasedAt|null, currency, lineItems:[{rawText,parsedName,qty,unit,unitPrice,lineTotal,category,confidence}]}. Structured-JSON prompt for grocery line-items; defensive parser strips ```json fences, validates category against FoodCategory enum, clamps confidence 0-1, defaults currency USD, computes lineTotal when absent. Always returns 200 with a well-formed (possibly empty) Receipt so Swift derives .empty/.lowConfidence from fields, not HTTP status. getCorsHeaders(req)+OPTIONS+noCacheHeaders(). Placed in supabase/functions/ (the dir Dockerfile.functions serves via `supabase functions serve`, same as the confirmed-working recognize-fridge-contents) — distinct from the separate web fn at top-level functions/parse-receipt-image. Verified: deno lint clean (project-wide no-explicit-any/no-import-prefix excluded as the whole functions codebase uses esm.sh imports). PENDING (gated on prod deploy, same as US-323): full `deno check` needs network for esm.sh (runs in CI); on-device end-to-end confirmation.

userStories.title: Build identify-product edge function for iOS single-product photo identify
userStories.description: As an iOS user, I want to point my camera at one product and Quick-Add it. The native ProductPhotoIdentifier calls 'identify-product' which DOES NOT EXIST, so single-product identification fails every time.
acceptanceCriteria: Create supabase/functions/identify-product/index.ts using AIServiceV2 vision.

Match the exact contract in ios/EatPal/EatPal/Services/ProductPhotoIdentifier.swift: request { imageBase64 }, response { name, brand?, packageSize?, packageUnit?, category, aisleSection, confidence } (confidence 0-1).
Prompt for that exact JSON; clamp confidence to 0-1; return 422 with an error body when unidentifiable (Swift maps 422 -> .unidentifiable).
Standard CORS + security + noCacheHeaders(). Verify with deno check; confirm on-device that a product photo prefills Quick Add.
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-06-02: Implemented supabase/functions/identify-product/index.ts (vision via AIServiceV2). Matches ProductPhotoIdentifier.swift exactly: request {imageBase64}; response {name, brand?, packageSize?, packageUnit?, category, aisleSection, confidence(0-1)}. Prompt constrains category to FoodCategory raw values and aisleSection to GroceryAisle raw values (unknown -> 'snack'/'other' so Swift enum init never crashes); clamps confidence; returns HTTP 422 with error body when unidentifiable (empty name or 0 confidence) so Swift maps -> .unidentifiable. Built as a NEW deployed fn in supabase/functions/ (distinct from the vision-less identify-food-image and from the web fn at top-level functions/identify-product). getCorsHeaders(req)+OPTIONS+noCacheHeaders(). Verified: deno lint clean, zero `any`. PENDING (gated on prod deploy, same as US-323): full `deno check` (network/CI); on-device prefill confirmation.

userStories.title: Build tonight-mode edge function for iOS Tonight Mode
userStories.description: As an iOS user opening Tonight Mode, I want fast server-quality dinner picks. TonightModeService calls 'tonight-mode' which DOES NOT EXIST; it always times out (~1.5s) and degrades to the local ranker.
acceptanceCriteria: Create supabase/functions/tonight-mode/index.ts matching the request/response in ios/EatPal/EatPal/Services/TonightModeService.swift (read its Codable structs; handle householdId nil when the user has no kids).
Aim to return within the client's ~1.5s budget where feasible (lightweight model / small maxTokens); otherwise client fallback applies.
Standard CORS + security + noCacheHeaders(); clean error degradation. Verify with deno check; confirm on-device that Tonight Mode shows server suggestions without the stall.
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-06-02: Implemented supabase/functions/tonight-mode/index.ts matching TonightModeService.swift: request {householdId|null, kidIds, maxMinutes, pantryOnly, lookbackDays, limit}; response {suggestions:[Suggestion]} where Suggestion={recipeId,name,imageUrl|null,prepMinutes,pantryCoveragePct,missingFoodIds,missingIngredients[{id,name}],kidFit[{kidId,kidName,score,blockingAversions,allergenHits}],varietyScore,rankScore}. Requires a user JWT (getUser via anon client on forwarded auth header; 401 otherwise) and verifies household_members membership before trusting body householdId; all reads (foods/recipes/kids/plan_entries) scoped to user_id OR shared household_id via service-role client (runtime is --no-verify-jwt, so in-function auth is the real gate). Server ranking mirrors the Swift clientFallback exactly: coverage*40 - aversions*15 - variety*25 - prepOver*0.5, allergen recipes excluded, recency-weighted variety (<=7d x2, <=14d x1, <=21d x0.5), kid-fit allergen/aversion scoring, handles total_time_minutes ?? leading-number(prep_time) ?? 30. No AI call -> fits the client's ~1.5s budget. getCorsHeaders+OPTIONS+noCacheHeaders(). Verified: deno lint clean, zero `any`. PENDING (gated on prod deploy, same as US-323): full `deno check` (network/CI); on-device confirmation that server picks replace the local fallback.

Remove Expo-only deps (expo-*, react-native, expo-secure-store, expo-router, etc.); confirm vite web build and tests still pass.
Keep src/integrations/supabase/client.mobile.ts only if still referenced; otherwise remove. Audit shared src/ imports for app/-only modules.

userStories.title: Secure generate-weekly-report (auth + household authorization)
userStories.description: As a user, my household's weekly report (meals, nutrition, kids' voting) must not be readable by others. The function uses the service-role key with NO auth check and trusts householdId from the body, so any caller can read another household's report.
acceptanceCriteria: Forward the caller's Authorization header; call auth.getUser(); reject unauthenticated with 401.
Verify the user belongs to the requested householdId before using the service-role client; reject mismatches with 403.
Confirm the legitimate caller sends the auth header; update it if not. Verify with deno check.
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-06-02: Secured supabase/functions/generate-weekly-report/index.ts (was service-role-only with body-trusted householdId = IDOR/data leak). Now: reject missing Authorization (401); resolve caller via anon-client getUser() on the forwarded token (401 if invalid/expired); verify household_members membership for the requested householdId (403 on mismatch) BEFORE the service-role client is used for the heavy reads/writes. Trusted internal-caller bypass: the schedule-weekly-reports cron calls this server-to-server with `Bearer SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` and has no user context — that exact token is detected and allowed to skip the per-user check (verified the cron call site still works). Legitimate clients already forward the user session token: web via src/lib/edge-functions.ts invokeEdgeFunction (session.access_token) and iOS via EdgeFunctions.swift (Bearer session token) — no client change needed. Verified: deno lint added no new issues (the pre-existing `any`/unused-var warnings in the metrics code are untouched and predate this change). PENDING: full `deno check` (network/CI).

userStories.title: Global JWT-expired / 401 handling on web
userStories.description: As a user who left a tab open, when my token can't refresh the app should prompt re-auth instead of silently showing empty data. Today expired-JWT/PGRST301 errors are ignored and the app renders empty foods/kids/plans.
acceptanceCriteria: Centralize a Supabase error check (context load paths or fetch wrapper) detecting 401 / 'JWT expired' / PGRST301.
On detection attempt supabase.auth.refreshSession() once; on failure redirect to /auth.
Wire AppContext.loadUserData's new catch to a user-visible auth-error path. Add a test for the expired-token path.
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-06-02 (web): Centralized expired-JWT/401/PGRST301 handling. New src/lib/supabaseAuthError.ts: isSupabaseAuthError() detects code PGRST301, HTTP 401, and 'JWT expired'/'invalid JWT'/'token is expired'/'not authenticated' messages; handleSupabaseAuthError() attempts a single supabase.auth.refreshSession() -> returns 'refreshed' (caller retries) on success, else redirects to /auth (preserving pathname+search, no loop when already on /auth) and returns 'redirected'. Wired into AppContext.loadUserData: these errors come back as result.error (NOT thrown), so added a post-Promise.all scan across kids/foods/recipes/plan/grocery reads that, on an auth error, refreshes-once-then-retries (guarded by a `retried` flag) or bails to /auth without applying empty data; the thrown-error catch gets the same treatment. Tests: src/lib/supabaseAuthError.test.ts (13 cases incl. the expired-token refresh-success/refresh-fail/redirect/throw/already-on-auth paths). tsc clean; eslint 0 errors on new files; full vitest suite 883 passing.

userStories.title: Fix SessionTimeoutWarning false 'session expiring' popups on desktop
userStories.description: As a desktop user, I get confusing 'Session Expiring Soon' dialogs at odd times. The component polls session.expires_at against a fixed window while the SDK auto-refreshes the token.
acceptanceCriteria: Drive the warning off onAuthStateChange (TOKEN_REFRESHED / SIGNED_OUT) instead of polling expires_at.
Only warn when a refresh actually fails (refresh token expired).
Remove or make meaningful the manual refreshSession() on 'Extend'. tsc clean; adjust a test.
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-06-02 (web): Rewrote SessionTimeoutWarning to be event-driven instead of polling expires_at. Old version polled every 60s and warned in a fixed 5-min pre-expiry window even though the client runs autoRefreshToken:true — so it fired on healthy sessions (the 'odd times' desktop popups). Now subscribes to supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange: TOKEN_REFRESHED/SIGNED_IN hide the warning (session healthy); only an UNEXPECTED SIGNED_OUT (not user-initiated, not already on /auth) surfaces a 'Session Expired — sign in again' dialog. Removed the meaningless manual refreshSession() 'Extend' button (post-SIGNED_OUT the refresh token is already gone) in favor of a 'Sign In Again' action that routes to /auth with redirect. NOTE: the component is currently not mounted anywhere in src/ (grep-confirmed), so this is a correctness/preventative fix — re-mounting it is a separate product decision. tsc clean; eslint clean.

userStories.title: Add missing RLS DELETE/INSERT policies on recent tables
userStories.description: As a user, deleting/creating my own rows in newer tables silently no-ops because RLS is enabled without the matching policy. CLAUDE.md requires SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: Add household/owner-scoped DELETE policies to household_preferences, kid_growth_events, insights_seasonal_recall (or document as append-only).
Add an INSERT policy to insights_seasonal_recall if any client writes to it (else document service-role-only).
Add WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id) to picky_win_preferences UPDATE policy for parity with user_preferences.
Additive, backward-compatible migrations only.
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-06-02 (additive migration 20260602000000_rls_policy_gaps.sql): closed RLS coverage gaps on the 2026-05-20 batch. Added household-scoped DELETE policies to household_preferences and insights_seasonal_recall, and a member-scoped DELETE to kid_growth_events (mirrors their existing SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE patterns). insights_seasonal_recall INSERT stays service-role-only (cron-populated — already documented on the table; client only dismisses via UPDATE). Added WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id) to the picky_win_preferences UPDATE policy (was USING-only; WITH CHECK blocks reassigning a row to another user_id) for parity with user_preferences. auto_restock_blocklist (SELECT/INSERT/DELETE) and kid_allergen_change_log (append-only, documented) were already correct. All statements idempotent (DROP POLICY IF EXISTS before CREATE); new DELETE policies only BROADEN access so older clients are unaffected. NOTE: applies on prod via the same deploy as US-323.

userStories.title: Verify backward-compat of handle_new_user re-raise + user_profiles drop ordering
userStories.description: As the DB owner, I need to confirm the recent signup-path migrations cannot break production signups. handle_new_user re-raises on ANY error inside the auth.users insert transaction and inserts a household with no ON CONFLICT; the user_profiles scaffold drop runs the day after the restore with no verification gate.
acceptanceCriteria: On the live DB, confirm handle_new_user no longer references user_profiles before relying on 20260513000000_drop_user_profiles_scaffold.sql.
Make household/household_members inserts in restore_handle_new_user idempotent (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING / existence check).
Consider not re-raising for non-critical secondary inserts so the auth user is still created.
Verify a fresh signup succeeds end-to-end (web + iOS) after any change.
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-06-02 (migration 20260602000001_idempotent_handle_new_user.sql). AC1 verified by inspection: the restored handle_new_user() (20260512000000) references ONLY profiles/households/household_members — NOT user_profiles — so the 20260513000000 scaffold drop cannot break the trigger; this migration re-asserts that correct body. AC2: made the household + parent-membership inserts idempotent — they now run ONLY when the user has no household_members row (the prior body created a fresh household every time it fired, and since households has a uuid PK the INSERT never conflicted -> duplicate households on a re-fired/retried signup), plus ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING on household_members. AC3 (re-raise): kept the loud re-raise deliberately — the catch-all swallow in the hijacked function is exactly what hid the 3-month no-households outage; with the idempotency guard the realistic failure mode (retried signup -> duplicate) is gone, so the handler now only catches genuinely unexpected errors (which we WANT surfaced), and a profile-without-household is still recoverable via the 20260512000000 backfill. CREATE OR REPLACE is additive (same signature/trigger). End-to-end fresh-signup verification on live web+iOS rides the prod deploy (US-323).

userStories.title: Web: optimistic-update rollback + error toasts on entity mutations
userStories.description: As a desktop user, when an edit fails server-side it still appears to succeed then silently reverts. update* mutations apply local state inside the .then regardless of error and give no feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Apply optimistic state immediately (before the await) and roll back on error.
On error, toast.error and restore prior state; at minimum guard the setState with if (!error).
Cover KidsContext, GroceryContext, RecipesContext, FoodsContext, PlanContext. tsc clean; failure-path tests.
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-06-02 (web): optimistic-update rollback + error toasts across all five entity contexts. Root bug: update*/delete* applied local state inside the Supabase .then REGARDLESS of error, so a server-rejected edit looked saved then silently reverted on the next realtime sync. New src/lib/optimisticMutation.ts runOptimisticMutation(setState, optimistic, serverCall, {logLabel, toastMessage}): applies the optimistic change immediately (snapshot captured via the functional updater — reliable because the awaited network call gives React time to flush), and on error rolls back to the snapshot + toast.error; auth errors route to handleSupabaseAuthError (US-316) instead of a generic toast. Applied to FoodsContext (updateFood/deleteFood/updateFoods/deleteFoods), KidsContext (updateKid/deleteKid, preserving active-kid fixup), GroceryContext (updateGroceryItem/deleteGroceryItem/deleteGroceryItems/toggleGroceryItem/clearCheckedGroceryItems — the last two now read current state from the closure rather than firing a side-effect inside a setState updater), RecipesContext (updateRecipe/deleteRecipe), PlanContext (updatePlanEntry/deleteWeekPlan). Tests: src/lib/optimisticMutation.test.ts (success keeps state; non-auth error rolls back + toasts; auth error rolls back WITHOUT toast; default message). tsc clean; eslint 0 errors on changed files; full vitest 883 passing (incl. existing AppContext grocery toggle/clear tests).

userStories.title: Wire actual vision into identify-food-image
userStories.description: As a user of in-app food-photo identification, identify-food-image currently ignores the image (comment: 'vision not yet supported') and sends only a text prompt, so results are guesses. AIServiceV2 now supports image messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Pass imageBase64 to AIServiceV2 as an AIMessage.images part instead of the placeholder text.
Keep the existing { success, foodData } response shape and fields.
Verify with deno check and a real image. Keep its shape distinct from identify-product (US-311).
userStories.notes: DONE 2026-06-02: Wired real vision into supabase/functions/identify-food-image/index.ts. It previously (a) passed only a text prompt with a 'vision not yet supported' placeholder AND (b) called generateContent() with a bare string (wrong signature). Now sends the base64 image as an AIMessage.images part (media-type sniffed from the base64 header) via the proper AIRequest, matching the working recognize-fridge-contents pattern. Response shape unchanged — { success, foodData } with name/variety/varietyOptions/category/confidence/description/servingSize/quantity/servingSizeOptions — so existing web+iOS callers are unaffected, and it stays distinct from identify-product (US-311)'s flat product/aisle shape. Added imageBase64 validation (400) and ```json fence stripping. Verified with deno lint (full `deno check` needs network for the esm.sh import; runs in CI). Real-image on-device check rides the functions deploy (US-323).

userStories.title: Swift hardening: remove crash-prone force-unwrap and add weak self

acceptanceCriteria: GroceryView.swift:687 — replace droppedFoods.first!.name with droppedFoods.first?.name ?? "1 item".
StoreKitService.swift:181-192 — add [weak self] to the detached transaction-listener Task (singleton, so defensive/parity).
