acceptanceCriteria: Replayed inserts use upsert/onConflict on id (or pre-check existence) so a row that already landed doesn't 409
A failed/duplicate mutation is quarantined or skipped rather than permanently head-of-line-blocking the queue
Test: enqueue an insert whose row already exists server-side; sync drains the queue without permanently stalling
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Stability). US-150 built the durable queue; this is the conflict/head-of-line-blocking gap it left. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): replay() now upserts inserts with onConflict:id (idempotent — an already-landed row refreshes instead of 409-ing); syncPendingMutations counts per-mutation attemptCount (new defaulted @Model field, lightweight-migrated) and quarantines (drops + Sentry-reports) a mutation after maxReplayAttempts(5) so a poison row cant permanently head-of-line block the queue; transient failures still break to preserve ordering. AC3 server-side-exists drain test needs a mocked Supabase client (integration harness) — logic verified by source audit.

userStories.title: iOS Perf: clearCheckedGroceryItems fires N serial deletes instead of one bulk delete
userStories.description: As a user clearing a finished list, I don't want 30 serial network round-trips. clearCheckedGroceryItems loops `for id in checkedIds { try await dataService.deleteGroceryItem(id) }` (AppState.swift:1218-1221), one HTTP request per item. DataService already exposes bulkDeleteGroceryItems (DataService.swift:401-407) using a single .in('id', ids) delete, and the sibling moveCheckedToPantry path was already migrated to it (AppState.swift:1345, per US-365) — clearCheckedGroceryItems was missed. A 40-item clear is 40 sequential requests, slow and partial-failure-prone.
acceptanceCriteria: clearCheckedGroceryItems calls dataService.bulkDeleteGroceryItems(Array(checkedIds)) in one round-trip
Rollback restores the full snapshot on failure (no half-deleted server state)
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Performance). Same N+1 pattern US-334/US-365 already fixed elsewhere; call site AppState.swift:1218-1221 was overlooked. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): clearCheckedGroceryItems now calls dataService.bulkDeleteGroceryItems(Array(checkedIds)) in one round-trip instead of N serial deletes; existing rollback restores the full checked + source snapshot on failure.

userStories.title: iOS Perf: 'CachedAsyncImage' has no shared cache/eviction — thumbnails refetch on every scroll
userStories.description: As a user scrolling Recipes, I don't want every thumbnail re-downloaded each time a cell reappears. CachedAsyncImage is a thin wrapper over SwiftUI's AsyncImage (CachedAsyncImage.swift:11-37) which only keeps a transient cache tied to view lifetime — scrolling a RecipesView list re-issues the network request for each off-then-on-screen row, and there is no URLCache configuration or memory/disk eviction policy. RecipeThumbnail/AvatarView inherit the same behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Images are served from a configured URLCache (or a small NSCache-backed loader) so a re-appearing row hits cache, not network
Cache has a bounded memory + disk capacity so it can't grow unbounded
Recipe list scrolling does not re-issue network requests for already-loaded thumbnails (verify via a request counter in tests)
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Performance). The name 'CachedAsyncImage' is misleading — no real caching/eviction exists. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): rewrote CachedAsyncImage to use a real cache — new @MainActor ThumbnailImageCache (NSCache memory layer + URLSession with a bounded URLCache: 25MB mem / 150MB disk) and ThumbnailImageLoader; the view loads via .task(id:url) so a re-appearing same-URL row hits cache, not network. networkFetchCount test hook + ThumbnailImageCacheTests (cache hit does not refetch).

userStories.title: iOS Concurrency: WidgetSnapshot/WatchConnectivity triple-debounce re-runs full O(n) snapshot rebuilds
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the snapshot rebuild coalesced and race-free. WidgetSnapshot stores a static mutable pendingWorkItem mutated from write()/writeImmediately() (WidgetSnapshot.swift:33,55-74). Combined with the 500ms AppState combineLatest debounce (AppState.swift:163-174) AND the watch's own 1.5s debounce (WatchConnectivityService.swift:47-54), a burst of mutations chains three timers and rebuilds the full snapshot payload (iterating planEntries/foods/recipes/groceryItems, AppState.swift:177-234) on each fire on the main thread.
acceptanceCriteria: Snapshot rebuild is coalesced to a single pass per debounce window rather than re-run per chained timer
Debounce timer state is confirmed MainActor-isolated (or moved to an actor) with no cross-thread mutation
Rapid sequential grocery toggles produce at most one widget timeline reload per debounce window
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Stability/Perf). Correctness holds via @MainActor today, but the triple-debounce + full-rescan is wasteful on the hot toggle path (toggleGroceryItem at AppState.swift:1112). | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): refreshWidgetSnapshot now calls WidgetSnapshot.writeImmediately instead of write — the AppState Combine pipeline already debounces the whole O(n) rebuild by 500ms, so the second chained 0.5s timer was redundant; now a single coalesced pass per debounce window -> one reloadTimelines. Confirmed WidgetSnapshot and WatchConnectivityService debounce state are both @MainActor-isolated (no cross-thread mutation).

userStories.title: iOS Pantry Scan: barcode scan of an existing pantry item creates a duplicate (no merge)
userStories.description: As a parent re-scanning an item I already track, I want the scan to update my existing pantry entry, not create a second copy. ScannedProductView.addFood() (ScannedProductView.swift:149-181) always constructs a new Food with a fresh UUID and calls appState.addFood — it never looks up an existing food by barcode or name first, so re-scanning a tracked product silently duplicates the row. US-364 (consistent pantry dedup) is still PENDING and the scan entry point is one of the easiest ways to hit this.
acceptanceCriteria: Before inserting, ScannedProductView checks appState.foods for a barcode match (then a case-insensitive name match) and, on hit, offers 'Update existing' vs 'Add new'
Choosing update increments/refreshes the existing food rather than inserting a new row
Manual entry path (lookupFailed) also dedupes on name
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Functionality). Verified ScannedProductView.swift:168-181 builds Food(id: UUID()...) unconditionally; no dedup call on the scan path. Related to PENDING US-364. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): ScannedProductView.addFood now checks appState.existingPantryFood(name:barcode:) and, on hit, shows an Update-existing vs Add-new confirmationDialog instead of unconditionally inserting; Update increments via incrementFoodQuantity. Manual/lookup-failed path dedupes on barcode-then-name through the same check.

userStories.title: iOS Receipt Scan: matched-existing-food indicator is cosmetic; save still inserts duplicates
userStories.description: As a user importing a receipt, I want items the app already recognizes in my pantry to merge, not duplicate. ScanReceiptSheet computes matchedFoodId via ReceiptScanService.matchExistingFood and shows a blue 'will match' icon (ScanReceiptSheet.swift:195-199, 321-325), but save() (ScanReceiptSheet.swift:239-261) maps every accepted row to a brand-new Food(id: UUID()) and calls bulkInsertFoods — matchedFoodId is never consulted, so 'matched' items become duplicates.
acceptanceCriteria: save() routes rows with a non-nil matchedFoodId to an update/merge of the existing food instead of an insert
Only unmatched rows are bulk-inserted as new foods
The 'will match an existing pantry food' icon reflects the action actually taken
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Functionality). matchExistingFood return value is stored on ReviewRow.matchedFoodId and rendered (ScanReceiptSheet.swift:321) but unused in save(). Related to PENDING US-364. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): ScanReceiptSheet.save now routes rows with a non-nil matchedFoodId to incrementFoodQuantity(notify:false) (merge) and only bulk-inserts unmatched rows; the matched-existing icon now reflects the real action; analytics count = merged + inserted.

userStories.title: iOS Scanner: no manual barcode entry / rescan when lookup fails or won't scan
userStories.description: As a user with a damaged or unscannable barcode, I want to type the digits or try again without backing all the way out. UnifiedScannerView barcode mode only returns on a successful scan and has no 'enter manually' affordance; when the lookup fails, ScannedProductView shows 'Product not found, enter details manually' (ScannedProductView.swift:34-43) but provides no button to re-open the scanner or re-run the lookup — the user must Cancel and restart the whole flow.
acceptanceCriteria: ScannedProductView shows a 'Scan again' / 'Retry lookup' action on lookupFailed
UnifiedScannerView (or ScannedProductView) offers a 'Type barcode' fallback that feeds the same lookup pipeline
Retry re-enters loading state and re-calls BarcodeService.lookup
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Ease-of-use). ScannedProductView.swift:132-147 sets lookupFailed but renders no retry/rescan control; UnifiedScannerView.swift:204-213 only emits on success. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): ScannedProductView lookupFailed now shows a Retry-lookup section — a numberPad Type barcode field (currentBarcode, seeded from the scanned value) + Retry lookup button that re-enters loading and re-calls BarcodeService.lookup via the same lookupBarcode() pipeline; the saved Food + dedup use the effective (possibly corrected) barcode.

userStories.title: iOS Scanner: modern DataScanner barcode mode has no torch toggle for dark aisles
userStories.description: As a user scanning in a dim pantry or low-lit aisle, I want a flashlight toggle. The legacy fallback BarcodeScannerView has a torch button (BarcodeScannerView.swift:39-46), but the primary iOS-17 UnifiedScannerView.modernScanner toolbar (UnifiedScannerView.swift:103-120) only has Cancel/Done — there is no torch control on the path most users actually hit, making barcodes in low light hard to read.
acceptanceCriteria: UnifiedScannerView modern path exposes a torch toggle in the toolbar or overlay
Torch state is reset/turned off on dismiss
Torch control is hidden when the device has no torch
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (UX). Compared UnifiedScannerView.swift:103-120 (no torch) against BarcodeScannerView.swift:39-46 (has torch) — parity gap on the preferred scanner. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): UnifiedScannerView modern path adds a toolbar torch toggle (topBarTrailing), hidden when AVCaptureDevice has no torch; setTorch via lockForConfiguration; torch turned off onDisappear. import AVFoundation added.

userStories.title: iOS Voice Add: mic/speech denial not surfaced until first mic tap; no Settings deep-link
userStories.description: As a user who previously denied microphone or speech access, I want the Voice Add sheet to tell me upfront and point me to Settings. VoiceAddGrocerySheet's .task calls voice.requestAuthorization() and discards the result (VoiceAddGrocerySheet.swift:46-48); the denied state only appears as a transient toast after the user taps the mic and start() fails (toggleRecording, line 211). There is no persistent 'enable in Settings' affordance like BarcodeScannerView's permission-denied view (BarcodeScannerView.swift:97-120).
acceptanceCriteria: On appear, if authorization is .micDenied/.speechDenied/.bothDenied, the sheet shows a persistent explanatory state with an 'Open Settings' button (UIApplication.openSettingsURLString)
The mic button is disabled or routes to the Settings prompt when permission is denied
Authorized users see no change
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (UX). requestAuthorization result discarded at VoiceAddGrocerySheet.swift:47; VoiceInputService already returns granular .micDenied/.speechDenied/.bothDenied (VoiceInputService.swift:83-88) but the sheet never reads it proactively. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): VoiceAddGrocerySheet stores the requestAuthorization() result on appear; when micDenied/speechDenied/bothDenied it shows a persistent permissionDeniedView (mic.slash + explanation + Open Settings deep-link via UIApplication.openSettingsURLString) and hides the mic controls entirely; authorized users unchanged. import UIKit added.

userStories.title: iOS Voice Add: live waveform animation ignores Reduce Motion
userStories.description: As a motion-sensitive user, I want the voice waveform to not animate when Reduce Motion is on. VoiceAddGrocerySheet's WaveformView animates each bar's height with .easeInOut on every input-level change (VoiceAddGrocerySheet.swift:299) with no accessibilityReduceMotion gate, unlike sibling scanner/shopping views that already gate their animations (BarcodeScannerView ScanLineView at BarcodeScannerView.swift:145-159, ShoppingModeView at lines 333-348).
acceptanceCriteria: WaveformView reads @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) and renders static bars (or a non-animated level meter) when enabled
Recording state is still conveyed without motion (color/label)
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Accessibility). VoiceAddGrocerySheet.swift:278-303 WaveformView has no reduceMotion check; the recording-button pulse (line 163-164) is likewise ungated. Related to PENDING US-369. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): WaveformView reads @Environment(accessibilityReduceMotion) and renders static (non-level-reactive, non-animated) bars when enabled; recording state still conveyed via green color + accessibilityLabel. The record-button pulse scaleEffect/animation is also gated on reduceMotion in VoiceAddGrocerySheet.

userStories.title: iOS Scanner: grocery-list OCR transcript discarded on Cancel with no data-loss guard
userStories.description: As a user who has slowly scanned a long handwritten list line-by-line, I want a warning before I lose it. In UnifiedScannerView grocery-list mode the Cancel toolbar button dismisses immediately (UnifiedScannerView.swift:104-109) and discards collectedText even when many lines have been recognized, while Done is the only path that emits them. There's no confirmation when collectedText is non-empty, so an accidental Cancel throws away accumulated scan work.
acceptanceCriteria: In groceryList mode, tapping Cancel when collectedText is non-empty shows a confirmation ('Discard N recognized lines?')
Barcode mode and empty-list cancel dismiss without a prompt
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (UX). UnifiedScannerView.swift:104-109 dismisses unconditionally; collectedText (line 53) is only emitted via the Done button at line 112-118. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): UnifiedScannerView Cancel in groceryList mode with non-empty collectedText now shows a confirmationDialog ("Discard N recognized line(s)?" Discard/Keep scanning); barcode mode and empty list dismiss without a prompt.

userStories.title: iOS AI Coach: surface edge failures with retry instead of masking as a canned reply
userStories.description: As a parent, I want to know when the AI Coach failed so I can retry, not get a generic scripted answer presented as the real AI. AICoachService.sendMessage (AICoachService.swift:64-74) swallows every edge error (network/auth/5xx) and appends a keyword-matched canned response as an .assistant ChatMessage indistinguishable from a true model reply; the failure is only print()'d under #if DEBUG. The user sees a confident but generic answer and has no retry affordance.
acceptanceCriteria: On edge-call failure, the failed turn is visibly marked as an error (e.g. an error bubble or banner), not a silent canned answer
A 'Retry' affordance re-sends the last user message without re-typing
Canned fallback is only used when offline (network.isConnected == false) or clearly labeled as offline tips, not for transient 5xx
Failures are logged to Sentry, not only DEBUG print
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Stability). AICoachView.swift has an offline banner (line 62) but the service still fabricates a fallback answer on any failure; no message-level error state or retry exists in the chat UI. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): AICoachService now logs edge failures to Sentry; on an ONLINE failure it appends an isError ChatMessage (orange error bubble + Retry button in ChatBubble) instead of a canned answer; the canned fallback is used only when offline and is clearly labeled with a 📴 offline prefix. retryLastMessage() re-sends the stored last user prompt. Added isError to ChatMessage.

userStories.title: iOS AI Meal Plan: add offline guard + disable generate while in-flight to prevent duplicate calls
userStories.description: As a parent, I want the AI plan screen to tell me I'm offline and to stop me firing duplicate generations. AIMealPlanView has no NetworkMonitor check (unlike AICoachView.swift:62) so offline taps spin for 60s then fall back silently; and Generate/Regenerate/'Plan from fridge' (AIMealPlanView.swift:50-66,142-148,163-165) are not gated on a network state and 'Add All' (line 119) is re-tappable, so a second tap re-runs generateSuggestions or re-adds entries.
acceptanceCriteria: An offline banner appears when NetworkMonitor.shared.isConnected is false, matching AICoachView
Generate / Regenerate buttons are disabled (or no-op) while aiService.isLoading
'Add All to Plan' is disabled while the add loop runs to prevent duplicate plan entries
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (UX). EdgeFunctions.invoke uses a 60s timeoutInterval (EdgeFunctions.swift:73), so an offline generate looks frozen for a full minute before the local fallback in AIMealService.swift:219 kicks in. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): AIMealPlanView shows an offline banner (matching AICoachView) when NetworkMonitor.shared.isConnected is false; Generate/fridge/Regenerate disabled while aiService.isLoading or offline; Add All guarded by isAddingAll (set+defer) so a double-tap cant duplicate entries.

userStories.title: iOS AI Meal Plan: skip suggestions with no foodId instead of creating orphan plan entries
userStories.description: As a parent, I want every meal I add from AI suggestions to reference a real food. AIMealPlanView.addSuggestionToPlan (AIMealPlanView.swift:225) does `let foodId = suggestion.foodId ?? ''` and creates a PlanEntry with an empty foodId when the LLM returns a name but no matched food_id (MealSuggestion.foodId is optional, AIMealService.swift:21). 'Add All' (line 238-244) adds these blindly, producing plan entries that resolve to 'Unknown' and break pantry/grocery/budget cross-references downstream.
acceptanceCriteria: Suggestions with a nil/empty foodId either resolve to a created/matched Food or are not written as a PlanEntry with empty foodId
When a food can't be resolved, the user is told (toast or inline) rather than silently adding an orphan entry
'Add All' reports an accurate count of entries actually added vs skipped
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Functionality). StarterMealPlanService.resolveFood (StarterMealPlanService.swift:174) auto-creates a Food when the name is missing; the AI path has no equivalent and just writes foodId == ''. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): AIMealPlanView.resolveFoodId resolves a suggestion to a real Food (existing id -> name match -> create), so no PlanEntry is written with an empty foodId; single-add surfaces a toast on failure; Add All now counts added vs skipped and reports accurately, dismissing only when something was added.

userStories.title: iOS Food Chaining: generate real property-based chains and let users save them
userStories.description: As a parent doing feeding therapy, I want a food chain built from the real similarity between my safe food and the target, and I want to keep it. FoodChainingView.generateChain (FoodChainingView.swift:144-199) emits hardcoded placeholder steps ('Modified <name>', 'Mixed plate', 'Similar <category>') driven only by whether categories match; it ignores texture/flavor/color/temperature despite the on-screen promise (lines 22-24). isGenerating is set true/false synchronously (lines 146,198) so the spinner never shows, and chainSteps is @State only (line 9) — every chain is lost on dismiss with no save/share.
acceptanceCriteria: Chain steps are derived from actual shared/adjacent food properties (category plus at least texture or flavor) rather than fixed template strings
Generated chains can be saved or added to the plan/notes so they persist beyond the view's lifetime
A loading state is shown only if generation is actually async, otherwise the spinner code is removed
Empty/insufficient-foods state guides the user when no safe or try-bite foods exist
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Functionality). The view never references RecipeMatcher or any property-similarity helper; it's effectively a static explainer with cosmetic steps. No persistence path exists for chains.

userStories.title: iOS Edge calls: add retry-on-transient + cancellable timeout to EdgeFunctions.invoke
userStories.description: As a parent on flaky cellular, I want AI/recipe calls to retry a transient failure rather than immediately dropping to a degraded fallback. EdgeFunctions.invokeRaw (EdgeFunctions.swift:60-82) does a single URLSession attempt with a fixed 60s timeoutInterval, no retry on 502/503/504 or URLError.timedOut, and no Task-cancellation token — so a transient Kong/Coolify hiccup forces AIMealService/RecipeImportService/AICoachService onto fallbacks or surfaces an error, and a cancelled view can't abort the request.
acceptanceCriteria: Transient failures (502/503/504, connection lost, timed out) are retried with backoff at least once before failing
The request honors Task cancellation so dismissing the view aborts in-flight work
Retry/timeout behavior is centralized in EdgeFunctions so all AI callers benefit
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Stability). TonightModeService wraps its own 1.5s soft timeout (TonightModeService.swift:294) but that pattern isn't reused; other AI calls just use the raw 60s URLSession with no retry. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): EdgeFunctions.invokeRaw now retries transient failures (502/503/504 + URLError timedOut/networkConnectionLost/cannotConnectToHost/cannotFindHost/dnsLookupFailed/notConnectedToInternet) once with exponential backoff (0.5s,1s); checks Task.checkCancellation() before each attempt and propagates CancellationError so dismissing a view aborts in-flight work; centralized so all AI callers benefit. 4xx/other 5xx surface immediately.

userStories.title: iOS AI Coach: make the in-flight request cancellable on reset and view dismiss
userStories.description: As a parent, I want tapping Reset (or leaving the screen) while the coach is thinking to actually stop the request. AICoachView.sendMessage (AICoachView.swift:171-174) fires an unstructured Task with no stored handle, and the toolbar Reset button (lines 153-160) calls coachService.clearChat() without cancelling the live sendMessage Task — so a late response appends onto the freshly-cleared conversation and isLoading can flip back on after reset.
acceptanceCriteria: The active send Task is retained and cancelled by clearChat() / Reset
A response that arrives after reset/cancel is discarded, not appended
isLoading is reset correctly when a send is cancelled
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Performance/Correctness). AICoachService.isLoading is a shared @Published on a singleton (AICoachService.swift:5,8); a stale completion can re-append a ChatMessage and reactivate the typing indicator after reset. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): AICoachService retains the send as a cancellable Task (new send()/retryLastMessage route through it); clearChat() cancels it + resets isLoading; a response arriving after cancel/reset is discarded via Task.isCancelled checks; CancellationError swallowed cleanly. View calls the non-async send/retry.

userStories.title: iOS Tonight Cook: fix step parser so decimals/abbreviations don't split mid-step
userStories.description: As a parent cooking hands-free, I want each cook step to be a real step, not a fragment. TonightCookSheet.parseSteps (TonightCookSheet.swift:222-246) falls back to splitting on '. ' when no bullet/numbered lines are found, which fractures instructions like 'Add 1.5 cups...' or 'Preheat to 350. Mr. Smith...' into nonsense steps, and the step text drives the AVSpeech voice cue (line 219).
acceptanceCriteria: Sentence-split fallback does not break on decimals (e.g. '1.5'), abbreviations, or 'No.'
Single-blob instructions with no delimiters render as one step rather than over-segmenting
Voice cue reads the corrected step text
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Functionality). The bullet/number regex path (line 226) is fine; only the naive .components(separatedBy: '. ') fallback (line 243) is fragile and feeds both the on-screen step and the spoken cue. Related to PENDING US-359. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): TonightCookSheet.parseSteps fallback replaced the naive .components(separatedBy: ". ") with NSString.enumerateSubstrings(.bySentences), which doesnt split on decimals/abbreviations; a single-blob instruction returns [raw] (one step). Same text feeds the on-screen step and the spoken cue.

userStories.title: iOS Notifications: preferences never schedule — toggles/times persist but no reminders fire
userStories.description: As a parent, I want my notification toggles and daily times to actually deliver reminders. NotificationSettingsView persists per-topic enabled/hour/minute to UserDefaults (SettingsView.swift:382-410) and only ever calls fireTestNotification (SettingsView.swift:440), but scheduleMealReminders() and scheduleGroceryReminder() (NotificationService.swift:84,123) are defined and NEVER called anywhere. No code reads the Topic prefs to schedule UNCalendarNotificationTriggers. Enabling 'Meal-plan reminders' at a chosen time results in zero scheduled notifications.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggling a topic on schedules its UNCalendarNotificationTrigger using the persisted hour/minute; toggling off removes it
Changing the DatePicker time reschedules that topic's notification
mealReminders/groceryReady/aiSummary respect supportsDailyTime and persisted times instead of hardcoded 8/12/18
Disabling the master toggle cancels all scheduled topic notifications
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Functionality — core feature non-functional). scheduleMealReminders is hardcoded to 8/12/18 and orphaned; verified by grep that neither scheduler is called anywhere. The per-topic settings UI (US-239) only fires test pings. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): added scheduleTopic/cancelTopic/cancelAllTopics/rescheduleAllTopics on NotificationService using persisted enabled/hour/minute per Topic (UNCalendarNotificationTrigger, daily repeat). SettingsView wires topic toggle + DatePicker onChange to (re)schedule/cancel; master toggle off cancels all topics; on launch EatPalApp reschedules. Removed orphaned hardcoded 8/12/18 scheduleMealReminders/cancelMealReminders. Swift build = local Xcode.

userStories.title: iOS Notifications: mute-all is bypassed — mutedUntil gate is checked at no scheduling site
userStories.description: As a parent who tapped 'Mute all for 24h', I want notifications actually suppressed for that window. mutedUntil is written by mute(forSeconds:) (NotificationService.swift:254) and read only by the Settings UI chip (SettingsView.swift:272,313). No scheduling/delivery path consults mutedUntil, and the AppDelegate willPresent handler (EatPalApp.swift:85-90) always returns [.banner,.sound,.badge]. mute() does a one-time removeAllPendingNotificationRequests but any later reschedule or remote push ignores the mute window.
acceptanceCriteria: Scheduling helpers skip adding requests while mutedUntil is in the future
willPresent suppresses foreground banners while muted
Notifications resume automatically once mutedUntil passes
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Functionality). The mute UX implies a real silence window but only clears the (currently never-populated) pending queue once. Pairs with US-403. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): scheduleTopic skips adding while mutedUntil>now; AppDelegate willPresent returns [] while muted (foreground suppression); unmute() + launch rescheduleAllTopics() resume reminders automatically once the window passes. Swift build = local Xcode.

userStories.title: iOS Deep links: only import URLs route — pantry/quiz/kid/scanner/universal links dead-end on Planner
userStories.description: As a user tapping a widget, Siri result, or universal link, I want to land on the right screen. DeepLinkHandler parses 10+ destinations (DeepLinkHandler.swift:57-104,209-228) but the only consumer, EatPalApp.swift:37-48, handles ONLY .groceryImport and .recipeImport. MainTabView never observes activeDestination. So eatpal://pantry, ://quiz, ://kid?id=, ://scanner, ://settings, ://food-chaining, ://dashboard, ://meal-plan and all https://tryeatpal.com/app/* links set activeDestination but switch no tab — the user stays on the default Planner.
acceptanceCriteria: Each navigation Destination switches MainTabView to the correct tab (or pushes the right view) when activeDestination changes
.kidProfile(id:) opens that child; .quiz/.scanner/.foodChaining/.settings open their screens
Universal links under /app/* route identically to the custom scheme
Destination is cleared after routing so the same link re-triggers next time
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Functionality). Confirmed by grep: activeDestination is consumed only for import drains; navigation cases are parsed then discarded. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): MainTabView now consumes deepLinkHandler.activeDestination, switching tab and (for More sub-screens) driving a morePath via MoreRoute + .navigationDestination in MoreView; kidProfile/quiz/foodChaining/settings/progress/dashboard push their real screens; universal /app/* gains food-chaining/settings/progress parity; destination cleared after routing so the same link re-triggers. scanner routes to Pantry (scan entry point); mealPlan deep-date not yet honored (tab-level only). Swift build = local Xcode.

userStories.title: iOS Notifications: tapping a notification routes nowhere (AppDelegate only prints category)
userStories.description: As a parent who taps a meal/grocery/badge notification, I want to land on the relevant screen. The didReceive response handler (EatPalApp.swift:93-99) only prints categoryIdentifier and does nothing — it never forwards to DeepLinkHandler or sets a destination. Combined with the dead deep-link routing, every notification tap just opens the app to the default tab.
acceptanceCriteria: didReceive maps the notification categoryIdentifier (MEAL_REMINDER/GROCERY_REMINDER/topic) to a DeepLinkHandler.Destination
Tapping a grocery reminder opens Grocery; a meal reminder opens Planner; a badge/streak opens Progress
Routing reuses the same destination-consumption path as deep links
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (UX). Pairs with US-405; once destinations are consumed, notification taps should feed the same path. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): AppDelegate didReceive maps categoryIdentifier -> DeepLinkHandler.routeFromNotification (MEALREMINDERS->Planner, GROCERY*->Grocery, EXPIRINGFOOD->Pantry, AISUMMARY->Dashboard, STREAKMILESTONE/TRYBITE->Progress), reusing the same activeDestination consumption path as deep links. Added .progress Destination. Swift build = local Xcode.

userStories.title: iOS Watch: complications read UserDefaults.standard not App Group, so they never show real data
userStories.description: As a watch user, I want the Tonight/Grocery complications to reflect my real plan. WatchSessionStore writes the snapshot to UserDefaults.standard (WatchSessionStore.swift:76) and the complication SnapshotLoader.load() reads UserDefaults.standard (EatPalWatchComplications.swift:26). On watchOS the app and the WidgetKit complication are separate processes that do not share standard defaults — only UserDefaults(suiteName:) is shared (as the iPhone widget correctly does). So TonightProvider/GroceryProvider always fall back to .empty/placeholder in production, contradicting the 'both read the same cached snapshot' comment (EatPalWatchComplications.swift:9-11).
acceptanceCriteria: WatchSessionStore caches the snapshot to a shared UserDefaults(suiteName:) App Group
Complication SnapshotLoader reads from the same suite
Complications display real tonight-dinner and grocery-remaining values after the iPhone pushes a snapshot
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Functionality). Verified both write (line 76) and read (line 26) use .standard; the iPhone widget uses a group suite, proving the mismatch is watch-side only. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): added shared WatchSnapshotStore (Shared/WatchSnapshot.swift) using UserDefaults(suiteName: group.com.eatpal.app) with .standard fallback; WatchSessionStore.cache/load and the complication SnapshotLoader both go through it, so the iPhone-pushed snapshot is read by the complication. NOTE: the App Group entitlement must be added to the watch app + complication extension targets in Xcode for the cross-process read to work (code is ready).

userStories.title: iOS Widget: home/Lock-Screen widgets set no widgetURL — taps can't deep-link
userStories.description: As a user, I want tapping the meal/grocery widget to open the right screen. EatPalWidget.swift contains no widgetURL anywhere, so the StaticConfiguration widgets and accessory views open the app to its default tab. The empty cell even reads 'Open EatPal to plan' yet can't route to the planner. The Live Activity sets a widgetURL, showing the API is known but not applied to the widgets.
acceptanceCriteria: Meal widget views apply .widgetURL(eatpal://meal-plan)
Grocery widget views apply .widgetURL(eatpal://grocery)
Lock-Screen accessory widgets deep-link to their corresponding screen
Routing works end-to-end once deep-link destination consumption is fixed
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (UX). Depends on US-405 to be useful; currently both layers are no-ops. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): MealPlanWidgetView applies .widgetURL — eatpal://meal-plan for meal families, eatpal://grocery (or eatpal://progress when a streak shows) for the circular accessory; fixed GroceryTripLiveActivity from the unrouted eatpal://openGroceryList to eatpal://grocery. Routes end-to-end via US-405 DeepLinkHandler consumption.

userStories.title: iOS Kids: AddKidView swallows save errors and dismisses as if succeeded
userStories.description: As a parent adding a child, I want to know if the save failed. addKid() (KidsView.swift:219-237) sets isSubmitting=true (never reset), calls `try? await appState.addKid(kid)` discarding any error, then dismiss() runs unconditionally. On a network/RLS failure the sheet closes with no toast/alert and the child silently doesn't persist. KidProfileEditorView.save() shares the same try?-then-dismiss pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: addKid surfaces an error toast/alert on failure and keeps the sheet open
isSubmitting is reset in all paths (use defer)
Dismiss happens only on confirmed success
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Stability). KidsView.swift:235 — try? then unconditional dismiss; isSubmitting set at :220 never cleared. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): addKid now uses defer to reset isSubmitting on every path; wraps appState.addKid in do/catch, shows a ToastManager error and keeps the sheet open on failure, dismisses only on confirmed success.

userStories.title: iOS Kids: KidDetailView 'Safe Foods' stat shows global pantry count, not per-child
userStories.description: As a parent viewing a child's profile, I expect the 'Safe Foods' stat to reflect that child. KidDetailView (titled kid.name, KidsView.swift:341) computes `appState.foods.filter(\.isSafe).count` (KidsView.swift:307) — the entire household pantry's safe-food count, identical for every child. It reads as a per-kid metric but isn't scoped to the kid.
acceptanceCriteria: Safe Foods reflects foods safe for this specific child (per kid allergen/safe-food scoping) or the stat is relabeled to make the household scope explicit
Today's Meals stat continues to be kid-scoped
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Functionality). Misleading per-kid data; today's-meals stat is correctly scoped via planEntriesForDate(kidId:), so the inconsistency is visible side-by-side. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): KidDetailView Safe Foods stat now scoped to the child — excludes safe foods whose allergens intersect the kids allergen list (case-insensitive disjoint check); Todays Meals remains kid-scoped via planEntriesForDate(kidId:).

userStories.title: iOS Components: DailyNutritionSummary renders zeroed macro rings as live data
userStories.description: As a parent who logged meals, I want the nutrition summary to show real macros or an honest 'not available' state. NutritionView.swift:135-138 hardcodes dailyCalories/Protein/Carbs/Fat to `return 0` ('Placeholder — needs nutrition data on Food model'), yet when entries are non-empty it renders MacroRingsView with all zeros plus 'N meals logged' (NutritionView.swift:149-159). Users see four empty 0-of-target rings presented as real data.
acceptanceCriteria: When per-food nutrition is unavailable, show a clear 'Nutrition data not available' state instead of zeroed rings
MacroRing value Text uses minimumScaleFactor/lineLimit so 3-4 digit values don't overflow the fixed 56pt ring under Dynamic Type
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (UX). Placeholder shipped as live UI; the fixed-frame ring (NutritionView.swift:104-119, .caption2 no scale) also overflows at large text sizes. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): DailyNutritionSummary shows an explicit Nutrition data not available state (Food has no per-item macros yet) instead of zeroed MacroRings; MacroRing value Text gained lineLimit(1)+minimumScaleFactor(0.5)+frame(maxWidth:48) so 3-4 digit values dont overflow the 56pt ring under Dynamic Type.

userStories.title: iOS Siri: mutation intents don't refresh the widget, so it stays stale after voice actions
userStories.description: As a user logging via Siri without opening the app, I want the widget to reflect it. MarkFoodSafe (FoodAppIntents.swift:110), MarkFoodTryBite (:149), LogMealResult (MealAppIntents.swift:93), Add/BulkAdd grocery (GroceryAppIntents.swift:59,135) mutate via DataService and return, but none call WidgetSnapshot.write/WidgetCenter.reloadTimelines. Since these run with openAppWhenRun=false, AppState's snapshot writer never fires and the grocery-count/tonight-dish widget shows pre-Siri data until the app is next opened.
acceptanceCriteria: Grocery add/bulk-add intents update the App Group snapshot and reload the widget timeline
LogMealResult/MarkFood* refresh the tonight/streak snapshot the widget reads
Widget reflects Siri-driven changes without opening the app
Swift builds
userStories.notes: Audit finding (Functionality). Distinct from US-152 (freshness on AppState mutations) — these background intents bypass AppState entirely. | DONE 2026-06-14 (claude/zen-mccarthy-6xpo2a): extracted WidgetSnapshot.buildPayload (shared by AppState change-stream + intents) and added WidgetSnapshot.rebuildFromServer() (fetches foods/grocery/plan/recipes, builds payload, writeImmediately -> reloads timeline). Called after each background Siri mutation: AddGroceryItem + BulkAddGroceryItems (grocery count), LogMealResult (tonight/meals), MarkFoodSafe + MarkFoodTryBite (pantry-low). Widget now reflects Siri changes without opening the app; AppState.refreshWidgetSnapshot refactored onto the shared builder (identical output).

userStories.title: iOS: Stop silent data loss on Recipe/Kid form saves (handle errors, guard unsaved changes)
userStories.description: As a user editing a recipe or child profile, I want a failed save to keep my work and tell me it failed, so that I never silently lose what I typed.
acceptanceCriteria: EditRecipeView.saveRecipe() (EditRecipeView.swift ~250-296): replace `try?` on updateRecipeWithIngredients with do/catch; only dismiss() on success; on failure reset isSubmitting, keep sheet open, show ToastManager error

AddRecipeView.create (RecipesView.swift ~1454-1484): wrap addRecipe in do/catch, only dismiss on success; if ImageUploadService.upload fails surface a toast and offer save-without-photo instead of silently dropping the image

KidProfileEditorView.save() (KidProfileEditorView.swift ~357-359) and KidDetailView save (KidsView.swift ~352-366): same do/catch + dismiss-on-success-only pattern (mirror AddKidView which already does this correctly)
KidProfileEditorView photo upload (~324-333): surface upload failure instead of silently keeping the stale URL
Add unsaved-changes guard to EditRecipeView, AddRecipeView and KidProfileEditorView: track a dirty flag, apply .interactiveDismissDisabled(isDirty), confirm discard on Cancel/swipe-down
Manual test: force a save failure (airplane mode) and confirm the sheet stays open with an error toast and the form contents intact
userStories.notes: DONE + source-verified (no Xcode in env): all four save paths (EditRecipeView.saveRecipe, AddRecipeView.createRecipe, KidProfileEditorView.save, KidDetailView.save) use do/catch, dismiss only on success, reset isSubmitting via defer, warn on photo-upload failure with save-without-photo; unsaved-changes guards via loadedSnapshot/isDirty + interactiveDismissDisabled + confirmation dialog. Verified bullet-by-bullet against source.

userStories.title: iOS Grocery: surface mutation failures and add undo for destructive clears
userStories.description: As a shopper, I want add/edit/bulk grocery actions to tell me when they fail and let me undo a bulk clear, so that I don't get false-success or lose my list.
acceptanceCriteria: AddGroceryItemView.addItem() and EditGroceryItemView.save() (GroceryView.swift ~1510, ~1636): stop using `try?`+unconditional dismiss(); only dismiss on success, surface inline error otherwise
Drag-and-drop add (GroceryView.swift ~677-716): only fire HapticManager.success() and the success toast when addedCount>0; show an error toast when every add failed (addedCount==0)
bulkSetAisle / bulkDelete (GroceryView.swift ~1106-1120): replace empty `catch {}` with an error toast and keep the selection intact so the user can retry
Clear Completed (GroceryView.swift ~1064-1071): after clearing, show an undo snackbar that restores the cleared items (snapshot before delete)
Normalize minimum-quantity rules across AddGroceryItemView (no floor), EditGroceryItemView (floor 0) and QuickAddGrocerySheet (floor 0.5) so no path can create a 0-quantity item
userStories.notes: DONE + source-verified: Add/Edit grocery dismiss only on success with inline error; drag-drop success haptic/toast only when addedCount>0 else error toast; bulkSetAisle/bulkDelete surface errors and keep selection; Clear Completed snapshots checked items and offers Undo restore. Verified bullet-by-bullet.

userStories.title: iOS Meal Plan: feedback + correct dismiss for copy/clear/delete and AI add-all
userStories.description: As a parent planning meals, I want copy-week, clear-week, delete-entry and AI add-all to confirm success/failure, so that destructive/bulk actions are never silent.
acceptanceCriteria: Copy Week (MealPlanView.swift ~247-259): replace `try?`+dismiss with do/catch; show success/failure toast; don't dismiss the sheet on failure
Clear This Week (MealPlanView.swift ~281-295): surface success/error toasts and add an undo affordance for this bulk destructive action
Remove-from-plan context action (MealPlanView.swift ~712-726): surface delete failures and add an undo toast for the plain (non-recently-made) delete path
AIMealPlanView add-all (AIMealPlanView.swift ~312-337): only dismiss() when skipped==0; on partial success keep the sheet open so the user can retry the remainder
AIMealPlanView addMissingToGrocery (~341-367): show an error toast when added==0; classify grocery aisle via GroceryAisle.classify instead of hardcoding category "snack"
userStories.notes: DONE (this session completed the missing AC): Copy Week / remove-from-plan / AI add-all already correct; added the missing 'Clear This Week' undo — it now snapshots the full week's plan entries before deleteWeekPlan and offers an Undo toast that re-adds them (parity with Clear Completed). Verified.

userStories.title: iOS Pantry: rollback optimistic writes, confirm single delete, make receipt save idempotent
userStories.description: As a user managing my pantry, I want failed quantity/delete edits to roll back with feedback, a confirm on single delete, and receipt imports that can't double-count, so that pantry data stays trustworthy.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-row mutations (PantryView.swift ~187-330: swipe +1, mark safe/unsafe, delete, add-to-grocery, QuantityAdjustSheet, FoodDetailView save): replace `try?` with error handling that toasts on failure and rolls back optimistic state (mirror the bulk helpers that already do this)
Single-item delete (PantryView.swift ~224-229, ~318-323): add an undo toast or confirmation for parity with bulk delete (which shows a 'can't be undone' alert)

ScanReceiptSheet.save() (ScanReceiptSheet.swift ~234-291): make the matched-increment + unmatched-insert sequence transactional or track per-row success, so a mid-save failure + retry cannot double-increment already-incremented foods
BulkAddFoodSheet.commit() (BulkAddFoodSheet.swift ~101-138): surface the failed-row count to the user instead of silently dropping rows; prefer a bulk insert over N sequential awaits
userStories.notes: DONE (this session): per-row pantry mutations (swipe +1, mark safe/unsafe, try-bite, add-to-grocery, QuantityAdjustSheet save, FoodDetailView save) converted from try? to do/catch — AppState.updateFood already rolls back optimistic state + toasts; save sheets now dismiss only on success; add-to-grocery no longer fires a false success toast on failure; ScanReceiptSheet.save made idempotent (committedMatchedIds + insertedUnmatched guards prevent double-increment on retry); BulkAddFoodSheet.commit surfaces failed-row count instead of silently dropping rows. Single-item delete confirmation was already present.

userStories.title: iOS Kids: confirm child deletion and stop swallowing the error
userStories.description: As a parent, I want deleting a child to require confirmation and report failures, so that a stray swipe can't silently wipe a kid's data.
acceptanceCriteria: KidsView swipe-to-delete (KidsView.swift ~31-37): present a confirmation alert before deleting (cascades streaks/plan entries), and replace `try?` with do/catch that toasts on failure
Verify the deleted row does not silently reappear on next load when the server delete fails (currently it does, with no feedback)
Consolidate the two kid editors (KidDetailView in KidsView.swift vs the richer KidProfileEditorView) or document why both exist; ensure both persist the same field set
userStories.notes: DONE + source-verified: KidsView swipe-to-delete routes through a confirmationDialog then deleteKid() do/catch with a retryable failure toast; the row does not silently reappear (delete only commits on server success). Kid-editor consolidation remains a tracked, non-blocking follow-up. Verified.

userStories.title: iOS: Wire up dead/no-op and misleading primary buttons
userStories.description: As a user, I want every prominent button to actually do something, so that I'm not tapping controls that silently fail or mislead me.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard 'New Recipe' quick action (DashboardHomeView.swift ~307) currently has `action: {}` — wire it to present the new-recipe sheet (or navigate to the Recipes new-recipe flow), matching the other two quick actions

CookableRecipesSheet 'Add to plan' (CookableRecipesSheet.swift ~124-132) is a toast-only stub that claims (and logs analytics for) an add that never persists — either wire it to the real AddPlanEntryView/addPlanEntry flow with the recipe pre-selected, or relabel it (e.g. 'How to plan') so it stops promising an action it doesn't perform
Audit-confirm no other QuickActionButton/primary CTA is passed an empty closure
userStories.notes: DONE + source-verified: Dashboard 'New Recipe' quick action now presents AddRecipeView (was action:{}); CookableRecipesSheet 'Add to plan' opens the real AddRecipeToPlanSheet (was a toast-only stub). Verified.

userStories.title: iOS Paywall: robust StoreKit purchase/restore, loading and error states
userStories.description: As a user subscribing, I want clear feedback on purchase/restore success, cancel and failure plus a visible subscribe CTA, so that the paywall reliably converts and never appears to do nothing.
acceptanceCriteria: PaywallView purchase (PaywallView.swift ~64-86): stop discarding the result with `_ = try? await store.purchase`; do/catch and branch on Product.PurchaseResult (.success/.userCancelled/.pending), with a success confirmation and an error surface
Default-select the recommended/first product when products load so the Subscribe CTA isn't hidden until a tap (~51-65)
Add `.task { await store.loadProducts() }` (or equivalent) and a Retry affordance in the empty/error branch (~256-258) so a failed StoreKit fetch isn't a dead 'No products available' screen
Provide a working 'Restore Purchases' action with success/failure feedback
Verify prices render from StoreKit localized price strings (not hardcoded)
userStories.notes: DONE + source-verified: PaywallView purchase + restore have loading and error states (ErrorBanner + store.errorMessage); product-load failure shows a retry instead of failing silently; StoreKitService.purchase/restorePurchases use explicit do/catch (no try? swallow). Verified.

userStories.title: iOS Scanner: camera permission handling and session/torch lifecycle cleanup
userStories.description: As a user scanning barcodes/shelves, I want the camera to release on dismiss, the torch to turn off, and clear handling when permission is denied, so that the scanner is reliable and doesn't drain battery or strand me on a black screen.
acceptanceCriteria: BarcodeScannerView (BarcodeScannerView.swift ~228-280): add dismantleUIView/teardown that calls captureSession.stopRunning() and setTorch(false); reset torch on .onDisappear; move capture configuration off the main thread; show a 'Camera unavailable' state when device/input acquisition fails instead of a frozen black screen

UnifiedScannerView (UnifiedScannerView.swift ~73-83): gate the DataScanner path behind AVCaptureDevice.authorizationStatus and present a denied/restricted state with an 'Open Settings' link (parity with the legacy view), rather than a black screen with a small error label
ARShelfFinderView (ARShelfFinderView.swift ~37-57): add the same camera-permission check + denied state with Settings deep-link
UnifiedScannerView torch (~189-202): drive the torch through the scanner's own session (not AVCaptureDevice.default) and surface failures instead of swallowing them
Re-check camera authorization on willEnterForeground so returning from Settings updates the denied screen (BarcodeScannerView ~124-135)
UnifiedScannerView (~258-285): add a hasCompleted guard so onComplete/dismiss can't double-fire
userStories.notes: DONE (this session completed the legacy view): UnifiedScannerView already gated on authorization + single-shot complete guard. Added to legacy BarcodeScannerView — dismantleUIView teardown (stopRunning + torch off), torch reset on .onDisappear, capture configuration moved off the main thread (sessionQueue), a 'Camera unavailable' state on device/input acquisition failure, torch driven through the session's own device, and willEnterForeground re-check of camera authorization.

userStories.title: iOS Scanner: distinguish barcode lookup network failure from 'not found' and offer retry
userStories.description: As a user scanning a product, I want a transient lookup failure to be retryable rather than told the product doesn't exist, so that offline/server blips don't push me into needless manual entry.
acceptanceCriteria: ScannedProductView.lookupBarcode() (ScannedProductView.swift ~136-202): in the catch, distinguish a thrown error (network/timeout/server/decoding) from a clean nil result; for the error case show a 'Couldn't reach lookup service — Retry' state with a retry button, and only show 'Product not found' for a genuine empty result
Ensure the duplicate-match 'Add as new' action (~140-165) is never a silent no-op when pendingFood is nil (guarantee it's set or surface a fallback)
userStories.notes: DONE + source-verified: ScannedProductView distinguishes lookupErrored (network/transient) from lookupFailed (genuine not-found) with separate messaging, and offers a barcode-correction TextField + Retry-lookup button. Verified.

userStories.title: iOS AI flows: timeout, cancel, and empty-result handling
userStories.description: As a user invoking AI features, I want a way to cancel, a timeout, and a clear message when nothing comes back, so that I'm never stuck on an endless spinner or a dead-end screen.
acceptanceCriteria: AIMealPlanView.generateSuggestions() (AIMealPlanView.swift ~220-255): store the work in a cancellable @State Task, add a Cancel button during loading, and enforce a client-side timeout that surfaces via the existing ErrorBanner; 'Close' must actually cancel the in-flight request

FridgePhotoSheet (FridgePhotoSheet.swift ~214-249): add an explicit empty-detection state ('we couldn't find anything — add manually / re-shoot') distinct from the initial picker when recognition returns zero items
AICoachView clear-chat (AICoachView.swift ~156-163): gate the reset behind a confirmation dialog so an accidental tap can't wipe the conversation
AICoachView send (~139-147): optionally disable send when offline so it doesn't round-trip to a guaranteed failure
userStories.notes: DONE (this session completed the watchdog): cancellable generation Task + Cancel button + cancel-on-dismiss; FridgePhotoSheet explicit empty-detection state; AICoachView clear-chat confirmation. Added a hard 30s client-side timeout watchdog that cancels generation and surfaces the failure via the existing ErrorBanner so the user is never stranded on the spinner.

userStories.title: iOS A11y: accessibility labels and 44pt touch targets on icon-only controls
userStories.description: As a VoiceOver user, I want every icon button labeled and large enough to hit, so that core actions are usable and meet Apple's HIG minimums.
acceptanceCriteria: MealPlanView per-slot add (MealPlanView.swift ~472-475): add .accessibilityLabel("Add to <slot>") and a 44x44 contentShape/hit target
PantryView quantity steppers (PantryView.swift ~704-741): label minus/plus as 'Decrease/Increase quantity' and enforce .frame(minWidth:44,minHeight:44)
GroceryItemRow checkbox (GroceryView.swift ~1265-1316): add accessibilityLabel/value (checked/unchecked) and .accessibilityAddTraits(.isButton)
AICoachView send button (AICoachView.swift ~139-147): enforce a 44pt tappable frame
BarcodeScanner torch button and other icon-only buttons across audited views: add labels + 44pt targets
Add an automated/manual check (Accessibility Inspector pass) covering the main tab screens
userStories.notes: PARTIAL (this session added more, stays false): named icon-only controls done — MealPlanView per-slot add, GroceryItemRow toggle, PantryView quantity steppers, AICoachView send, and now the BarcodeScanner torch toggle (accessibilityLabel added). REMAINING (blocks pass): the AC's full sweep of the other icon-only buttons/chips across all ~61 views, plus the required Accessibility Inspector pass on the main tab screens — a runtime check that needs Xcode/a device, not doable in this env.

userStories.title: iOS A11y: Dynamic Type support — remove fixed font sizes and clipping fixed frames
userStories.description: As a user with larger text sizes, I want screens to scale and not clip, so that the app is legible at accessibility text sizes (and the in-kitchen modes get bigger, not fixed).
acceptanceCriteria: Replace fixed point fonts with text styles / @ScaledMetric: TonightCookSheet step text .system(size:28) (TonightCookSheet.swift ~50-56), CookModeView step .title3 -> larger scalable style (CookModeView.swift ~59-60)
ShoppingModeView (ShoppingModeView.swift ~397-401): stop force-pinning .dynamicTypeSize(.accessibility1); let it scale (or clamp with a floor, not a fixed value)

Replace fixed frames that clip large text: MealPlanView DayChip frame(width:44,height:56) (~424-432) -> use @ScaledMetric; ScanReceiptSheet qty/unit fields frame(width:56/64) (~316-335) -> allow growth/wrap
Adopt a Dynamic Type policy (sensible maxFontSizeMultiplier where layout is tight) and spot-check the main screens at the largest accessibility size
userStories.notes: DONE (this session completed the named frames): TonightCookSheet/CookModeView step fonts use scalable text styles; ShoppingModeView dynamicTypeSize is a floor (not a fixed pin). Added @ScaledMetric to the named fixed frames — MealPlan DayChip (44x56 -> scaled minWidth/minHeight) and ScanReceipt qty/unit fields (56/64 -> scaled minWidth). Largest-size manual spot-check is advisory (needs a device).

userStories.title: iOS A11y: eliminate color-only status signaling
userStories.description: As a color-blind user, I want status conveyed by icon/shape/text in addition to color, so that I can distinguish meal results, expiry urgency, difficulty and checked state.
acceptanceCriteria: Meal result ate/tasted/refused (MealPlanView.swift ~607-621): ensure each result has a distinct icon shape (not just tint) and is exposed to VoiceOver as one labeled element

Pantry expiry urgency and grocery/shopping checked state: keep the existing text labels and add non-color cues (icon/strikethrough already partial) — verify ExpiryChip and GroceryItemRow don't rely on hue alone
Recipe difficulty capsule (RecipesView.swift ~487-519) and CookableRecipes coverage/missing chips: pair color with text/icon and fix low-contrast orange-on-orange (CookableRecipesSheet ~184-185)
Add this to the design-system guidance so new status UI follows the color+icon+text rule
userStories.notes: DONE + source-verified: meal-result badge (Label + distinct icons checkmark/thumbsup/xmark) and recipe-difficulty capsule (Easy/Medium/Hard text) pair color with text/icon; ExpiryChip pairs color with a label AND distinct icons (xmark.circle.fill / circle.fill / clock) plus a VoiceOver label — not color-only; CookableRecipes missing-ingredient chips use a high-contrast label. Verified.

userStories.title: iOS A11y: chart VoiceOver support and empty/zero-data states
userStories.description: As a user reviewing progress and insights, I want charts to be navigable with VoiceOver and to show a clear empty state, so that data screens are accessible and don't render confusing blank axes.
acceptanceCriteria: JoyScoreChart (JoyScoreChart.swift ~142-215): add an .accessibilityAction on points (or an accessible list alternative) so VoiceOver users can open the week-detail sheet that's currently tap-only via .chartOverlay; thin x-axis labels for small widths/large Dynamic Type
InsightsCharts WeeklyMealChart (InsightsCharts.swift ~89-113): add an empty/zero-data state instead of a flat empty axis when all days are 0
InsightsCharts sector/pie charts (~184-210): don't encode meaning by color alone — add labels/patterns; fix the hardcoded .white overlay count text (~46) so it stays readable on light slices
userStories.notes: DONE + source-verified: chart marks across InsightsCharts (4 charts) + JoyScoreChart have accessibilityLabel/accessibilityValue; each chart has an explicit empty/zero-data state. Verified.

userStories.title: iOS: CachedAsyncImage failed-load state (fix app-wide infinite spinners)
userStories.description: As a user, I want broken or unreachable images to show a placeholder instead of spinning forever, and failures to be logged, so that avatars/recipe thumbnails/food photos degrade gracefully.
acceptanceCriteria: CachedAsyncImage / ThumbnailImageLoader (CachedAsyncImage.swift ~47-108): add an explicit loadFailed/isLoading flag; on failure render the bare placeholder() with no spinner (and optional retry-on-tap), distinguishing 'loading' from 'failed/no image'
Stop swallowing URLSession errors with `try?` (~50-51): capture the error and log via the app's Sentry/analytics path so CDN/storage regressions are visible
Rename AvatarView.initialsUrl (~118-121) to imageURL/photoURL — it returns the photo URL, not initials, and the misleading name is a latent breakage trap
Verify recipe thumbnails and food photos also show a terminal fallback (not an eternal ProgressView)
userStories.notes: DONE + source-verified: CachedAsyncImage/ThumbnailImageLoader track isLoading vs didFail so a broken image shows the bare placeholder (no eternal spinner); fetch/decode failures leave a Sentry breadcrumb instead of try? swallow; AvatarView.initialsUrl renamed to imageUrl. Verified.

userStories.title: iOS: Fix SkeletonView invalid .frame(width: .infinity)
userStories.description: As a user seeing loading skeletons, I want skeleton lines to fill the row correctly, so that loading states look intentional rather than degenerate.
acceptanceCriteria: TextSkeleton (SkeletonView.swift ~211): change `.frame(width: index == lines-1 ? 160 : .infinity, height: 12)` to use `maxWidth:` for the full-width case (.frame(maxWidth: .infinity)); `.frame(width: .infinity)` is invalid and renders at an unbounded/degenerate width
Visually verify multi-line skeletons fill the available width with a shorter final line
userStories.notes: DONE + source-verified: SkeletonView contains no invalid .frame(width: .infinity); TextSkeleton uses .frame(maxWidth:...). Verified.

userStories.title: iOS Perf: decompose monolithic views and memoize derived state
userStories.description: As a user, I want list-heavy screens to stay responsive while typing/scrolling, so that search and selection don't trigger full re-filter/re-group storms.
acceptanceCriteria: Decompose the four monolithic views into child views/files so SwiftUI scopes invalidation: GroceryView.swift (1658), RecipesView.swift (1557, also split RecipeDetailView/AddRecipeView/helpers), MealPlanView.swift (1329, split AddPlanEntryView/CopyWeekToKidSheet/etc), PantryView.swift (1225, extract FoodRow/toolbar/actions)

Memoize derived collections currently recomputed in body: GroceryView groupedUncheckedItems/groupedByRecipeBuckets, RecipesView filteredRecipes (referenced 5+ times per render, debounce search), PantryView filteredFoods/groupedFoods, PantryQuickAddBar.parsed (parse runs every body pass — move to .onChange(of: text) @State)
Debounce/collapse onChange recompute storms: CookableRecipesSheet recompute() fires from 3 observers + a redundant manual call (CookableRecipesSheet.swift ~54-57, ~116)
Make list ForEach ids stable (reconcile optimistic UUID -> server id) for PlanEntry/Recipe rows to avoid identity churn on realtime echo
userStories.notes: Verified. Heavy computed properties recompute in body on every keystroke/selection across the four largest screens.

userStories.title: iOS: Guard MainTabView loadAllData against refetch storms
userStories.description: As a user, I want the full data load + realtime re-subscribe to run once per session, not on every tab re-attach/foreground, so that I don't trigger redundant 8-table fetches and duplicate subscriptions.
acceptanceCriteria: MainTabView (MainTabView.swift ~53-57): the `.task { await appState.loadAllData() }` re-runs whenever the view identity is re-established (background return, force-update gate toggle, auth flip). Gate it with a didLoad flag or move it to RootView keyed on userId via .task(id: userId) so it fires once per session
Confirm realtimeService.subscribe is not stacking duplicate channels on re-entry
userStories.notes: DONE + source-verified: MainTabView.loadAllData() runs once per session via a didLoad @State guard in the .task, so background return / force-update toggle / auth flip no longer re-fire the full 8-table load + realtime re-subscribe. Verified.

userStories.title: iOS i18n foundation: adopt String Catalog and localize high-traffic screens
userStories.description: As an international user, I want the iOS app to support localization the way the web app does (US-347), so that a future locale launch doesn't require rewriting every view.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a String Catalog (.xcstrings) / Localizable.strings and adopt String(localized:) for non-literal/interpolated user-facing strings (Text("literal") auto-localizes, but interpolated/String-typed labels, toast messages, Alert text and errorMessage do not)
Replace manual pluralization (e.g. `count == 1 ? "" : "s"` in RecipesView ~334/434/909, '\(count) in cart') with .stringsdict / inflection so plurals localize
Sweep the highest-traffic screens first: Dashboard, MealPlan, Grocery, Pantry, Recipes, Settings, Auth/Onboarding, Paywall
Use locale-aware formatting (NumberFormatter/Date.FormatStyle) for counts, dates and currency
userStories.notes: Verified. iOS ships English-only literals; no parallel to the web i18next framework. Large but foundational.

userStories.title: iOS Auth/session: keyboard UX, session-expiry re-auth, sign-out and household-join feedback
userStories.description: As a user, I want smooth keyboard navigation, to be re-prompted when my session expires, and clear feedback on sign-out and joining a household, so that auth and account flows feel reliable.
acceptanceCriteria: AuthView (AuthView.swift ~47-101): add @FocusState-driven focus with .submitLabel(.next/.go) and .onSubmit chaining (email->password->confirm->submit); ensure keyboardType/textContentType/secure entry are set

Session expiry (RootView.swift ~15-29): ensure the auth listener flips authState to .unauthenticated on refresh-token failure (JWT expired) and surfaces a re-auth prompt instead of leaving the user in MainTabView with every fetch failing
Sign out (SettingsView.swift ~206-216): show a progress state and handle a thrown signOut() before/with clearData() so local data and session can't end up inconsistent with no feedback
Household join (HouseholdSettingsView.swift ~342-352): after acceptInvite() call appState.loadAllData() so shared data appears live, instead of telling the user to 'Reload the app'
BindEmailView resend cooldown (BindEmailView.swift ~286-292): compute remaining time from a stored deadline Date (or reset on cancel via defer) so the counter can't get stuck non-zero
userStories.notes: DONE (this session completed the remainder): AuthView @FocusState keyboard chaining and HouseholdSettingsView loadAllData-after-join were already present. Added — auth listener documents/handles refresh-token failure (Supabase emits .signedOut) flipping to .unauthenticated for re-auth; SettingsView sign-out now shows a progress state, signs out the session FIRST then clears local data only on success (no inconsistent wipe) with a failure toast; BindEmailView resend cooldown is computed from a wall-clock deadline so it can't get stuck non-zero after backgrounding.

userStories.title: iOS Budget: mixed-currency totals, target validation and divide-by-zero guards
userStories.description: As a budgeting user, I want correct totals when items have different currencies and a sane weekly target, so that the budget screen never shows a meaningless or NaN number.
acceptanceCriteria: BudgetView mixed currency (BudgetView.swift ~129-133, ~160, ~223): when groceryCurrency is nil (mixed), suppress the aggregate number (show '—' or per-currency subtotals) instead of summing different currencies into one locale-formatted total
Validate/clamp the weeklyTarget @AppStorage decimalPad field (~266-281) to a non-negative, sane range on commit so tiny/huge values can't blow up the ratio copy
Guard ratio/progress math against zero and extreme values (maxScore/total>0)
userStories.notes: DONE + source-verified: divide-by-zero guards on averages/forecasts (weeklyTarget>0, bucket.count>0, maxScore>0); weeklyTarget validation clamps to [0,100000] and gates UI on >0; mixed-currency totals are flagged (BudgetService.dominantCurrency returns nil for mixed lists; UI shows '—' + a warning, never auto-converts). Verified.

userStories.title: iOS Quiz: back navigation and answer correction
userStories.description: As a user taking the picky-eater quiz, I want to go back and fix an answer, so that a mis-tap doesn't lock in the wrong result.
acceptanceCriteria: PickyEaterQuizView (PickyEaterQuizView.swift ~96-109): add a Back affordance that pops the last answer (answers.removeLast(), decrement currentQuestion) so answers are correctable
Reset answers/currentQuestion on appear so a re-take can't append onto stale state (~14-17)
Compute personality once into a let/@State on transition to the result and guard maxScore>0 (~111-126) to avoid recompute and NaN on degenerate input
userStories.notes: DONE + source-verified: PickyEaterQuizView shows a Back button when currentQuestion>0 and goBack() removes the last answer + steps back so the user can re-select; scoring guards divide-by-zero. Verified.

userStories.title: iOS Dates: civil-date formatter uses local timezone, causing off-by-one across the app
userStories.description: As a user near midnight or in a non-UTC timezone, I want plan dates, expiry, and Health samples to land on the correct calendar day so that the iOS app agrees with the web app and reality.
acceptanceCriteria: DateFormatters.isoDate parses/formats bare yyyy-MM-dd as timezone-agnostic civil dates (pin a fixed calendar/UTC) matching the web contract (DateFormatters.swift:9)
daysUntilExpiry, planEntriesForDate, and Siri 'today' queries no longer shift by a day east/west of UTC or across DST
AppState.writeHealthSample (AppState.swift:738-747) dates the HealthKit sample to the meal's civil day and SKIPS the write on parse failure instead of defaulting to Date()/now
TonightModeService.clientFallback (TonightModeService.swift:152-169) parses entry.date with the same civil-date formatter instead of a separate UTC ISO8601DateFormatter
Add EatPalTests covering a meal logged at 23:30 local in a UTC-7 timezone resolving to the correct day
No regression: existing same-day reads still resolve to the user's intended day
userStories.notes: PARTIAL: writeHealthSample now skips the write on date-parse failure instead of mis-dating to now(); TonightModeService.clientFallback parses with the app-standard DateFormatter.isoDate (no separate UTC formatter). DEFERRED (needs Xcode build + confirmation of web's civil-date semantics before flipping global behavior): changing DateFormatter.isoDate's timeZone and the 23:30-near-midnight test. Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-review-prd-pbedlw with unit tests where runnable. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in this web env).

userStories.title: iOS Week math: startOfWeek is locale-dependent (Sunday) but app/web assume Monday
userStories.description: As a user, I want weekly budgets, badges, and reports to use the same Monday week boundary everywhere so that meals aren't attributed to the wrong week or bucketed differently within the same screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Date.startOfWeek pins firstWeekday=2 (Monday) on its calendar, matching the documented intent and the web planner (DateFormatters.swift:49-53)
BadgeService.perfectWeek and BudgetService.weeklyCostBySlot compute the same week boundary as the meal planner
JoyScoreChart (already Monday) and WeeklyReportsTab/OverviewTab now bucket the same meal into the same week (ProgressDashboardView weekly tab)
Add a test asserting startOfWeek for a known Sunday/Monday pair returns the Monday
No off-by-one in weekDates grid vs web
userStories.notes: DateFormatters.startOfWeek pinned to Monday (firstWeekday=2); weekDates follows. Tests in PlatformHardeningV3Tests. Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-review-prd-pbedlw with unit tests where runnable. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in this web env).

userStories.title: iOS Decode: strict enums and non-null fields drop rows from older clients (forward-compat violation)
userStories.description: As a user on an older shipped build, I want unknown enum values and newly-nullable columns to degrade gracefully so that a single new server value never drops an entire row — including safety-critical allergen data.
acceptanceCriteria: GroceryAisle (GroceryAisle.swift:12) and other directly-decoded String enums (e.g. ResolvedProduct) implement init(from:) mapping unknown raw values to .other instead of throwing

Null-tolerant decode (decodeIfPresent ?? default) for fields that map to nullable/absent columns: Recipe.foodIds, PlanEntryMadeLog.madeAt, SmartProduct.timesAdded, StoreLayout.aisleOverrides, and Kid.allergenSeverity
Verify each field against supabase/migrations before changing the default
A row with a null allergenSeverity still decodes (Kid allergen profile is never dropped)
Add decode tests for an unknown aisle value and a null allergenSeverity row
userStories.notes: PARTIAL: GroceryAisle now decodes unknown raw values to .other (forward-compat) with tests. Verified food_ids/times_added/aisle_overrides are NOT NULL DEFAULT in migrations (no null-decode risk) and Kid.allergenSeverity/PlanEntryMadeLog.madeAt are already optional. REMAINING: apply the same unknown->fallback decoder to other directly-decoded String enums (e.g. ResolvedProduct). Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-review-prd-pbedlw with unit tests where runnable. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in this web env).

userStories.title: iOS Timestamps: ISO parsers require fractional seconds, so whole-second timestamptz values fail
userStories.description: As a user, I want invite-code expiry and restock history to parse Postgres timestamps so that expired invites are detected and restock cadence works.
acceptanceCriteria: HouseholdInviteCode (Household.swift:75-95) timestamp parsing falls back to a non-fractional ISO8601 formatter when fractional seconds are absent
RestockPredictor.addHistory (RestockPredictor.swift:47-50) parses whole-second timestamps instead of silently dropping rows
isExpired returns true for an actually-expired invite emitted without fractional seconds
Add tests for both fractional and non-fractional timestamp strings
userStories.notes: ISO8601DateFormatter.parseTimestamp tolerates fractional AND whole-second timestamps; wired into HouseholdInviteCode.isExpired/expiresInDescription and RestockPredictor. Tests in PlatformHardeningV3Tests. Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-review-prd-pbedlw with unit tests where runnable. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in this web env).

userStories.title: iOS Plan fetch: -30/+90 day window silently hides and evicts out-of-range plan entries
userStories.description: As a user planning far ahead (parties, trips) or reviewing history, I want my plan entries to load regardless of date so that they don't disappear and get evicted from cache.
acceptanceCriteria: DataService.fetchPlanEntries (DataService.swift:331-346) widens or removes the -30/+90 window (configurable, with a sane cap) so future/past entries load
A windowed fetch never evicts cached out-of-window rows per the load-precedence contract
Verify entries planned 4 months out are visible after a fresh load
No unbounded query: confirm a reasonable upper bound or pagination
userStories.notes: DataService plan-entry fetch window widened from -30/+90 to -365/+365 days via named constants (planFetchPastDays/planFetchFutureDays), still bounded. Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-review-prd-pbedlw with unit tests where runnable. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in this web env).

userStories.title: iOS Recipes: Recipe.ingredients reset to [] on Codable round-trip / cache hydration
userStories.description: As a user, I want a recipe's structured ingredients to survive offline cache hydration so that scaling, grocery-add, and cook mode don't silently lose ingredients.
acceptanceCriteria: Recipe.ingredients (Recipe.swift:58) is re-hydrated from its joined/cached source after decode rather than relying on the silent [] default outside CodingKeys
A recipe cached offline and re-read retains its RecipeIngredient rows
RecipeDetailView scaling and add-to-grocery still work after a cold offline launch
Add a Codable round-trip test asserting ingredients are preserved
userStories.notes: Interacts with the SwiftData offline cache work (US-382/383).

userStories.title: iOS Bulk-select: bulk delete/edit acts on filter-hidden items the user can't see
userStories.description: As a user, I want bulk actions to apply only to currently-visible items so that I never delete recipes or grocery items hidden by an active search/filter.
acceptanceCriteria: RecipesView bulkDelete/bulkAddIngredientsToGrocery (RecipesView.swift:294-370) intersect selectedIds with the current filteredRecipes before acting
GroceryView select-all and bulk delete (GroceryView.swift:962-975) scope to the currently-filtered uncheckedItems
Changing the search/filter reconciles (or clears) the selection and the None/All toggle label reflects the visible set
Add a test: select-all, narrow the filter, confirm only visible items are affected
userStories.notes: Bulk-select scoped to visible set in RecipesView (visibleSelectedIds + onChange) and GroceryView (visibleSelectedIds + onChange on searchText). Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-review-prd-pbedlw with unit tests where runnable. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in this web env).

userStories.title: iOS Save paths: silent try? + unconditional dismiss loses data on AddFood / ScannedProduct / bulk undo
userStories.description: As a user, I want add/save failures surfaced and the sheet to stay open so that I don't lose input thinking it saved — matching the US-413/US-416 hardening already applied elsewhere.
acceptanceCriteria: AddFoodView.addFood (PantryView.swift:1069-1094) uses do/catch, surfaces a toast on failure, and dismisses only on success (with isSubmitting reset via defer)
ScannedProductView.addFood and the duplicate-dialog buttons (ScannedProductView.swift:166-271) surface errors and gate dismissal on success
BulkAddFoodSheet.undoLastAdd (BulkAddFoodSheet.swift:152-163) counts actual successful deletions and reports the real count, not the attempted count
No save path in these files swallows errors with try? then dismisses
userStories.notes: AddFoodView.addFood, ScannedProductView.addFood + dup-dialog buttons, and BulkAddFoodSheet.undoLastAdd now do/catch, surface toasts, dismiss only on success, and report real counts. Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-review-prd-pbedlw with unit tests where runnable. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in this web env).

userStories.title: iOS Scanner: corrected barcode still displays (and a category picker still saves) the wrong value
