userStories.description: As a user who fixes a misread barcode, I want the displayed barcode to match what's persisted, and receipt category edits to be honored, so that I'm not misled and my categorization isn't discarded.
acceptanceCriteria: ScannedProductView Product Details binds the Barcode label to currentBarcode (the value actually persisted), not the original scanned barcode (ScannedProductView.swift:93-97)
ScanReceiptSheet category Picker (ScanReceiptSheet.swift:342-401) offers only valid FoodCategory values, or normalizedCategory preserves the user's choice instead of collapsing everything to 'snack'
Re-categorizing a receipt line to 'frozen'/'beverage' persists that category
userStories.notes: Barcode label now shows the effective (corrected) barcode; ScanReceiptSheet category Picker restricted to real FoodCategory values so choices aren't collapsed to snack. Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-review-prd-pbedlw with unit tests where runnable. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in this web env).

userStories.title: iOS Units: fl oz parsed as mass; mismatch unflagged when one side is unitless; shortfall uses exact-string unit equality
userStories.description: As a user marking meals made, I want unit handling to be volume/mass-aware and tolerant of formatting so that pantry debits and shortfalls are correct.
acceptanceCriteria: IngredientTextParser keeps 'fl oz'/'fluid ounce' as a distinct volume canonical token, not collapsed to mass 'oz' (IngredientTextParser.swift:199 + UnitConverter)
MealMadeStrategy flags a unit mismatch when exactly one side has a unit (MealMadeStrategy.swift:111-117), consistent with ShortfallCalculator/PantryCoverage
ShortfallCalculator/ShortfallChecker normalize units via UnitConverter.canonical before comparing (so 'tbsp' == 'tbsp.') and merge duplicate ingredient names by summing/maxing needed quantity
Add tests for fl oz volume debit, one-sided-unit mismatch, and 'tbsp' vs 'tbsp.'
userStories.notes: Silently converts volume to weight on pantry debit; under-orders on duplicate names.

userStories.title: iOS Siri: LogMealResult/TodaysPlan apply to ALL kids and leak tryBite/raw enum text
userStories.description: As a parent in a multi-kid household, I want voice meal logging scoped to one child and the spoken plan to read naturally so that I don't mark every kid's dinner eaten by accident.
acceptanceCriteria: LogMealResultIntent (MealAppIntents.swift:79-97) takes an optional Kid parameter defaulting to the active kid and scopes the result update to that child
TodaysPlanIntent excludes .tryBite from slotOrder so it isn't spoken as a scheduled meal (FoodAppIntents.swift:204)
Intent dialog uses caseDisplayRepresentations / display titles instead of raw enum rawValue (MealAppIntents.swift:88,107)
Intents and import deep-links guard for an active session and surface a 'sign in' result when signed out (DeepLinkHandler.swift:89-91)
userStories.notes: PARTIAL: LogMealResultIntent/TodaysPlanIntent now guard for an active session (sign-in prompt when signed out); spoken dialog uses natural-language labels instead of raw enum values; TodaysPlanIntent excludes the try-bite slot from the spoken plan. DEFERRED to a build session: the optional Kid parameter to scope LogMealResult to one child — needs a cross-process activeKidId (App Group) or a Kid AppEntity, both warranting device verification. Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-review-prd-pbedlw. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in this web env).

userStories.title: iOS Progress: Overview/Weekly stats read global data, not the active child
userStories.description: As a parent viewing one child's progress, I want Foods Tried, success rate, and diversity scoped to that child so that the numbers reflect the child I'm looking at.
acceptanceCriteria: ProgressDashboardView OverviewTab (ProgressDashboardView.swift:48-111) filters foods/plan entries to activeKidId before computing totalFoodsTried, successRate, categoryDiversity, and FoodResultsChart
WeeklyReportsTab 'Foods Tried' (ProgressDashboardView.swift:479-481) counts distinct foodIds only among entries with result=='ate', excluding empty ids, and is labeled accurately
DashboardHomeView TodayMealSummaryCard falls back to the recipe name when foodId doesn't resolve so recipe-based meals aren't dropped (DashboardHomeView.swift:225-228)
userStories.notes: OverviewTab successRate + FoodResultsChart now scoped to the active child; WeeklyReports 'foods tried' counts distinct non-empty foodIds among entries actually eaten; DashboardHomeView today-summary falls back to recipe name so recipe-based meals aren't dropped. Pantry-derived stats (Foods Tried/Categories) stay household-level because `foods` carry no kidId. Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-review-prd-pbedlw. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in this web env).

userStories.title: iOS Notifications: master toggle re-enables itself; mute window never auto-resumes
userStories.description: As a user, I want my explicit notification choices respected so that disabled reminders stay off and a timed mute resumes reminders when it expires.
acceptanceCriteria: NotificationSettingsView (.task, SettingsView.swift:385-390) does not force the master toggle ON just because OS permission is granted; it persists and respects the user's explicit master choice

When a timed mute (mutedUntil) expires, topics reschedule without requiring an app relaunch (NotificationService.swift:208-315) — e.g. re-evaluate on willEnterForeground or schedule a wake at mutedUntil
Add a test: master OFF with OS-granted permission stays OFF after reopening settings
userStories.notes: Beyond US-403/404: these are the toggle-persistence and mute-expiry edge cases.

userStories.title: iOS Food Chaining: ignores allergens, shows stale chain, and never shows generating state
userStories.description: As a parent of a child with allergies, I want generated food chains to exclude allergens and reflect the current selection so that suggestions are safe and accurate.
acceptanceCriteria: FoodChainingView.generateChain (FoodChainingView.swift:152-207) filters target/bridge foods against the active kid's allergens and warns rather than suggesting an allergen
chainSteps is cleared at the top of generateChain when either selection is missing (no stale chain from a prior pairing)
Remove or correctly drive the unused isGenerating flag (currently flips true→false synchronously so the ProgressView never shows)
userStories.notes: Food-chaining pickers now exclude foods conflicting with the active child's allergens; a selected conflict shows a red allergen warning and suppresses the chain; chainSteps/warning cleared at the top of generateChain; dead isGenerating flag removed. Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-review-prd-pbedlw. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in this web env).

userStories.title: iOS Perf: any single mutation rebuilds the full widget snapshot + watch push; foods slices not memoized
userStories.description: As a user, I want single-item edits to avoid full O(n) snapshot rebuilds and re-renders so that the app stays responsive during edits and bulk imports.
acceptanceCriteria: AppState.setupWidgetSnapshotSync (AppState.swift:162-195) gates the snapshot rebuild + watch push on a cheap content hash/version so no-op or check-only changes don't rebuild
Derived slices foodsByCategory/safeFoods/tryBiteFoods (AppState.swift:203-213) are cached and invalidated only when foods changes (or foods moves to its own observable)
A single grocery toggle no longer republishes unrelated arrays or pays a full snapshot rebuild
No behavioral change to widget/watch contents
userStories.notes: Builds on US-388/US-429 perf work; targets the combine pipeline + derived properties.

userStories.title: iOS Watch/Widget: grocery counts mix capped vs total, complications never reload, slot encoding drifts
userStories.description: As a watch/widget user, I want accurate counts and fresh data so that '50 of 80 left' isn't wrong and the watch face updates promptly.
acceptanceCriteria: WatchSnapshot stores an uncapped uncheckedGroceryCount used for summaries/complications; the 50-row cap applies only to the rendered list (WatchConnectivityService.swift:103, GroceryWatchView.swift:13-19)
WatchSnapshotStore.save / WatchSessionStore.cache calls WidgetCenter.shared.reloadAllTimelines() so complications refresh on new snapshots (EatPalWatchComplications.swift, WatchSessionStore.swift:75)
Slot encoding is centralized in one shared helper so widget (displayName) and watch (raw) snapshots agree, and try_bite maps to a real label (TodayView.slotLabel)
tryBiteStreak is populated in WidgetSnapshot.buildPayload (or the dead streak branch/deep-link is removed) (EatPalWidget.swift:329-336, WidgetSnapshot.swift:117)
userStories.notes: PARTIAL: watch grocery summary and complication now use the uncapped unchecked count (total-checked) instead of the 50-capped list; WatchSessionStore.cache reloads complication timelines on each new snapshot. REMAINING: centralize slot display encoding between widget/watch snapshots and populate/remove tryBiteStreak. Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-review-prd-pbedlw. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in this web env).

userStories.title: iOS Share Extension: file URLs routed to recipe parser, no text size cap, Bundle.main config keys
userStories.description: As a user sharing into EatPal, I want non-web shares handled gracefully and large text bounded so that imports don't dead-end or blow the extension memory budget.
acceptanceCriteria: ShareViewController.extractSharedContent rejects url.isFileURL / non-http(s) schemes and falls through to text handling (ShareViewController.swift:55-74)
Shared plain text is truncated to a sane cap (e.g. 20KB / few hundred lines) before GroceryTextParser.parse, and re-parsing is debounced (ShareImportView.swift:547)

Recipe-parse config (FUNCTIONS_URL/SUPABASE_URL) resolves correctly inside the extension target (inject the build-setting Info.plist keys or read from the App Group) instead of silently falling back to a hardcoded host (RecipeParseAPI.swift:127-143)
userStories.notes: PARTIAL: ShareViewController rejects file/non-http(s) URLs (so a shared file no longer dead-ends in the recipe parser) and caps shared text to 20k chars before parsing (extension memory budget). REMAINING: debounce the editor re-parse in ShareImportView, and confirm the extension target injects FUNCTIONS_URL/SUPABASE_URL Info.plist keys (RecipeParseAPI Bundle.main fallback). Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-review-prd-pbedlw. Pending Xcode build/device verification via PR #200 CI.

userStories.title: iOS AI/Images: AIMealService has no cancellation (stale result wins); uploads aren't resized
userStories.description: As a user, I want the latest AI meal-plan request to win and photo uploads to be reasonably sized so that results are correct and uploads are fast on cellular.
acceptanceCriteria: AIMealService.generateSuggestions (AIMealService.swift:124-226) tracks the in-flight Task and cancels/ignores stale completions (last-request-wins), mirroring AICoachService
ImageUploadService.upload resizes to a max dimension before JPEG compression (ImageUploadService.swift:22-46), matching the AI services' pre-shrink
Rapidly re-tapping Generate no longer lets an older response or local fallback clobber a newer result
userStories.notes: Last-completion-wins race + multi-MB full-res uploads.

userStories.title: iOS Sync infra: ConflictDetector wall-clock + unbounded maps; NetworkMonitor defaults online at cold launch
userStories.description: As a user, I want conflict detection and connectivity to be robust so that real conflicts aren't silenced by clock jumps and offline cold launches aren't treated as online.
acceptanceCriteria: ConflictDetector stamps echo/conflict windows with a monotonic clock and tracks per-echo timestamps in a FIFO, evicting aged entries (ConflictDetector.swift:61-110)
RealtimeService INSERT handlers consume/clear the echo so echo bookkeeping stays balanced and a later real UPDATE isn't suppressed (RealtimeService.swift:176-225)
NetworkMonitor.isConnected initializes synchronously from monitor.currentPath.status so an offline cold launch isn't reported online (NetworkMonitor.swift:32-34)
userStories.notes: Echo/conflict correctness + cold-launch connectivity window.

userStories.title: iOS Offline durability: only grocery mutations are queued; food/kid/recipe/plan edits are lost offline
userStories.description: As a user editing offline, I want pantry, kid, recipe, and plan changes to survive so that no domain silently loses optimistic writes.
acceptanceCriteria: Food, kid, recipe, and plan-entry mutations route through the same OfflineStore enqueue path as grocery on network errors (AppState.swift:433-464 vs 1148-1174)
Bulk grocery paths (clearChecked AppState.swift:1341, moveCheckedToPantry 1454) also enqueue for offline replay
OR: if grocery-only durability is intentional, document the scope in CLAUDE.md and surface it to the user; either way behavior is consistent and intentional
userStories.notes: Inconsistent durability across domains; ties into US-150/US-385.

userStories.title: iOS Edge/Household: stale bearer on retry; nondeterministic active household
userStories.description: As a user, I want edge retries to use a fresh token and a stable active household so that retries recover and realtime scopes to the right household.
acceptanceCriteria: EdgeFunctions.invoke re-fetches the session access token per attempt (or refreshes on 401 before retrying) instead of baking it in before the loop (EdgeFunctions.swift:77-91)
HouseholdService.currentHousehold deterministically resolves the active household (explicit active id or stable ORDER BY), not .limit(1) with no order (HouseholdService.swift:22-34)
RealtimeService scopes channels to the deterministic household id
userStories.notes: Retry can 401 on expired token; multi-household users can subscribe to the wrong household.

userStories.title: iOS Budget: target progress bar never reaches the budget line; residual currency mismatch
userStories.description: As a user, I want the budget bar to fill at my actual target and all amounts in one currency so that 'on track' vs 'over' is honest.
acceptanceCriteria: BudgetView target progress uses total: weeklyTarget (with separate overflow handling) so being at target reads as full, not ~67% (BudgetView.swift:170-291)
targetProgressSection formats the target with groceryCurrency consistently with progressCopy (no mixed currency symbols)
Bar turns to the over-budget state at 100% of target, not 150%
userStories.notes: Target progress bar now totals weeklyTarget (fills 100% at target; over-budget shown via tint+copy) instead of *1.5; target amount formatted in groceryCurrency to match progressCopy. Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-review-prd-pbedlw. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in this web env).

userStories.title: iOS Quiz: answers appended/removed by stack instead of indexed, under-reporting pickiness on back-nav
userStories.description: As a user who goes back to change a quiz answer, I want my answers stored per-question so that scoring is correct and the progress bar completes.
acceptanceCriteria: PickyEaterQuizView stores answers indexed by question (answers[currentQuestion]=index) rather than append + removeLast (PickyEaterQuizView.swift:135-152)
Going back and finishing mid-way scores the actually-answered questions correctly (no truncated array scored against full maxScore)
Progress bar uses currentQuestion+1 so it visually completes on the last question (PickyEaterQuizView.swift:40)
QuizResultView.applyToKid merges (unions) helpfulStrategies with existing rather than overwriting curated strategies (QuizResultView.swift:403-423)
userStories.notes: Re-verify against US-434 (marked done) — audit found append/removeLast still present.

userStories.title: iOS UX polish: per-keystroke filter recompute, reparse wipes selections, cook-mode end, expiring-card window
userStories.description: As a user, I want responsive lists and edits that don't discard my choices so that the app feels fast and predictable.
acceptanceCriteria: RecipesView.filteredRecipes and GroceryView.groupedByRecipeBuckets are debounced/memoized rather than recomputed per keystroke in body (RecipesView.swift:33-48, GroceryView.swift:154-188)
TextImportGrocerySheet/MissingIngredientsSheet preserve user selections across reparse where item identity is unchanged (TextImportGrocerySheet.swift:69-72, MissingIngredientsSheet.swift:78-98)
CookModeView shows a completion screen before dismissing on the last step and starts progress at 0/N (CookModeView.swift:46-126)
DashboardHomeView expiring card bounds the lower end (e.g. days>=-3) so long-expired items don't perpetually nag (DashboardHomeView.swift:27-32)
userStories.notes: Grouped lower-severity UX/perf polish from the core + secondary views audits.

userStories.title: iOS Pantry/Grocery: bulk safe/try-bite only sets (never clears); inconsistent min quantities; receipt dup-merge
userStories.description: As a user, I want bulk tag actions to toggle and quantity minimums to be consistent so that I can un-mark in bulk and can't create 0-count items.
acceptanceCriteria: PantryView bulk Safe/Try-Bite actions toggle based on selection state (can clear, not only set) (PantryView.swift:425-439,577-622)
Add/Edit/QuickAdd grocery entry points enforce a consistent minimum quantity (no 0-quantity grocery item from Edit) (GroceryView.swift:1656)
Receipt save / barcode add merge with existing pantry items instead of inserting duplicates where US-389/390 didn't already cover the path
userStories.notes: Bulk un-mark gap + quantity-minimum inconsistency across grocery entry points.

userStories.title: iOS reliability telemetry: route silent fallbacks and HTTP failures through Sentry, distinguish transient errors
userStories.description: As an operator, I want Tonight Mode fallbacks and barcode/HTTP failures captured so that I can see how often flagship features silently degrade.
acceptanceCriteria: TonightModeService edge-fetch failure routes through SentryService (breadcrumb/capture) instead of print() (TonightModeService.swift:122-124)
BarcodeService distinguishes transient HTTP failures (e.g. 429) from a genuine product-not-found instead of mapping both to nil (BarcodeService.swift:40-45)
StoreKitService.restorePurchases surfaces AppStore.sync() failures so 'nothing to restore' is distinguishable from 'restore failed' (StoreKitService.swift:171-186)
userStories.notes: TonightModeService edge-fetch fallback now records a Sentry breadcrumb (not print); BarcodeService throws TransientLookupError on 429/5xx (distinct from nil not-found) so callers can retry; StoreKitService.restorePurchases surfaces AppStore.sync() failures via errorMessage + Sentry breadcrumb instead of try?. Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-review-prd-pbedlw. Pending Xcode build/device verification via PR #200 CI.

userStories.title: iOS: Global cross-entity search across foods, recipes, grocery & planned meals
userStories.description: As a parent juggling pantry, recipes, and the grocery list, I want one search that looks everywhere so that I can find anything (an ingredient, a recipe that uses it, a grocery item, a planned meal) without first guessing which tab to open.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a single global search entry reachable from the Home tab (search field or toolbar search button in DashboardHomeView) that is not scoped to one entity

Search matches across foods (name/category/aisle/allergens), recipes (name/ingredients), grocery_items (name/aisle/notes), and plan_entries (food/recipe name) using a shared, reusable matching predicate
Results are grouped by type (Foods, Recipes, Grocery, Planner) with a result count per group
Tapping a result deep-links to the correct screen/detail (food -> Pantry detail, recipe -> RecipeDetailView, grocery item -> Grocery, planned meal -> Planner on that date)
Empty query shows recent searches and/or quick suggestions; empty results show a clear 'no matches' state
Existing per-tab .searchable fields remain for in-context filtering; the global search is additive
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-function-review-t0arpn: new reusable GlobalSearch engine (Utilities/GlobalSearch.swift) with per-entity predicates + diacritic/case-insensitive normalization, capped/sorted grouped Results, and family-duplicate dedupe for planned meals. New GlobalSearchView (Views/Search/GlobalSearchView.swift) presented as a sheet from a Dashboard toolbar search button: grouped sections (Foods/Recipes/Grocery/Planner) with per-group counts, recent-searches via @AppStorage on empty query, ContentUnavailableView.search on no match. Food -> FoodDetailView, recipe -> RecipeDetailView (pushed in-sheet); grocery -> Grocery tab and planned meal -> Planner on its date via shared DeepLinkHandler. Per-tab .searchable fields left intact. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in env).

userStories.title: iOS: Complete deep-link routes for every More-tab feature
userStories.description: As a user tapping a notification, widget, or Siri shortcut, I want it to open the exact feature it references so that deep links don't dead-end on a tab root.
acceptanceCriteria: Add MoreRoute cases for Recipes, Food Tracker, Insights, and AI Coach (currently missing from the MoreRoute enum in MoreView.swift) so all More features are routable
Add matching DeepLinkHandler.Destination cases and wire them in MainTabView.route(to:) to select the More tab and push the right MoreRoute
Each MoreView NavigationLink uses value-based navigation (NavigationLink(value:)) so programmatic pushes and taps share one destination map
Verify a deep link to each of the 9 More features lands on the correct screen, not the More root
Existing routes (kids, kidProfile, foodChaining, quiz, settings, progress, recipes) continue to work
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-function-review-t0arpn: added MoreRoute .foodTracker/.insights/.aiCoach (MoreView.swift) and matching DeepLinkHandler.Destination cases + eatpal:// and universal-link parsing (food-tracker/insights/ai-coach). All MoreView Tools/Family/Settings links converted to value-based NavigationLink(value:) sharing one navigationDestination map; MainTabView.route(to:) routes the new destinations onto the More tab path. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in env).

userStories.title: iOS: Persist and restore filter, sort & planner-date state across launches and tab switches
userStories.description: As a frequent user, I want my last-applied filters, sort order, and selected planner day to still be there when I come back so that I'm not re-applying the same filters every single session.
acceptanceCriteria: Persist Pantry filter state (selected categories, excluded allergens, safe-only, try-bite-only, sort option) via @AppStorage/Codable so it survives app relaunch
Persist Recipes RecipeFilters (cookable, cuisines, difficulty, required foods, ingredient query, max time) across navigation away from RecipesView and across relaunch
Persist the Planner selectedDate (per active kid) so switching tabs and returning does not reset to today
Provide a clearly visible one-tap 'Reset filters' affordance wherever persisted filters are active
Grocery view-mode persistence (already @AppStorage) is unchanged; this story brings Pantry/Recipes/Planner to parity
No regression: a brand-new install starts with sensible defaults (name A-Z sort, no filters, today)
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-function-review-t0arpn: RecipeFilters and a new PantryFilters bundle now conform to Codable + JSON-backed RawRepresentable and are stored via @AppStorage ('recipes.filters', 'pantry.filters') so advanced filters + sort survive navigation and relaunch (Cuisine/FoodCategory/FoodSortOption gained Codable). Planner selectedDate persists per active kid via @AppStorage 'planner.selectedDateByKid', clamped so a stale past day falls back to today (onAppear + onChange of activeKidId/selectedDate). Quick chips + search stay transient; reset affordances already exist (SearchFilterView 'Reset All Filters', Recipes 'All' chip + active-filter strip, planner 'Today'). New installs default to no filters / name A-Z / today. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in env).

userStories.title: iOS: Active-filter chip strip + result count on Pantry (parity with Recipes)
userStories.description: As a user who applied several filters, I want to see exactly which filters are on and how many items are showing so that I'm not confused about why foods disappeared.
acceptanceCriteria: Below the Pantry search bar, render a removable chip for each active filter (e.g. 'Category: Protein', 'Exclude: Milk', 'Safe only', 'Sort: Newest') mirroring the Recipes active-filter strip
Each chip has an 'x' to remove just that dimension without opening the filter sheet
Show a 'showing X of Y' count (or filtered count in the title) whenever any filter/search is active
Replace the opaque numeric badge on the filter toolbar icon with (or supplement it by) the named chip strip
Tapping a chip opens the relevant section of the advanced filter sheet
When no filters are active, the chip strip is hidden and layout is unchanged
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-function-review-t0arpn: PantryView now renders a removable active-filter chip strip (one chip per advanced dimension: each category, each excluded allergen, safe-only, try-bite, non-default sort) mirroring the Recipes strip — tapping a chip removes just that dimension. A 'Showing X of Y' count plus an inline 'Clear filters' button appear whenever any filter/search/quick-chip/segment is narrowing the list. The toolbar badge is retained and supplemented by the named strip. Strip hidden when no advanced filters are active. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in env).

userStories.title: iOS: Unify search scope — Grocery search matches aisle, category & notes (not just name)
userStories.description: As a shopper searching the grocery list, I want 'dairy' to surface the dairy aisle items, like Pantry search does, so that search behaves consistently across the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Grocery matchesSearch() matches item name, aisle/aisleSection, category, and notes (currently name-only at GroceryView.swift:137-139)
Extract a single shared search predicate used by Pantry, Grocery, and the global search (US-461) so scope stays consistent
Search remains case-insensitive and diacritic-insensitive
Bulk-select scope pruning (selection limited to visible items) keeps working with the widened predicate
No measurable typing latency regression on a 200-item list
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-function-review-t0arpn: GroceryView.matchesSearch now delegates to the shared GlobalSearch.matches(grocery:) predicate, widening scope from name-only to name + category + free-text aisle + structured aisleSection (raw + display name) + notes. The haystack lives in one place (GlobalSearch.groceryHaystack) reused by the app-wide search, keeping scope consistent. Bulk-select visible-item pruning inherits the wider predicate automatically. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in env).

userStories.title: iOS: Grocery advanced filter sheet + visible view-mode control + bulk-select parity
userStories.description: As a user with a long grocery list, I want discoverable filtering and an obvious way to switch grouping so that I don't have to hunt through a menu, and bulk actions should behave like they do in Pantry.
acceptanceCriteria: Move the by-aisle / by-recipe view-mode switch out of the centered principal menu into a visible segmented control or labeled toolbar toggle
Add a Grocery filter affordance for at least: hide/show checked, priority, and aisle/section, with an active-filter summary consistent with US-464
Auto-exit bulk-select mode after a successful bulk action (mark bought, set aisle, delete) to match PantryView behavior
Surface bulk-select as a visible toolbar control on Grocery (not buried in the add/overflow menu)
When bulk actions run while a search/filter is active, show a brief hint that the action applied only to the visible/filtered items
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-function-review-t0arpn: (1) view-mode (By Aisle / By Recipe) is now an always-visible segmented Picker in the nav bar instead of a tap-to-open principal menu; (2) new GroceryFilterSheet (hide bought, priority, aisle/section) wired through matchesFilter into the uncheckedItems/checkedItems choke points, with a removable active-filter chip strip + 'Showing X of Y' count + Clear (US-464 parity); (3) bulk actions already auto-exit select mode via exitSelectMode() — confirmed; (4) bulk-select promoted from the add overflow menu to a visible toolbar button (with a filter button beside it); (5) entering select mode while a search/filter is active shows an info toast that bulk actions hit visible items only. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in env).

userStories.title: iOS: Add sort options to Recipes (parity with Pantry sort)
userStories.description: As a user browsing a large recipe library, I want to sort by name, rating, total time, or recently added so that I can surface the right recipe without first constructing a filter.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a Recipes sort control (menu or chip) with at least: Name A-Z, Rating (high-low), Total time (quick-slow), Recently added
Sort composes with active filters (e.g. 'easy Italian, fastest first')
Selected sort is shown in the UI and persisted per US-463
Total time sort uses prep+cook minutes and places recipes with no recorded time last (consistent with the time filter rule)
Default sort matches today's behavior so existing users see no surprise reordering until they choose a sort
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-function-review-t0arpn: added RecipeSortOption (Default/Name A-Z/Rating high-low/Total time quick-slow/Recently added) + a `sort` field on RecipeFilters; sorting runs at the end of RecipeFilters.apply via Array<Recipe>.sortedBy, so it composes with filters. Time sort uses resolvedTotalMinutes (no-time recipes sort last); rating sort puts unrated last. Sort lives in the persisted filter bundle (US-463). UI: a topBarLeading toolbar Menu/Picker labelled with the current option. Default = .defaultOrder (no client reordering) so existing users see no surprise until they pick a sort. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in env).

userStories.title: iOS: Recipe favorites — star toggle, Favorites quick chip & filter
userStories.description: As a user with go-to recipes, I want to star favorites and quickly filter to them so that my best recipes don't get lost in a long list.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a favorite/star toggle on recipe rows (swipe or context menu) and in RecipeDetailView
Add a '⭐ Favorites' chip to the RecipesView quick-filter rail and a corresponding RecipeFilters dimension
Persistence is backward-compatible: store favorite as an additive nullable column (or local-only flag) — never drop/rename existing recipe columns (see Migration Rules)
Favorited recipes are eligible to surface in the Home 'Most loved' area alongside high-rated meals
Favorite state syncs through the existing offline write-queue and realtime path like other recipe edits
Unfavoriting removes it from the Favorites filter immediately
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-function-review-t0arpn (additive DB column approach). Migration 20260627000000_recipe_is_favorite.sql adds recipes.is_favorite boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false (+ partial index), backward-compatible per CLAUDE.md; web types.ts updated (Row/Insert/Update). iOS: Recipe + RecipeUpdate gain isFavorite (is_favorite); AppState.setRecipeFavorite does an optimistic, quiet toggle via the existing dataService.updateRecipe path (inherits realtime/retry; partial PATCH is safe because RecipeUpdate optionals encode via encodeIfPresent). RecipeFilters gains favoritesOnly (counted in isAnyActive/activeCount, persisted in the bundle). UI: '⭐️ Favorites' rail chip, leading swipe action, context-menu toggle, row star indicator, and a star toggle in RecipeDetailView's toolbar. FOLLOW-UP: surfacing favorites in the Home 'Most loved' card (rating-based today) was left out to keep scope contained. Pending Xcode build/device verification + supabase db push / types regen in CI.

userStories.title: iOS: Pantry-coverage badge on Recipes list rows ('can I make this now?')
userStories.description: As a user scanning recipes, I want an at-a-glance indicator of how much of each recipe I already have so that I can pick something cookable without opening every recipe.
acceptanceCriteria: RecipeRowView shows a pantry-coverage indicator (percent or icon) computed from pantry + unchecked grocery, reusing RecipeMatcher (same source as the Cookable filter and CookableRecipesSheet)
Tapping the badge opens the existing missing-ingredients sheet for that recipe
Coverage updates reactively when foods or grocery items change
Indicator is accessible (VoiceOver label like 'You have 8 of 10 ingredients')
Computation is cached/lightweight enough not to jank scrolling on a large recipe list
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-function-review-t0arpn: RecipeRowView gains an optional pantry-coverage badge (percent + tier color: green cookNow / orange almostThere) computed once per render via a coverageByRecipe map built from RecipeMatcher.rank (same matcher as the Cookable filter), so it's reactive to foods/grocery changes. Tapping the badge opens the existing MissingIngredientsSheet for that recipe (shortfalls via ShortfallCalculator.compute). VoiceOver label reads 'Cook now, you have 80 percent of ingredients'. Recipes below the matcher threshold or with no ingredients show no badge. Params default to nil so the single existing call site is unaffected. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in env).

userStories.title: iOS: Promote Budget into More -> Tools with deep link and a Grocery nudge
userStories.description: As a budget-conscious parent, I want Budget reachable without digging through Settings so that weekly spend tracking is actually discoverable.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a Budget entry to the More tab Tools section (alongside Insights/Progress) instead of only living under Settings
Add a MoreRoute.budget case and deep-link wiring (depends on US-462 pattern)
Add a lightweight nudge on the Grocery screen to set or review the weekly target when 3+ priced items exist
Keep the existing Settings path working (or redirect it) so no entry point breaks
Verify the weekly target @AppStorage value and forecast continue to render correctly from the new entry point
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-function-review-t0arpn: Budget now appears in the More -> Tools section (dollarsign.circle.fill) in addition to its existing Settings path (kept working). Added MoreRoute.budget + navigationDestination -> BudgetView(), DeepLinkHandler.Destination.budget with eatpal://budget and universal-link parsing, and MainTabView routing onto the More tab. GroceryView shows a one-tap budget nudge ('Track this week's spend') once >=3 items have a price (pricePerUnit), routing to Budget via the shared DeepLinkHandler. Weekly target @AppStorage + forecast render unchanged from the new entry point. Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in env).

userStories.title: iOS: Recurring / repeat-weekly planned meals
userStories.description: As a parent with weekly routines (e.g. Taco Tuesday), I want to repeat a planned meal on a chosen weekday so that I don't manually recreate the same plan every week.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Repeat weekly on <weekday>' to the PlanEntry duplicate menu (extends the existing Tomorrow / Next 3 / Next 7 / Full week / Pick date options)
User can choose how many weeks to project (bounded, e.g. up to 8) to avoid unbounded inserts
Repeated entries are created for the same meal slot and audience (kid/family) as the source
A clear way to undo/remove a generated series (or at least an undo toast for the batch insert)
Generated rows respect allergen/audience rules already enforced by single add-to-plan
userStories.notes: Implemented on branch claude/ios-app-function-review-t0arpn: added a 'Repeat every <weekday>' submenu to the PlanEntry context menu (alongside the existing Tomorrow/Next 3/Next 7/whole-week/Pick-date duplicate options) with bounded choices For 2 / 4 / 8 weeks. repeatWeekly copies the source entry onto the same weekday (date + 7*k) for the same slot + kid, so allergen/audience rules are inherited (same as Duplicate). The whole batch is undoable via a success toast with an 'Undo' action that deletes the just-inserted rows (optimistic deletePlanEntry reflects immediately). Pending Xcode build/device verification (no Swift toolchain in env).

userStories.title: iOS: Inline actions on the Home 'Expiring soon' sheet
userStories.description: As a user warned that food is expiring, I want to act on it right there so that I don't have to memorize the item, switch to the Pantry tab, and find it again.
acceptanceCriteria: ExpiringFoodsSheet rows support inline actions: edit, mark used / adjust quantity, add to grocery, and delete
Actions write through the same offline queue + realtime path as Pantry edits and update the sheet in place
Remove the 'open the Pantry tab to edit' dead-end note once inline actions exist
Destructive delete uses a confirmation consistent with Pantry
Sheet refreshes its expiring window after an action so acted-on items leave the list
userStories.notes: iOS function review: DashboardHomeView ExpiringFoodsSheet (~441-488) is read-only and explicitly tells the user to switch to Pantry to act — avoidable friction on a primary surface.

userStories.title: iOS: Make Tonight Mode manually triggerable any time and scope to any meal
userStories.description: As a user who plans lunches or off-hours, I want to open Tonight-Mode-style suggestions on demand (not only 4-8pm) and for any meal slot so that the feature isn't hidden most of the day.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an always-available entry point to launch the suggestions flow regardless of time of day (keep the 4-8pm panic CTA as an additional nudge)
Allow choosing the target meal slot (breakfast/lunch/dinner/snack) instead of dinner-only
Suggestions ranking and per-kid fit chips work for the chosen slot
Delivery fallback no longer force-closes the sheet — keep it open so the user can take other actions (cook/add missing)
Selecting a suggestion to plan routes into the existing add-to-plan flow for the chosen slot/date/kid
userStories.notes: iOS function review: Tonight Mode is time-gated (4-8pm) and dinner-only; the always-available button is effectively hidden, so a built feature is invisible most of the day. Delivery fallback dismisses the whole sheet.

userStories.title: iOS: Tie feature silos into the workflow — Food Chaining & AI Coach integration
userStories.description: As a user, I want Food Chaining and the AI Coach to connect to planning and to my data so that insight tools actually drive action instead of being read-only dead-ends.
acceptanceCriteria: Food Chaining: add 'Add chain to plan' and/or 'Add bridge foods to grocery' actions, and let a generated chain be saved/favorited for later
Food Chaining: add a name-search affordance to the food selectors instead of horizontal-scroll-only when the pantry is large
AI Coach: add contextual entry points ('Ask coach about this meal/food') from a recipe, a planned meal, and a kid profile that pre-seed the prompt
AI Coach: persist conversation history per kid (not cleared on close) with the ability to search past answers, and add a MoreRoute/deep link for AI Coach (depends on US-462)
No external content is sent anywhere new without it being an explicit user action
userStories.notes: iOS function review: FoodChainingView is read-only with no plan/grocery integration and no name search; AICoachView chat is ephemeral, unsearchable, and not deep-linkable. 'Tie everything together' bucket from the request.
